items.name: wrapping_add
items.signature: fn wrapping_add(a: T, b: T) -> T
items.doc: Adds `a` and `b`, wrapping silently on overflow instead of trapping.

items.name: wrapping_sub
items.signature: fn wrapping_sub(a: T, b: T) -> T
items.doc: Subtracts `b` from `a`, wrapping silently on overflow instead of trapping.

items.name: wrapping_mul
items.signature: fn wrapping_mul(a: T, b: T) -> T
items.doc: Multiplies `a` and `b`, wrapping silently on overflow instead of trapping.

items.name: default
items.doc: Returns `T::default()`, inferring `T` from the call-site context.

items.name: alloc
items.doc: Allocates `size` bytes of uninitialized memory. Returns null on allocation failure.

items.name: dealloc
items.doc: Frees memory previously returned by `alloc` or `realloc_buf`. Null is a no-op.

items.name: realloc_buf
items.signature: fn realloc_buf(ptr: *mut u8, size: i64) -> *mut u8
items.doc:
Resizes the allocation at `ptr` to `size` bytes, moving it if necessary.
Returns the new pointer; on failure returns null and leaves the original allocation intact.

items.name: copy_bytes
items.signature: fn copy_bytes(dst: *mut u8, src: *const u8, n: i64) -> void
items.doc: Copies `n` bytes from `src` to `dst`; regions must not overlap (use `move_bytes` if they may).

items.name: move_bytes
items.signature: fn move_bytes(dst: *mut u8, src: *const u8, n: i64) -> void
items.doc: Copies `n` bytes from `src` to `dst`; safe for overlapping regions.

items.name: fill_bytes
items.signature: fn fill_bytes(ptr: *mut u8, val: i32, n: i64) -> void
items.doc: Fills `n` bytes starting at `ptr` with `val` (low 8 bits used).

items.name: zero_bytes
items.signature: fn zero_bytes(ptr: *mut u8, n: i64) -> void
items.doc: Zeroes `n` bytes starting at `ptr`.

items.name: compare_bytes
items.signature: fn compare_bytes(a: *const u8, b: *const u8, n: i64) -> i32
items.doc: Compares `n` bytes at `a` and `b`; returns negative, zero, or positive (as `memcmp`).

items.name: swap
items.signature: fn swap(a: *mut T, b: *mut T) -> void
items.doc: Swaps the values at two non-overlapping pointers of the same type.

items.name: replace
items.signature: fn replace(ptr: *mut T, new_val: T) -> T
items.doc: Replaces the value at `ptr` with `new_val`, returning the old value.

items.name: take
items.doc:
Replaces the value at `ptr` with `T::default()`, returning the old value.
Useful for moving ownership out of a container field without a partial-move borrowck error.

items.name: swap_ref
items.signature: fn swap_ref(a: &mut T, b: &mut T) -> void
items.doc: Swaps the values behind two exclusive references.

items.name: replace_ref
items.signature: fn replace_ref(dst: &mut T, src: T) -> T
items.doc: Moves `src` into `dst`, returning the previous value at `dst`.

items.name: take_ref
items.doc: Moves the value out of `dst`, replacing it with `T::default()`.

items.name: is_ok
items.doc: Returns `true` if the result is `Ok`.

items.name: is_err
items.doc: Returns `true` if the result is `Err`.

items.name: is_ok_and
items.signature: fn is_ok_and(self: Result, pred: fn(T) -> bool) -> bool
items.doc:
Returns `true` if the result is `Ok` and `pred` returns `true` for the contained value.

Consumes `self` (the value is passed to `pred` by value).

items.name: is_err_and
items.signature: fn is_err_and(self: Result, pred: fn(E) -> bool) -> bool
items.doc:
Returns `true` if the result is `Err` and `pred` returns `true` for the contained error.

Consumes `self` (the error is passed to `pred` by value).

items.name: unwrap
items.doc:
Returns the contained `Ok` value; panics if the result is `Err`.

The panic message is fixed — the error value is not formatted into it.

items.name: unwrap_err
items.doc: Returns the contained `Err` value; panics if the result is `Ok`.

items.name: expect
items.doc: Returns the contained `Ok` value; panics with `msg` if the result is `Err`.

items.name: expect_err
items.doc: Returns the contained `Err` value; panics with `msg` if the result is `Ok`.

items.name: unwrap_or
items.signature: fn unwrap_or(self: Result, default: T) -> T
items.doc:
Returns the contained `Ok` value or `default`.

`default` is eagerly evaluated; use `unwrap_or_else` for a lazily computed fallback.

items.name: unwrap_or_else
items.signature: fn unwrap_or_else(self: Result, f: fn(E) -> T) -> T
items.doc: Returns the contained `Ok` value or computes a fallback from the error with `f`.

items.name: ok
items.doc: Converts to `Option<T>`: `Ok(v)` becomes `Some(v)`, discarding the error.

items.name: err
items.doc: Converts to `Option<E>`: `Err(e)` becomes `Some(e)`, discarding the success value.

items.name: map
items.signature: fn map(self: Result, f: fn(T) -> U) -> Result
items.doc: Applies `f` to a contained `Ok` value, leaving an `Err` value untouched.

items.name: map_err
items.signature: fn map_err(self: Result, f: fn(E) -> F) -> Result
items.doc: Applies `f` to a contained `Err` value, leaving an `Ok` value untouched.

items.name: map_or
items.signature: fn map_or(self: Result, default: U, f: fn(T) -> U) -> U
items.doc: Returns `default` if `Err`, or applies `f` to the contained `Ok` value.

items.name: map_or_else
items.signature: fn map_or_else(self: Result, dfl: fn(E) -> U, f: fn(T) -> U) -> U
items.doc: Maps to `U` by applying `f` to a contained `Ok` value, or `dfl` to a contained `Err`.

items.name: and
items.signature: fn and(self: Result, res: Result) -> Result
items.doc:
Returns `res` if the result is `Ok`, otherwise the `Err` value of `self`.

`res` is eagerly evaluated; use `and_then` for a lazily computed continuation.

items.name: or
items.signature: fn or(self: Result, res: Result) -> Result
items.doc:
Returns `res` if the result is `Err`, otherwise the `Ok` value of `self`.

`res` is eagerly evaluated; use `or_else` for a lazily computed fallback.

items.name: and_then
items.signature: fn and_then(self: Result, f: fn(T) -> Result) -> Result
items.doc: Calls `f` with the contained `Ok` value, otherwise propagates the `Err`.

items.name: or_else
items.signature: fn or_else(self: Result, f: fn(E) -> Result) -> Result
items.doc: Calls `f` with the contained `Err` value, otherwise propagates the `Ok`.

items.name: as_ref
items.doc: Converts from `&Result<T, E>` to `Result<&T, &E>`, borrowing the contained value.

items.name: as_mut
items.doc: Converts from `&mut Result<T, E>` to `Result<&mut T, &mut E>`.

items.name: result_unwrap_or_default
items.doc:
Returns the contained `Ok` value, or `T::default()` if `Err`.

Free-fn form of Rust's `Result::unwrap_or_default`.

items.name: result_flatten
items.doc: Removes one level of `Result` nesting; free-fn form of `flatten`.

items.name: result_transpose
items.doc:
Transposes a `Result` of an `Option` into an `Option` of a `Result`; free-fn form of
`transpose`.

`Ok(None)` → `None`; `Ok(Some(v))` → `Some(Ok(v))`; `Err(e)` → `Some(Err(e))`.

items.name: flatten
items.doc: Removes one level of nesting: `Ok(inner)` yields `inner`, `Err(e)` stays `Err(e)`.

items.name: transpose
items.doc:
Transposes a `Result` of an `Option` into an `Option` of a `Result`.

`Ok(None)` → `None`; `Ok(Some(v))` → `Some(Ok(v))`; `Err(e)` → `Some(Err(e))`.

items.name: unwrap_or_i32
items.signature: fn unwrap_or_i32(r: Result, def: i32) -> i32
items.doc: Returns the contained `Ok` value or `def`; `i32`-monomorphic shim for `unwrap_or`.

items.name: is_ok_i32
items.doc: Returns `true` if `r` is `Ok`; `i32`-monomorphic shim for `is_ok`.

items.name: is_err_i32
items.doc: Returns `true` if `r` is `Err`; `i32`-monomorphic shim for `is_err`.

items.name: reverse
items.doc: Returns the reversed ordering: `Less` <-> `Greater`, `Equal` unchanged.

items.name: is_lt
items.doc: Returns `true` if the ordering is `Less`.

items.name: is_le
items.doc: Returns `true` if the ordering is `Less` or `Equal`.

items.name: is_eq
items.doc: Returns `true` if the ordering is `Equal`.

items.name: is_ne
items.doc: Returns `true` if the ordering is not `Equal`.

items.name: is_gt
items.doc: Returns `true` if the ordering is `Greater`.

items.name: is_ge
items.doc: Returns `true` if the ordering is `Greater` or `Equal`.

items.name: then
items.signature: fn then(self: Ordering, other: Ordering) -> Ordering
items.doc: Returns `other` if `self` is `Equal`, else `self`; chains lexicographic comparisons.

items.name: then_with
items.signature: fn then_with(self: Ordering, f: fn() -> Ordering) -> Ordering
items.doc: Returns `f()` if `self` is `Equal`, else `self`; lazy variant of `then`.

items.name: cmp_opt_is_lt
items.doc: Returns `true` if `o` is `Some(Less)`; `None` (incomparable) yields `false`.

items.name: cmp_opt_is_le
items.doc: Returns `true` if `o` is `Some(Less)` or `Some(Equal)`; `None` yields `false`.

items.name: cmp_opt_is_gt
items.doc: Returns `true` if `o` is `Some(Greater)`; `None` yields `false`.

items.name: cmp_opt_is_ge
items.doc: Returns `true` if `o` is `Some(Greater)` or `Some(Equal)`; `None` yields `false`.

items.name: max
items.signature: fn max(self: i32, other: i32) -> i32

items.name: min

items.signature: fn clamp(self: i32, lo: i32, hi: i32) -> i32

items.name: clamp

items.name: count_zeros
items.doc: Returns the number of zero bits.

items.name: leading_zeros
items.doc: Returns the number of leading zero bits.

items.name: trailing_zeros
items.doc: Returns the number of trailing zero bits.

items.name: count_zeros
items.doc: Returns the number of zero bits.

items.name: leading_zeros
items.doc: Returns the number of leading zero bits.

items.name: trailing_zeros
items.doc: Returns the number of trailing zero bits.

items.name: count_zeros
items.doc: Returns the number of zero bits.

items.name: leading_zeros
items.doc: Returns the number of leading zero bits.

items.name: trailing_zeros
items.doc: Returns the number of trailing zero bits.

items.name: count_zeros
items.doc: Returns the number of zero bits.

items.name: leading_zeros
items.doc: Returns the number of leading zero bits.

items.name: trailing_zeros
items.doc: Returns the number of trailing zero bits.

items.name: saturating_add
items.signature: fn saturating_add(self: i32, other: i32) -> i32
items.doc: Returns `self + other`, saturating at the numeric bounds instead of overflowing.

items.name: saturating_sub
items.signature: fn saturating_sub(self: i32, other: i32) -> i32
items.doc: Returns `self - other`, saturating at the numeric bounds instead of overflowing.

items.name: saturating_mul
items.signature: fn saturating_mul(self: i32, other: i32) -> i32
items.doc: Returns `self * other`, saturating at the numeric bounds instead of overflowing.

items.name: saturating_abs
items.doc: Returns the absolute value, saturating: the minimum value maps to the maximum.

items.name: saturating_neg
items.doc: Returns `-self`, saturating: the minimum value maps to the maximum.

items.name: checked_add
items.signature: fn checked_add(self: i32, other: i32) -> Option
items.doc: Returns `Some(self + other)`, or `None` on overflow.

items.name: checked_sub
items.signature: fn checked_sub(self: i32, other: i32) -> Option
items.doc: Returns `Some(self - other)`, or `None` on overflow.

items.name: checked_mul
items.signature: fn checked_mul(self: i32, other: i32) -> Option
items.doc: Returns `Some(self * other)`, or `None` on overflow.

items.name: checked_div
items.signature: fn checked_div(self: i32, other: i32) -> Option
items.doc: Returns `Some(self / other)`, or `None` if `other == 0` or on overflow (`MIN / -1`).

items.name: checked_rem
items.signature: fn checked_rem(self: i32, other: i32) -> Option
items.doc: Returns `Some(self % other)`, or `None` if `other == 0` or on overflow (`MIN % -1`).

items.name: checked_neg
items.doc: Returns `Some(-self)`, or `None` when `self` is the minimum value.

items.name: checked_abs
items.doc: Returns `Some(|self|)`, or `None` when `self` is the minimum value.

items.name: overflowing_add
items.signature: fn overflowing_add(self: i32, other: i32) -> (i32, bool)
items.doc: Returns the wrapped sum and a flag that is `true` if overflow occurred.

items.name: overflowing_sub
items.signature: fn overflowing_sub(self: i32, other: i32) -> (i32, bool)
items.doc: Returns the wrapped difference and a flag that is `true` if overflow occurred.

items.name: overflowing_mul
items.signature: fn overflowing_mul(self: i32, other: i32) -> (i32, bool)
items.doc: Returns the wrapped product and a flag that is `true` if overflow occurred.

items.name: overflowing_neg
items.doc: Returns the wrapped negation and a flag; `(MIN, true)` for the minimum value.

items.name: overflowing_abs
items.doc: Returns the wrapped absolute value and a flag; `(MIN, true)` for the minimum value.

items.name: wrapping_add
items.signature: fn wrapping_add(self: i32, other: i32) -> i32
items.doc: Returns `self + other`, wrapping around at the boundary of the type.

items.name: wrapping_sub
items.signature: fn wrapping_sub(self: i32, other: i32) -> i32
items.doc: Returns `self - other`, wrapping around at the boundary of the type.

items.name: wrapping_mul
items.signature: fn wrapping_mul(self: i32, other: i32) -> i32
items.doc: Returns `self * other`, wrapping around at the boundary of the type.

items.name: wrapping_neg
items.doc: Returns `-self`, wrapping: the minimum value maps to itself.

items.name: wrapping_abs
items.doc: Returns the absolute value, wrapping: the minimum value maps to itself.

items.name: rotate_left
items.signature: fn rotate_left(self: u32, n: u32) -> u32
items.doc: Rotates the bits left by `n` (taken modulo 32).

items.name: rotate_right

items.doc: Rotates the bits right by `n` (taken modulo 32).

items.name: rotate_left

items.doc: Rotates the bits left by `n` (taken modulo 32).

items.name: rotate_right

items.doc: Rotates the bits left by `n` (taken modulo 64).

items.name: rotate_right
items.signature: fn rotate_right(self: u64, n: u32) -> u64
items.doc: Rotates the bits right by `n` (taken modulo 64).

items.name: rotate_left
items.signature: fn rotate_left(self: i64, n: u32) -> i64
items.doc: Rotates the bits left by `n` (taken modulo 64).

items.name: rotate_right
items.signature: fn rotate_right(self: i64, n: u32) -> i64
items.doc: Rotates the bits right by `n` (taken modulo 64).

items.name: swap_bytes

items.name: to_le
items.doc: Converts to little-endian; identity on this little-endian target.

items.name: from_le
items.doc: Converts from little-endian; identity on this little-endian target.

items.doc: Converts to big-endian by swapping bytes.

items.name: from_be
items.doc: Converts from big-endian by swapping bytes.

items.doc: Returns the absolute value, wrapping: the minimum value maps to itself.

items.name: overflowing_add
items.signature: fn overflowing_add(self: i64, other: i64) -> (i64, bool)
items.doc: Returns the wrapped sum and a flag that is `true` if overflow occurred.

items.name: overflowing_sub
items.signature: fn overflowing_sub(self: i64, other: i64) -> (i64, bool)
items.doc: Returns the wrapped difference and a flag that is `true` if overflow occurred.

items.name: overflowing_mul
items.signature: fn overflowing_mul(self: i64, other: i64) -> (i64, bool)
items.doc: Returns the wrapped product and a flag that is `true` if overflow occurred.

items.name: overflowing_neg
items.doc: Returns the wrapped negation and a flag; `(MIN, true)` for the minimum value.

items.name: overflowing_abs
items.doc: Returns the wrapped absolute value and a flag; `(MIN, true)` for the minimum value.

items.name: checked_add
items.signature: fn checked_add(self: i64, other: i64) -> Option
items.doc: Returns `Some(self + other)`, or `None` on overflow.

items.name: checked_sub
items.signature: fn checked_sub(self: i64, other: i64) -> Option
items.doc: Returns `Some(self - other)`, or `None` on overflow.

items.name: checked_mul
items.signature: fn checked_mul(self: i64, other: i64) -> Option
items.doc: Returns `Some(self * other)`, or `None` on overflow.

items.name: checked_div
items.signature: fn checked_div(self: i64, other: i64) -> Option
items.doc: Returns `Some(self / other)`, or `None` if `other == 0` or on overflow (`MIN / -1`).

items.name: checked_rem
items.signature: fn checked_rem(self: i64, other: i64) -> Option
items.doc: Returns `Some(self % other)`, or `None` if `other == 0` or on overflow (`MIN % -1`).

items.name: checked_neg
items.doc: Returns `Some(-self)`, or `None` when `self` is the minimum value.

items.name: checked_abs
items.doc: Returns `Some(|self|)`, or `None` when `self` is the minimum value.

items.name: saturating_add
items.signature: fn saturating_add(self: i64, other: i64) -> i64
items.doc: Returns `self + other`, saturating at the numeric bounds instead of overflowing.

items.name: saturating_sub
items.signature: fn saturating_sub(self: i64, other: i64) -> i64
items.doc: Returns `self - other`, saturating at the numeric bounds instead of overflowing.

items.name: saturating_mul
items.signature: fn saturating_mul(self: i64, other: i64) -> i64
items.doc: Returns `self * other`, saturating at the numeric bounds instead of overflowing.

items.name: saturating_abs
items.doc: Returns the absolute value, saturating: the minimum value maps to the maximum.

items.name: saturating_neg
items.doc: Returns `-self`, saturating: the minimum value maps to the maximum.

items.name: cmp_min_by
items.signature: fn cmp_min_by(a: T, b: T, compare: fn(T, T) -> Ordering) -> T
items.doc: Returns the smaller of `a` and `b` per `compare`; `a` when they compare equal.

items.name: cmp_max_by
items.signature: fn cmp_max_by(a: T, b: T, compare: fn(T, T) -> Ordering) -> T
items.doc: Returns the larger of `a` and `b` per `compare`; `b` when they compare equal.

items.name: cmp_min_by_key
items.signature: fn cmp_min_by_key(a: T, b: T, key: fn(T) -> K) -> T
items.doc: Returns the argument whose `key` projection is smaller; `a` when the keys compare equal.

items.name: cmp_max_by_key
items.signature: fn cmp_max_by_key(a: T, b: T, key: fn(T) -> K) -> T
items.doc: Returns the argument whose `key` projection is larger; `b` when the keys compare equal.

items.name: wrapping_mul

items.doc: Returns `self * other`, wrapping around at the boundary of the type.

items.name: wrapping_neg

items.doc: Returns `self + other`, wrapping around at the boundary of the type.

items.name: wrapping_mul

items.doc: Returns `self * other`, wrapping around at the boundary of the type.

items.doc: Returns `self - other`, wrapping around at the boundary of the type.

items.name: wrapping_neg
items.doc: Returns `-self`, wrapping: the minimum value maps to itself.

items.doc: Returns `self + other`, wrapping around at the boundary of the type.

items.name: wrapping_sub

items.doc: Returns `self - other`, wrapping around at the boundary of the type.

items.name: wrapping_neg

items.signature: fn bitor(self: bool, rhs: bool) -> bool

items.name: bitxor

items.name: is_continue
items.doc: Returns `true` if this is `Continue`.

items.name: is_break
items.doc: Returns `true` if this is `Break`.

items.name: continue_value
items.doc: Returns the `Continue` payload as `Some`, or `None` if `Break`; consumes `self`.

items.name: break_value
items.doc: Returns the `Break` payload as `Some`, or `None` if `Continue`; consumes `self`.

items.name: map_continue

items.doc: Maps the `Continue` payload with `f`, passing `Break` through unchanged.

items.name: map_break
items.signature: fn map_break(self: ControlFlow, f: MapBFn) -> ControlFlow
items.doc: Maps the `Break` payload with `f`, passing `Continue` through unchanged.

items.name: iter_eq_by
items.signature: fn iter_eq_by(a: I, b: J, eq: fn(T, U) -> bool) -> bool
items.doc: Returns `true` iff both iterators yield the same number of items and `eq(x, y)` holds pairwise.

items.name: iter_cmp
items.signature: fn iter_cmp(a: I, b: J) -> Ordering
items.doc: Compares two iterators lexicographically; a shorter matching prefix compares `Less`.

items.name: iter_lt
items.signature: fn iter_lt(a: I, b: J) -> bool
items.doc: Returns `true` iff `a` is lexicographically less than `b`.

items.name: iter_le
items.signature: fn iter_le(a: I, b: J) -> bool
items.doc: Returns `true` iff `a` is lexicographically less than or equal to `b`.

items.name: iter_gt
items.signature: fn iter_gt(a: I, b: J) -> bool
items.doc: Returns `true` iff `a` is lexicographically greater than `b`.

items.name: iter_ge
items.signature: fn iter_ge(a: I, b: J) -> bool
items.doc: Returns `true` iff `a` is lexicographically greater than or equal to `b`.

items.name: iter_ne
items.signature: fn iter_ne(a: I, b: J) -> bool
items.doc: Returns `true` iff the two iterators are not pairwise equal.

items.name: iter_eq
items.signature: fn iter_eq(a: I, b: J) -> bool
items.doc: Returns `true` iff both iterators yield the same number of items and all pairs compare equal.

items.name: iter_max_by_key
items.signature: fn iter_max_by_key(iter: I, key: fn(T) -> K) -> Option
items.doc: Returns the item whose `key` projection is maximal, or `None` if empty; last of equals wins (Rust).

items.name: iter_min_by_key
items.signature: fn iter_min_by_key(iter: I, key: fn(T) -> K) -> Option
items.doc: Returns the item whose `key` projection is minimal, or `None` if empty; first of equals wins.

items.name: iter_rposition
items.signature: fn iter_rposition(iter: I, pred: fn(T) -> bool) -> Option
items.doc:
Returns the forward index of the first back-to-front item satisfying `pred`, or `None`.
Needs DEI + ExactSize to translate the back position into a front-relative index.

items.name: iter_rev
items.doc: Creates a `RevIter` over `iter`; free-fn form of `DoubleEndedIterator::rev`.

items.name: iter_copied
items.doc: Creates a `CopiedIter` over a by-ref iterator; free-fn form of Rust's `.copied()`.

items.name: iter_map
items.signature: fn iter_map(iter: I, f: F) -> MapIter<I, T, R, F>
items.doc: Creates a `MapIter` applying `f` to each item of `iter`; free-fn form of `Iterator::map`.

items.name: iter_filter
items.signature: fn iter_filter(iter: I, pred: fn(T) -> bool) -> FilterIter<I, T>
items.doc: Creates a `FilterIter` yielding the items of `iter` that satisfy `pred`.

items.name: iter_scan
items.signature: fn iter_scan(iter: I, state: St, f: fn(&mut St, T) -> Option) -> ScanIter<I, T, St, B>
items.doc: Creates a `ScanIter` with initial `state`, applying `f` to each item.

items.name: iter_fuse
items.signature: fn iter_fuse(iter: I, zero: T) -> FuseIter<I, T>
items.doc: Creates a `FuseIter` over `iter`; `zero` fills the phantom field and is never yielded.

items.name: iter_filter_map
items.signature: fn iter_filter_map(iter: I, f: fn(T) -> Option) -> FilterMapIter<I, T, B>
items.doc: Creates a `FilterMapIter` applying `f` and skipping items mapped to `None`.

items.name: iter_take
items.signature: fn iter_take(iter: I, n: i64, _zero: T) -> TakeIter<I, T>
items.doc: Creates a `TakeIter` capping `iter` at `n` items; `_zero` only anchors `T` inference.

items.name: iter_skip
items.signature: fn iter_skip(iter: I, n: i64, _zero: T) -> SkipIter<I, T>
items.doc: Creates a `SkipIter` dropping the first `n` items of `iter`; `_zero` only anchors `T`.

items.name: iter_take_while
items.signature: fn iter_take_while(iter: I, pred: fn(T) -> bool, zero: T) -> TakeWhileIter<I, T>
items.doc: Creates a `TakeWhileIter`; `zero` fills the phantom field and is never yielded.

items.name: iter_skip_while
items.signature: fn iter_skip_while(iter: I, pred: fn(T) -> bool, zero: T) -> SkipWhileIter<I, T>
items.doc: Creates a `SkipWhileIter`; `zero` fills the phantom field and is never yielded.

items.name: iter_step_by
items.signature: fn iter_step_by(iter: I, step: i64, zero: T) -> StepByIter<I, T>
items.doc: Creates a `StepByIter` with stride `step`; `zero` fills the phantom field.

items.name: iter_inspect
items.signature: fn iter_inspect(iter: I, visit: fn(T) -> void, zero: T) -> InspectIter<I, T>
items.doc: Creates an `InspectIter` calling `visit` per item; `zero` fills the phantom field.

items.name: iter_peekable
items.signature: fn iter_peekable(iter: I, _zero: T) -> PeekableIter<I, T>
items.doc: Creates a `PeekableIter` over `iter`; `_zero` only anchors `T` inference.

items.name: iter_intersperse
items.signature: fn iter_intersperse(iter: I, sep: T, zero: T) -> IntersperseIter<I, T>
items.doc: Creates an `IntersperseIter` with separator `sep`; `zero` seeds the peek buffer.

items.name: iter_flatten
items.signature: fn iter_flatten(outer: O, zero_inner: I) -> FlattenIter<O, I, T>
items.doc: Creates a `FlattenIter` over `outer`; `zero_inner` seeds the inactive `cur` slot.

items.name: iter_array_chunks
items.signature: fn iter_array_chunks(iter: I, zero_buf: [T; 0], anchor: T) -> ArrayChunksIter<I, T, N>
items.doc: Creates an `ArrayChunksIter`; `zero_buf` and `anchor` are placeholder initialisers.

items.name: iter_map_windows
items.signature: fn iter_map_windows(iter: I, f: F, zero_buf: [T; 0], anchor: T, zero_r: R) -> MapWindowsIter<I, T, R, F, N>
items.doc: Creates a `MapWindowsIter`; the `zero_*`/`anchor` args are placeholder initialisers.

items.name: iter_flat_map
items.signature: fn iter_flat_map(iter: I, f: F, zero_t: T, zero_inner: II, zero_u: U) -> FlatMapIter<I, T, F, II, U>
items.doc: Creates a `FlatMapIter`; the `zero_*` args are placeholder initialisers, never yielded.

items.name: iter_by_ref
items.doc:
Creates a `ByRefIter` borrowing `it`; chained adapters then don't consume the original.
Raw-pointer backed — the wrapper must not outlive `it`.

items.name: iter_once
items.doc: Creates an iterator yielding `val` exactly once.

items.name: iter_empty
items.doc: Creates an iterator yielding nothing; `zero` fills the phantom field.

items.name: iter_repeat
items.doc: Creates an infinite iterator repeating `val`.

items.name: iter_cycle
items.signature: fn iter_cycle(iter: I, zero: T) -> CycleIter<I, T>
items.doc: Creates a `CycleIter` repeating `iter` endlessly; `zero` fills the phantom field.

items.name: iter_enumerate
items.signature: fn iter_enumerate(iter: I, _zero: T) -> EnumIter<I, T>
items.doc: Creates an `EnumIter` over `iter`; `_zero` only anchors `T` inference.

items.name: iter_chain
items.signature: fn iter_chain(a: A, b: B, _zero: T) -> ChainIter<A, B, T>
items.doc: Creates a `ChainIter` yielding `a` then `b`; `_zero` only anchors `T` inference.

items.name: iter_zip
items.signature: fn iter_zip(a: A, b: B, _zt: T, _zu: U) -> ZipIter<A, B, T, U>
items.doc: Creates a `ZipIter` over `a` and `b`; `_zt`/`_zu` only anchor `T`/`U` inference.

items.name: iter_over_slice
items.doc:
Creates a `SliceIter` borrowing `s`.
Holds a raw pointer into the slice — the iterator must not outlive `s`.

items.name: iter_count
items.doc: Consumes `iter`, returning the number of items; free-fn form of `Iterator::count`.

items.name: iter_fold
items.signature: fn iter_fold(iter: I, init: B, f: F) -> B
items.doc: Folds every item into an accumulator starting from `init`; free-fn form of `Iterator::fold`.

items.name: iter_for_each
items.signature: fn iter_for_each(iter: I, f: F) -> void
items.doc: Calls `f` on each item, consuming `iter`; free-fn form of `Iterator::for_each`.

items.name: iter_product_i32
items.doc: Multiplies all `i32` items; an empty iterator yields `1`.

items.name: iter_product_i64
items.doc: Multiplies all `i64` items; an empty iterator yields `1`.

items.name: iter_max_by
items.signature: fn iter_max_by(iter: I, cmp: fn(T, T) -> i32) -> Option
items.doc: Returns the comparator-maximal item, or `None` if empty; the last of equals wins (Rust).

items.name: iter_min_by
items.signature: fn iter_min_by(iter: I, cmp: fn(T, T) -> i32) -> Option
items.doc: Returns the comparator-minimal item, or `None` if empty; the first of equals wins.

items.name: iter_max
items.doc: Returns the maximum item, or `None` if empty; of equal maxima the last is kept (Rust).

items.name: iter_min
items.doc: Returns the minimum item, or `None` if empty; of equal minima the first is kept.

items.name: iter_max_i32
items.doc: Returns the largest `i32` item, or `None` if empty.

items.name: iter_min_i32
items.doc: Returns the smallest `i32` item, or `None` if empty.

items.name: iter_max_i64
items.doc: Returns the largest `i64` item, or `None` if empty.

items.name: iter_min_i64
items.doc: Returns the smallest `i64` item, or `None` if empty.

items.name: iter_sum_i32
items.doc: Sums all `i32` items; an empty iterator yields `0`.

items.name: iter_sum_i64
items.doc: Sums all `i64` items; an empty iterator yields `0`.

items.name: iter_from_fn
items.doc: Creates an iterator that calls `f` on every `next`; `f` returning `None` ends iteration.

items.name: iter_successors
items.signature: fn iter_successors(first: Option, succ: F) -> SuccessorsIter<T, F>
items.doc: Creates an iterator yielding `first`, then successive `succ` results until one is `None`.

items.name: iter_repeat_n

items.doc: Creates an iterator yielding `value` exactly `count` times; `count <= 0` yields nothing.

items.name: iter_once_with
items.signature: fn iter_once_with(f: F, zero: T) -> OnceWithIter<T, F>
items.doc: Creates an iterator firing `f` once on the first `next`; `zero` fills the phantom field.

items.name: iter_repeat_with
items.signature: fn iter_repeat_with(f: F, zero: T) -> RepeatWithIter<T, F>
items.doc: Creates an infinite iterator calling `f` on every `next`; `zero` fills the phantom field.

items.name: split_byte
items.doc: Splits `s` on each occurrence of the single byte `sep`, yielding sub-`str` views into `s`.

items.name: str_split_pat
items.doc: Splits `s` on each match of pattern `p`, yielding sub-`str` views into `s`.

items.name: str_from_raw
items.doc:
Constructs a `str` fat pointer from a raw pointer and explicit byte count.
Compiler intrinsic — sema intercepts all call sites by name; the body is never called.
Analogous to Rust's `std::slice::from_raw_parts::<u8>`.
Safety: `ptr` must be valid for `len` bytes and remain alive for the `str` lifetime.

items.name: str_from_cstr
items.doc:
Constructs a `str` from a null-terminated C string (length via `str_len`).
Safety: `ptr` must be a valid, non-null, null-terminated byte string.

items.name: str_len
items.doc: Returns the byte length of a null-terminated C string, excluding the terminator.

items.name: str_byte_at
items.doc:
Returns the `i`-th UTF-8 byte of `s`, or 0 if `i` is out of range.
Safe wrapper over the raw index so byte scans need no `unsafe` at call sites.

items.name: mem_copy
items.signature: fn mem_copy(dst: *mut u8, dst_off: i64, src: *const u8, n: i64) -> void
items.doc:
Copies `n` bytes from `src` into `dst` starting at byte offset `dst_off`.
Forward byte-by-byte copy; overlapping regions are not supported (memcpy, not memmove).

items.name: write_i32
items.signature: fn write_i32(buf: *mut u8, off: i64, n: i32) -> i64
items.doc: Writes the decimal representation of `n` into `buf[off..]`; returns bytes written.

items.name: write_i64
items.signature: fn write_i64(buf: *mut u8, off: i64, n: i64) -> i64
items.doc: Writes the decimal representation of `n` into `buf[off..]`; returns bytes written.

items.name: write_u64
items.signature: fn write_u64(buf: *mut u8, off: i64, n: u64) -> i64
items.doc: Writes the decimal representation of `n` into `buf[off..]`; returns bytes written.

items.name: write_bool
items.signature: fn write_bool(buf: *mut u8, off: i64, v: bool) -> i64
items.doc: Writes `"true"` or `"false"` into `buf[off..]`; returns bytes written (4 or 5).

items.name: bytes_arr_eq
items.doc: Returns `true` iff the two arrays are byte-for-byte equal.

items.name: str_eq
items.doc: Returns `true` iff `a` and `b` have the same length and byte content.

items.name: str_cmp
items.doc: Compares `a` and `b` lexicographically by bytes; returns -1, 0, or 1.

items.name: str_starts_with
items.doc: Returns `true` if `s` begins with the byte sequence `prefix`.

items.name: str_ends_with
items.doc: Returns `true` if `s` ends with the byte sequence `suffix`.

items.name: str_index_of
items.doc:
Returns the byte offset of the first occurrence of `needle` in `s`, or -1 if not found.
Empty needle returns 0. Naive search.

items.name: str_contains
items.doc: Returns `true` if `needle` occurs in `s` as a byte substring.

items.name: find_in
items.signature: fn find_in(self: &[u8], haystack: &[u8], from: i64) -> i64

items.name: find_in
items.signature: fn find_in(self: u8, haystack: &[u8], from: i64) -> i64

items.name: str_find_pat
items.doc: Returns the byte offset of the first match of `pat` in `s`, or -1 if none.

items.name: str_contains_pat
items.doc: Returns `true` if `pat` matches anywhere in `s`.

items.name: str_starts_with_pat
items.doc: Returns `true` if `pat` matches at the start of `s`.

items.name: str_trim_start
items.doc: Trims leading ASCII whitespace, returning a sub-`str` of `s`.

items.name: str_trim_end
items.doc: Trims trailing ASCII whitespace, returning a sub-`str` of `s`.

items.name: str_trim
items.doc: Trims leading and trailing ASCII whitespace, returning a sub-`str` of `s`.

items.name: encode_len
items.doc:
Returns the number of bytes needed to encode `cp` in UTF-8 (1..=4), or 0 if `cp` > U+10FFFF.
Surrogates report 3, matching `encode`, which emits U+FFFD (3 bytes) for them.

items.name: seq_len
items.doc:
Returns the length of the UTF-8 sequence whose lead byte is `b` (1..=4).
Stray continuation bytes yield 1 (caller skips one byte).

items.name: decode
items.doc:
Decodes the codepoint at `ptr[0..]`, returning U+FFFD for any invalid sequence.
No bounds check — caller must ensure the full length declared by the lead byte is readable.

items.name: encode
items.signature: fn encode(cp: u32, buf: *mut u8) -> i64
items.doc:
Encodes `cp` as UTF-8 into `buf`, returning the number of bytes written (1..=4).
Surrogate or out-of-range `cp` writes U+FFFD instead (3 bytes written and returned).
`buf` must have at least 4 writable bytes.

items.name: char_count
items.signature: fn char_count(ptr: *const u8, len: i64) -> i64
items.doc:
Counts the codepoints in `ptr[0..len]` — exactly the number of items `Chars` yields.
Steps by lead-byte declared length without validating; a truncated tail counts one per byte.

items.name: is_valid
items.signature: fn is_valid(ptr: *const u8, len: i64) -> bool
items.doc: Returns `true` iff `ptr[0..len]` is well-formed UTF-8 (rejects overlongs and surrogates).

items.name: chars_from_raw
items.signature: fn chars_from_raw(ptr: *const u8, len: i64) -> Chars
items.doc:
Creates a `Chars` iterator over the raw byte range `ptr[0..len]`.
The buffer must stay valid for the iterator's lifetime.

items.doc: Returns an iterator over the codepoints of `s`.

items.name: panic
items.doc:
Aborts execution with an unrecoverable error, printing `panic: <msg>` to stderr.
If a recovery handler is installed (`catch_unwind` or the `#[test]` runner), control
longjmps to it instead of aborting the process.

items.name: assert
items.doc: Panics with `msg` if `cond` is false.

items.name: location
items.signature: fn location(file: &[u8], line: u32, column: u32) -> Location
items.doc: Creates a `Location` for the given source position.

items.name: catch_unwind
items.doc:
Runs `f` under panic recovery: `Ok(())` on normal return, `Err(msg)` if `f` panicked.
The error is the `str` passed to `panic()`. Diverges from Rust: takes a plain fn-ptr
(no closure captures) and captures the message as `str`, not `Box<dyn Any>`.

items.name: is_some
items.doc: Returns `true` if the option is a `Some` value.

items.name: is_none
items.doc: Returns `true` if the option is a `None` value.

items.name: unwrap
items.doc:
Returns the contained `Some` value.
Panics if the value is `None`.

items.name: expect
items.doc:
Returns the contained `Some` value.
Panics with `msg` if the value is `None`.

items.name: unwrap_or
items.signature: fn unwrap_or(self: Option, default: T) -> T
items.doc: Returns the contained `Some` value or the provided `default`.

items.name: unwrap_or_else
items.signature: fn unwrap_or_else(self: Option, f: fn() -> T) -> T
items.doc:
Returns the contained `Some` value or computes one from `f`.
`f` is a fn-pointer; non-capturing closures coerce automatically.

items.name: map
items.signature: fn map(self: Option, f: fn(T) -> U) -> Option
items.doc: Maps `Option<T>` to `Option<U>` by applying `f` to a contained value.

items.name: map_or
items.signature: fn map_or(self: Option, default: U, f: fn(T) -> U) -> U
items.doc: Returns `default` if `None`, otherwise applies `f` to the contained value.

items.name: map_or_else
items.signature: fn map_or_else(self: Option, dfl: fn() -> U, f: fn(T) -> U) -> U
items.doc: Computes a result from `dfl` if `None`, otherwise applies `f` to the contained value.

items.name: ok_or
items.signature: fn ok_or(self: Option, err: E) -> Result
items.doc: Transforms into `Result<T, E>`, mapping `Some(v)` to `Ok(v)` and `None` to `Err(err)`.

items.name: ok_or_else
items.signature: fn ok_or_else(self: Option, err: fn() -> E) -> Result
items.doc: Transforms into `Result<T, E>` like `ok_or`, computing the error lazily from `err`.

items.name: and
items.signature: fn and(self: Option, optb: Option) -> Option
items.doc: Returns `None` if `self` is `None`, otherwise returns `optb`.

items.name: and_then
items.signature: fn and_then(self: Option, f: fn(T) -> Option) -> Option
items.doc: Returns `None` if `self` is `None`, otherwise applies `f` to the wrapped value.

items.name: is_some_and
items.signature: fn is_some_and(self: Option, pred: fn(T) -> bool) -> bool
items.doc: Returns `true` iff the option is `Some` and `pred` accepts the wrapped value.

items.name: or
items.signature: fn or(self: Option, other: Option) -> Option
items.doc: Returns `self` if it contains a value, otherwise returns `other`.

items.name: or_else
items.signature: fn or_else(self: Option, f: fn() -> Option) -> Option
items.doc: Returns `self` if it contains a value, otherwise calls `f` and returns the result.

items.name: take
items.doc: Takes the value out of the option, leaving `None` in its place.

items.name: replace
items.signature: fn replace(self: &mut Option, value: T) -> Option
items.doc: Replaces the value in the option with `value`, returning the old value if present.

items.name: as_ref
items.doc: Converts `&Option<T>` to `Option<&T>`.

items.name: as_mut
items.doc: Converts `&mut Option<T>` to `Option<&mut T>`.

items.name: xor
items.signature: fn xor(self: Option, other: Option) -> Option
items.doc: Returns `Some` iff exactly one of `self` and `other` is `Some`, otherwise `None`.

items.name: filter
items.signature: fn filter(self: Option, pred: fn(T) -> bool) -> Option
items.doc:
Returns `Some(v)` if `self` is `Some(v)` and `pred(v)` is `true`, otherwise `None`.
`pred` receives the wrapped value by bitwise copy; the original is returned untouched.

items.name: option_unzip
items.doc: Unzips `Option<(A, B)>` into `(Option<A>, Option<B>)`; `None` yields `(None, None)`.

items.name: option_zip
items.signature: fn option_zip(a: Option, b: Option) -> Option
items.doc: Zips two options: returns `Some((t, u))` iff both are `Some`, otherwise `None`.

items.name: option_unwrap_or_default
items.doc: Returns the contained `Some` value or `T::default()`.

items.name: option_flatten
items.doc: Flattens `Option<Option<T>>` into `Option<T>`.

items.name: flatten
items.doc: Flattens `Option<Option<T>>` into `Option<T>`.

items.name: unzip
items.doc: Unzips into `(Option<A>, Option<B>)`; `None` yields `(None, None)`.

items.name: transpose
items.doc: Transposes into `Result<Option<T>, E>`; `None` maps to `Ok(None)`.

items.name: unwrap_or_i32
items.signature: fn unwrap_or_i32(opt: Option, def: i32) -> i32
items.doc:
Returns the contained value or `def`.
Compat shim for older call-sites; prefer `opt.unwrap_or(def)`.

items.name: is_some_i32
items.doc:
Returns `true` if `opt` is `Some`.
Compat shim for older call-sites; prefer `opt.is_some()`.

items.name: type_id_of
items.doc:
Returns the `TypeId` of `_T` — the `TypeId::of::<T>()` analogue.
`_T` appears only at the type level (via `type_uid::<_T>()`), never in the
signature; the `_`-prefix keeps the unused-type-param lint quiet. Both halves
are folded at mono.

items.name: any_is
items.doc:
Returns whether the erased value `a` holds a `_T` — the `(dyn Any).is::<T>()` analogue.
Sound for struct/enum erased values only; see the primitive caveat in the module header.

items.name: array_into_iter
items.doc: Converts an owned `[T; N]` array into an iterator that yields its elements by value.

items.name: array_from_ref
items.doc: Views a single reference as a 1-element array reference (zero-cost pointer reinterpret).

items.name: array_from_mut
items.doc: Views a single mutable reference as a 1-element mutable array reference.

items.name: is_ascii
items.doc: Returns `true` if the byte is in the ASCII range (`0..=127`).

items.name: is_ascii_uppercase
items.doc: Returns `true` for ASCII uppercase letters `'A'..='Z'`.

items.name: is_ascii_lowercase
items.doc: Returns `true` for ASCII lowercase letters `'a'..='z'`.

items.name: is_ascii_alphabetic
items.doc: Returns `true` for ASCII letters `'A'..='Z'` or `'a'..='z'`.

items.name: is_ascii_digit
items.doc: Returns `true` for ASCII digits `'0'..='9'`.

items.name: is_ascii_alphanumeric
items.doc: Returns `true` for ASCII letters or digits.

items.name: is_ascii_hexdigit
items.doc: Returns `true` for ASCII hex digits: `0-9`, `A-F`, or `a-f`.

items.name: is_ascii_octdigit
items.doc: Returns `true` for ASCII octal digits `'0'..='7'`.

items.name: is_ascii_whitespace
items.doc: Returns `true` for ASCII whitespace: space, tab, LF, FF, or CR (VT excluded, as in Rust).

items.name: is_ascii_control
items.doc: Returns `true` for ASCII control characters: `0..=31` or DEL (127).

items.name: is_ascii_punctuation
items.doc: Returns `true` for ASCII punctuation (byte ranges 33..=47, 58..=64, 91..=96, 123..=126).

items.name: is_ascii_graphic
items.doc: Returns `true` for printable ASCII characters excluding space: `'!'..='~'` (33..=126).

items.name: to_ascii_uppercase
items.doc: Returns the byte with `'a'..='z'` mapped to `'A'..='Z'`; all other bytes unchanged.

items.name: to_ascii_lowercase
items.doc: Returns the byte with `'A'..='Z'` mapped to `'a'..='z'`; all other bytes unchanged.

items.name: eq_ignore_ascii_case
items.doc: Returns `true` if the two bytes are equal ignoring ASCII case.

items.name: make_ascii_uppercase
items.doc: Converts the byte to ASCII uppercase in place.

items.name: make_ascii_lowercase
items.doc: Converts the byte to ASCII lowercase in place.

items.name: from_u8
items.doc: Creates an `AsciiChar` from a byte, returning `None` if `b >= 128`.

items.name: from_u8_unchecked
items.doc:
Creates an `AsciiChar` without range validation.

Caller must guarantee `b < 128`; a larger byte silently breaks the type invariant.

items.name: as_u8
items.doc: Returns the underlying byte value.

items.name: as_char
items.doc: Returns the character widened to `char` (same codepoint).

items.name: is_alphabetic
items.doc: Returns `true` for ASCII letters `'A'..='Z'` or `'a'..='z'`.

items.name: is_lowercase
items.doc: Returns `true` for ASCII lowercase letters `'a'..='z'`.

items.name: is_uppercase
items.doc: Returns `true` for ASCII uppercase letters `'A'..='Z'`.

items.name: is_digit
items.doc: Returns `true` for ASCII digits `'0'..='9'`.

items.name: is_alphanumeric
items.doc: Returns `true` for ASCII letters or digits.

items.name: is_hexdigit
items.doc: Returns `true` for ASCII hex digits: `0-9`, `A-F`, or `a-f`.

items.name: is_whitespace
items.doc:
Returns `true` for whitespace: space, tab, LF, VT, FF, or CR.

Includes VT (0x0B), unlike `u8::is_ascii_whitespace`.

items.name: is_control
items.doc: Returns `true` for ASCII control characters: `0..=31` or DEL (127).

items.name: to_ascii_lowercase
items.doc: Returns the character lowercased if `'A'..='Z'`, unchanged otherwise.

items.name: to_ascii_uppercase
items.doc: Returns the character uppercased if `'a'..='z'`, unchanged otherwise.

items.name: ascii_null
items.doc: Returns the NUL character (`0x00`).

items.name: ascii_bell
items.doc: Returns the BEL (bell) character (`0x07`).

items.name: ascii_backspace
items.doc: Returns the BS (backspace) character (`0x08`).

items.name: ascii_tab
items.doc: Returns the TAB character (`0x09`).

items.name: ascii_line_feed
items.doc: Returns the LF (line feed) character (`0x0A`).

items.name: ascii_vert_tab
items.doc: Returns the VT (vertical tab) character (`0x0B`).

items.name: ascii_form_feed
items.doc: Returns the FF (form feed) character (`0x0C`).

items.name: ascii_carriage_ret
items.doc: Returns the CR (carriage return) character (`0x0D`).

items.name: ascii_escape
items.doc: Returns the ESC (escape) character (`0x1B`).

items.name: ascii_space
items.doc: Returns the SPACE character (`0x20`).

items.name: ascii_delete
items.doc: Returns the DEL character (`0x7F`).

items.name: drop
items.doc:
Takes ownership of `value` and drops it immediately (mirrors `core::mem::drop`).
The by-value parameter is dropped when this function returns, so callers can
force an early drop of an owned value (`drop(x);`) before its lexical scope
would otherwise end. Generic over all `T`; for a non-droppable `T` this is a
no-op move.

items.name: cell_take
items.doc: Takes the cell's value, leaving `T::default()` in its place.

items.name: ref_map
items.signature: fn ref_map(orig: Ref<T>, f: fn(&T) -> &U) -> Ref<U>
items.doc: Maps a `Ref<T>` into a `Ref<U>` for a component of the borrowed data, keeping the borrow.

items.name: ref_filter_map
items.signature: fn ref_filter_map(orig: Ref<T>, f: fn(&T) -> Option) -> Result
items.doc: Maps a `Ref<T>` to `Ref<U>` if `f` yields `Some`, else returns the original guard in `Err`.

items.name: ref_mut_map
items.signature: fn ref_mut_map(orig: RefMut<T>, f: fn(&mut T) -> &mut U) -> RefMut<U>
items.doc: Maps a `RefMut<T>` into a `RefMut<U>` for a component of the borrowed data.

items.name: refcell_take
items.doc:
Takes the wrapped value, leaving `T::default()` in its place.
Panics if the value is currently borrowed.

items.name: once_cell_new
items.doc: Creates an empty `OnceCell`; storage is seeded with `T::default()` but never observed.

items.name: lazy_cell_new
items.doc: Creates a `LazyCell` that initialises with `init` on first `force`.

items.doc: Stores `v`; the ordering argument is currently ignored (seq-cst).

items.name: fetch_add_ordered
items.signature: fn fetch_add_ordered(self: *mut AtomicI16, delta: i16, _ord: Ordering) -> i16
items.doc: Atomically adds `delta`, returning the previous value; ordering currently ignored (seq-cst).

items.name: then
items.signature: fn then(self: bool, f: fn() -> T) -> Option
items.doc: Returns `Some(f())` if `self` is `true`, else `None`.

items.name: then_some
items.signature: fn then_some(self: bool, x: T) -> Option
items.doc: Returns `Some(x)` if `self` is `true`, else `None`.

items.name: ok_or
items.signature: fn ok_or(self: bool, err: E) -> Result
items.doc: Returns `Ok(())` if `self` is `true`, else `Err(err)`.

items.name: ok_or_else
items.signature: fn ok_or_else(self: bool, f: fn() -> E) -> Result
items.doc: Returns `Ok(())` if `self` is `true`, else `Err(f())`.

items.name: is_ascii
items.doc: Returns `true` if `self` is in the ASCII range (`U+0000..=U+007F`).

items.name: is_ascii_alphabetic
items.doc: Returns `true` if `self` is an ASCII letter (`a-z` or `A-Z`).

items.name: is_ascii_lowercase
items.doc: Returns `true` if `self` is an ASCII lowercase letter (`a-z`).

items.name: is_ascii_uppercase
items.doc: Returns `true` if `self` is an ASCII uppercase letter (`A-Z`).

items.name: is_ascii_digit
items.doc: Returns `true` if `self` is an ASCII decimal digit (`0-9`).

items.name: is_ascii_alphanumeric
items.doc: Returns `true` if `self` is an ASCII letter or decimal digit.

items.name: is_ascii_hexdigit
items.doc: Returns `true` if `self` is an ASCII hexadecimal digit (`0-9`, `a-f`, `A-F`).

items.name: is_ascii_whitespace
items.doc: Returns `true` if `self` is ASCII whitespace: space, tab, LF, FF, or CR.

items.name: is_ascii_control
items.doc: Returns `true` if `self` is an ASCII control character (`U+0000..=U+001F` or `U+007F`).

items.name: is_ascii_punctuation
items.doc: Returns `true` if `self` is an ASCII punctuation character (graphic and non-alphanumeric).

items.name: is_ascii_graphic
items.doc: Returns `true` if `self` is an ASCII graphic character (`U+0021..=U+007E`).

items.name: is_whitespace
items.doc:
Returns `true` if `self` is whitespace.

Covers ASCII whitespace plus `U+00A0`, `U+2007`, `U+202F` only — not the full Unicode set.

items.name: is_alphabetic
items.doc: Returns `true` if `self` is alphabetic (ASCII letters only for now; no Unicode tables).

items.name: is_lowercase
items.doc: Returns `true` if `self` is lowercase (ASCII subset only for now).

items.name: is_uppercase
items.doc: Returns `true` if `self` is uppercase (ASCII subset only for now).

items.name: is_numeric
items.doc: Returns `true` if `self` is numeric (ASCII decimal digits only for now).

items.name: is_alphanumeric
items.doc: Returns `true` if `self` is alphanumeric (ASCII subset only for now).

items.name: is_control
items.doc: Returns `true` if `self` is a control character (ASCII controls only for now).

items.name: to_ascii_lowercase
items.doc: Returns the ASCII-lowercase equivalent of `self`; chars other than `A-Z` are unchanged.

items.name: to_ascii_uppercase
items.doc: Returns the ASCII-uppercase equivalent of `self`; chars other than `a-z` are unchanged.

items.name: to_digit
items.signature: fn to_digit(self: char, radix: u32) -> Option
items.doc:
Returns the value of `self` as a digit in `radix`, or `None` if it is not one.

Digits are `0-9` and case-insensitive `a-z` for values 10-35, filtered by `radix`.
Unlike Rust, a `radix` outside `2..=36` does not panic.

items.name: len_utf8
items.doc: Returns the number of bytes (1-4) `self` occupies when encoded as UTF-8.

items.name: is_continue
items.doc: Returns `true` if `self` is `ControlFlow::Continue`.

items.name: is_break
items.doc: Returns `true` if `self` is `ControlFlow::Break`.

items.name: error_chain_len
items.doc:
Counts links in the error chain starting at `e` (1 if `e` has no source, 2 if
it sources one error, ...).
Walks until `source` returns `None`; bounded by the chain's depth — caller's
responsibility if cycles are introduced via `source()`.

items.name: size

items.name: null
items.doc:
Returns the null `*const T`.

Safe to call; reading or writing through the returned pointer is UB.

items.name: null_mut
items.doc:
Returns the null `*mut T`.

Safe to call; reading or writing through the returned pointer is UB.

items.name: read
items.doc:
Reads and returns the `T` at `src`, taking ownership (bit-for-bit copy).

`src` must point at an initialized `T`; for non-`Copy` `T` the source
location must not be read again afterwards (by-value move semantics).

items.name: write
items.signature: fn write(dst: *mut T, src: T) -> void
items.doc:
Writes `src` to `dst` without reading or dropping the previous pointee.

Safe to use on uninitialized destinations (`MaybeUninit` / collection internals).

items.name: copy
items.signature: fn copy(src: *const T, dst: *mut T, count: i64) -> void
items.doc:
Copies `count` elements from `src` to `dst`; the regions may overlap.

`memmove` semantics — the copy direction is chosen to handle overlap correctly.

items.name: copy_nonoverlapping
items.signature: fn copy_nonoverlapping(src: *const T, dst: *mut T, count: i64) -> void
items.doc:
Copies `count` elements from `src` to `dst`; the regions must not overlap.

Cheaper than `copy` — no overlap direction check. Overlapping regions are UB.

items.name: swap
items.signature: fn swap(x: *mut T, y: *mut T) -> void
items.doc: Swaps the values at `x` and `y`.

items.name: replace
items.signature: fn replace(dst: *mut T, src: T) -> T
items.doc: Replaces the value at `dst` with `src` and returns the old value.

items.name: drop_in_place
items.doc:
Executes the pointee's `Drop` in place, leaving the storage untouched.

Runs only a user `impl Drop`; fields are NOT recursively dropped (Logos
explicit-`Drop` convention). The storage must not be read or re-dropped after.

items.name: eq
items.signature: fn eq(a: *const T, b: *const T) -> bool
items.doc: Returns `true` if `a` and `b` have the same address (pointee values ignored).

items.name: addr_eq
items.signature: fn addr_eq(a: *const T, b: *const U) -> bool
items.doc: Returns `true` if `a` and `b` have the same address, across pointee types.

items.name: read_volatile
items.doc:
Reads the `T` at `src` with volatile semantics.

Currently a plain load — the backend has no volatile op yet (see section note).

items.name: write_volatile
items.signature: fn write_volatile(dst: *mut T, src: T) -> void
items.doc:
Writes `src` to `dst` with volatile semantics.

Currently a plain store — the backend has no volatile op yet (see section note).

items.name: read_unaligned
items.doc:
Reads the `T` at `src` without an alignment requirement.

Plain load today; x86-64 tolerates unaligned scalar access (see section note).

items.name: write_unaligned
items.signature: fn write_unaligned(dst: *mut T, src: T) -> void
items.doc:
Writes `src` to `dst` without an alignment requirement.

Plain store today; x86-64 tolerates unaligned scalar access (see section note).

items.name: fnv1a_bytes
items.signature: fn fnv1a_bytes(ptr: *const u8, len: u64) -> u64
items.doc:
Computes 64-bit FNV-1a over the byte range `[ptr, ptr + len)`.

Byte-slice hash for string / byte-buffer keys — the case `FxHasher`'s typed writers
don't cover. Non-cryptographic; adequate for hash-map collision distribution on
non-adversarial inputs. Caller must ensure `ptr` is valid for `len` bytes.

items.name: black_box
items.doc: Returns `x` unchanged, as an optimization barrier the compiler cannot fold across calls.

items.name: spin_loop
items.doc:
Hints that the caller is in a busy-wait spin loop.
No-op today; backend would emit `pause` (x86) / `yield` (AArch64) here.

items.name: unreachable_unchecked
items.doc: Asserts this point is unreachable; diverges via `abort()` if control reaches it anyway.

items.name: PI
items.doc: Returns π.

items.name: TAU
items.doc: Returns τ = 2π.

items.name: E
items.doc: Returns Euler's number e.

items.name: SQRT_2
items.doc: Returns √2.

items.name: LN_2
items.doc: Returns ln 2.

items.name: LN_10
items.doc: Returns ln 10.

items.name: sqrt_f64
items.doc: Returns the square root of `x` (NaN for `x < 0`).

items.name: cbrt_f64
items.doc: Returns the cube root of `x`.

items.name: hypot_f64
items.signature: fn hypot_f64(x: f64, y: f64) -> f64
items.doc: Returns the Euclidean distance `sqrt(x*x + y*y)`, avoiding intermediate overflow/underflow.

items.name: sin_f64
items.doc: Returns the sine of `x` (radians).

items.name: cos_f64
items.doc: Returns the cosine of `x` (radians).

items.name: tan_f64
items.doc: Returns the tangent of `x` (radians).

items.name: asin_f64
items.doc: Returns the arcsine of `x` in radians, in `[-π/2, π/2]` (NaN for `|x| > 1`).

items.name: acos_f64
items.doc: Returns the arccosine of `x` in radians, in `[0, π]` (NaN for `|x| > 1`).

items.name: atan_f64
items.doc: Returns the arctangent of `x` in radians, in `(-π/2, π/2)`.

items.name: atan2_f64
items.signature: fn atan2_f64(y: f64, x: f64) -> f64
items.doc:
Returns the angle of the point `(x, y)` in radians, in `(-π, π]`.
Note the `(y, x)` argument order.

items.name: sinh_f64
items.doc: Returns the hyperbolic sine of `x`.

items.name: cosh_f64
items.doc: Returns the hyperbolic cosine of `x`.

items.name: tanh_f64
items.doc: Returns the hyperbolic tangent of `x`.

items.name: exp_f64
items.doc: Returns e raised to the power `x`.

items.name: exp2_f64
items.doc: Returns 2 raised to the power `x`.

items.name: expm1_f64
items.doc: Returns `e^x - 1`, computed accurately for `x` near zero.

items.name: ln_f64
items.doc: Returns the natural logarithm of `x` (NaN for `x < 0`, `-inf` for `x == 0`).

items.name: log2_f64
items.doc: Returns the base-2 logarithm of `x` (NaN for `x < 0`, `-inf` for `x == 0`).

items.name: log10_f64
items.doc: Returns the base-10 logarithm of `x` (NaN for `x < 0`, `-inf` for `x == 0`).

items.name: ln_1p_f64
items.doc: Returns `ln(1 + x)`, computed accurately for `x` near zero (NaN for `x < -1`).

items.name: pow_f64
items.signature: fn pow_f64(x: f64, y: f64) -> f64
items.doc: Returns `x` raised to the power `y`.

items.name: floor_f64
items.doc: Returns the largest integral value `<= x` (rounds toward `-inf`).

items.name: ceil_f64
items.doc: Returns the smallest integral value `>= x` (rounds toward `+inf`).

items.name: round_f64
items.doc: Rounds `x` to the nearest integral value, ties away from zero.

items.name: trunc_f64
items.doc: Returns the integral part of `x` (rounds toward zero).

items.name: abs_f64
items.doc: Returns the absolute value of `x`.

items.name: fmod_f64
items.signature: fn fmod_f64(x: f64, y: f64) -> f64
items.doc: Returns the remainder of `x / y` with the sign of `x` (NaN for `y == 0`).

items.name: copysign_f64
items.signature: fn copysign_f64(x: f64, y: f64) -> f64
items.doc: Returns a value with the magnitude of `x` and the sign of `y`.

items.name: abs_i64
items.doc:
Returns the absolute value of `x`.
Traps on `i64::MIN` (negation overflows).

items.name: abs_i32
items.doc:
Returns the absolute value of `x`.
Traps on `i32::MIN` (negation overflows).

items.name: min_i64
items.signature: fn min_i64(a: i64, b: i64) -> i64
items.doc: Returns the smaller of `a` and `b`.

items.name: max_i64
items.signature: fn max_i64(a: i64, b: i64) -> i64
items.doc: Returns the larger of `a` and `b`.

items.name: min_i32
items.signature: fn min_i32(a: i32, b: i32) -> i32
items.doc: Returns the smaller of `a` and `b`.

items.name: max_i32
items.signature: fn max_i32(a: i32, b: i32) -> i32
items.doc: Returns the larger of `a` and `b`.

items.name: min_f64
items.signature: fn min_f64(a: f64, b: f64) -> f64
items.doc:
Returns the smaller of `a` and `b`.
Returns `b` whenever `a < b` is false, so NaN handling differs from libm `fmin`.

items.name: max_f64
items.signature: fn max_f64(a: f64, b: f64) -> f64
items.doc:
Returns the larger of `a` and `b`.
Returns `b` whenever `a > b` is false, so NaN handling differs from libm `fmax`.

items.name: new
items.doc: Creates a `ParseIntError` with the given `kind`.

items.name: new
items.doc: Creates a `TryFromIntError` with the given `kind`.

items.name: new
items.doc: Creates a `ParseFloatError` with the given `kind`.

items.name: f32_to_bits
items.doc: Returns the raw IEEE 754 bit pattern of `x` as a `u32` (same as `f32::to_bits`).

items.name: f32_from_bits
items.doc:
Reinterprets the IEEE 754 bit pattern `b` as an `f32` (same as `f32::from_bits`).
Any bit pattern is accepted, including ones that decode to NaN or infinity.

items.name: f64_to_bits
items.doc: Returns the raw IEEE 754 bit pattern of `x` as a `u64` (same as `f64::to_bits`).

items.name: f64_from_bits
items.doc:
Reinterprets the IEEE 754 bit pattern `b` as an `f64` (same as `f64::from_bits`).
Any bit pattern is accepted, including ones that decode to NaN or infinity.

items.name: f32_classify
items.doc: Returns the `FpCategory` of `x` by IEEE 754 exponent/mantissa inspection (`f32::classify`).

items.name: i32_from_str_radix
items.doc:
Parses `s` as an `i32` in the given `radix`, mirroring `i32::from_str_radix`.
Accepts a leading `+`/`-`; errors: `Empty`, `InvalidDigit`, `PosOverflow`/`NegOverflow`.
`radix` is unvalidated (no panic outside `2..=36`, unlike Rust); digit set `0-9`, `a-z`/`A-Z`.

items.name: i64_from_str_radix
items.doc:
Parses `s` as an `i64` in the given `radix`, mirroring `i64::from_str_radix`.
Accepts a leading `+`/`-`; errors: `Empty`, `InvalidDigit`, `PosOverflow`/`NegOverflow`.
`radix` is not validated (no panic outside `2..=36`, unlike Rust).
Trap: `i64::MIN` itself is rejected as `NegOverflow` (magnitude is capped at `i64::MAX`).

items.name: u32_from_str_radix
items.doc:
Parses `s` as a `u32` in the given `radix`, mirroring `u32::from_str_radix`.
Accepts a leading `+`; a leading `-` is `InvalidDigit`. Errors: `Empty`,
`InvalidDigit`, `PosOverflow`. `radix` is not validated (no panic outside `2..=36`).

items.name: u64_from_str_radix
items.doc:
Parses `s` as a `u64` in the given `radix`, mirroring `u64::from_str_radix`.
Accepts a leading `+`; a leading `-` is `InvalidDigit`. Errors: `Empty`,
`InvalidDigit`, `PosOverflow`. `radix` is not validated (no panic outside `2..=36`).

items.name: i32_from_str
items.doc: Parses `s` as a base-10 `i32`; `s.parse::<i32>()` lowers here.

items.name: i64_from_str
items.doc: Parses `s` as a base-10 `i64`; `s.parse::<i64>()` lowers here.

items.name: u32_from_str
items.doc: Parses `s` as a base-10 `u32`; `s.parse::<u32>()` lowers here.

items.name: u64_from_str
items.doc: Parses `s` as a base-10 `u64`; `s.parse::<u64>()` lowers here.

items.name: f64_from_str
items.doc:
Parses `s` as an `f64`, mirroring `f64::from_str`; the whole input must match.
Accepts optional `+`/`-`, decimal mantissa with optional fraction, optional
`e`/`E` exponent, and case-insensitive `inf`/`infinity`/`nan`.
Not correctly rounded: accurate to a few ulp (unlike Rust's exact `dec2flt`).

items.name: f32_from_str
items.doc:
Parses `s` as an `f32` by parsing as `f64` and narrowing (see `f64_from_str`).
Double rounding may differ from a direct `f32` parse by an ulp.

items.name: contains
items.signature: fn contains(self: RangeInclI32, item: i32) -> bool
items.doc: Returns `true` if `item` lies in the closed interval `[current, end]`.

items.name: is_empty
items.doc: Returns `true` once the range has yielded its final value (or was constructed empty).

items.name: contains
items.signature: fn contains(self: RangeInclI64, item: i64) -> bool
items.doc: Returns `true` if `item` lies in the closed interval `[current, end]`.

items.name: is_empty
items.doc: Returns `true` once the range has yielded its final value (or was constructed empty).

items.name: rc_new
items.doc: Creates a new `Rc<T>` owning `val` in a fresh single-block heap allocation (strong = 1).

items.name: slice_split_at
items.doc:
Splits `s` at `mid`, returning raw `(ptr, len)` pairs for both halves.
`mid` is clamped to `s.len()` (no panic, unlike Rust). Rebuild typed `&[T]` halves
with `slice_from_raw`.

items.name: slice_get_range
items.signature: fn slice_get_range(s: &[T], lo: i64, hi: i64) -> &[T]
items.doc:
Returns the sub-slice `s[lo..hi]` (backs the range-indexing desugar).
Bounds are clamped to `[0, len]`: open range forms pass `lo=0` / `hi=i64::MAX` and the
clamp resolves them against the true length. Inclusive `..=hi` passes `hi+1`.
Empty when `hi <= lo` after clamping.

items.name: slice_from_raw
items.doc:
Constructs an `&[T]` fat pointer from a raw pointer and element count.
Compiler intrinsic (sema intercepts the call); mirrors `str_from_raw` in `lang/str`.
`ptr` must be valid for reads of `len` elements for as long as the result is used.

items.name: slice_chunks
items.doc:
Returns an iterator over non-overlapping `size`-element chunks of `s`.
The last chunk is shorter when `s.len() % size != 0`; empty input yields no chunks.
`size` must be positive: non-positive `size` is unchecked, and `size == 0` makes `next`
yield empty chunks forever on a non-empty slice.

items.name: slice_windows
items.doc:
Returns an iterator over overlapping `size`-element windows of `s`, advancing by 1.
Yields nothing if `size <= 0` or `size > s.len()` (unlike Rust, `size == 0` does not panic).

items.name: slice_binary_search
items.doc:
Binary-searches sorted `s` for `target`.
Returns `Ok(index)` on a match (any one, if `target` is duplicated) or `Err(i)` where `i`
is the insertion index that keeps `s` sorted. Unspecified result if `s` is not sorted.

items.name: slice_sort
items.signature: fn slice_sort(base: *mut T, n: i64) -> void
items.doc:
Sorts the `n` elements at `base` in place, ascending (stable insertion sort, O(n^2)).
`base` must be valid for reads and writes of `n` elements. Intended for small `n`.

items.name: unicode_general_category
items.doc:
Returns the Unicode general category of codepoint `cp`.
Accurate only for ASCII + Latin-1 Supplement (U+0000–U+00FF), with Pd/Ps/Pe lumped into
`Po`; every codepoint above U+00FF maps to `GeneralCategory::Other`.

items.name: unicode_is_alphabetic
items.doc:
Returns `true` if `cp` is a letter (`Lu`, `Ll`, or `Lo`).
Category coverage stops at U+00FF; letters above that return `false`.

items.name: unicode_is_lowercase
items.doc:
Returns `true` if `cp` is a lowercase letter (`Ll`).
Category coverage stops at U+00FF; lowercase letters above that return `false`.

items.name: unicode_is_uppercase
items.doc:
Returns `true` if `cp` is an uppercase letter (`Lu`).
Category coverage stops at U+00FF; uppercase letters above that return `false`.

items.name: unicode_is_numeric
items.doc:
Returns `true` if `cp` is a decimal digit (`Nd`).
With the current table only ASCII `0`–`9` qualify.

items.name: unicode_is_whitespace
items.doc:
Returns `true` if `cp` has the Unicode `White_Space` property.
Uses an explicit full list (not limited to U+00FF), but omits U+0085 (NEL), unlike UCD
and Rust's `char::is_whitespace`.

items.name: unicode_is_control
items.doc:
Returns `true` if `cp` is a control code (`Cc`): U+0000–U+001F or U+007F–U+009F.
Exact for all of Unicode — `Cc` exists only in those ranges.

items.name: vlen_write
items.signature: fn vlen_write(dst: *mut u8, value: u64) -> u32

items.name: vec_new
items.doc: Creates an empty `Vec<T>`; allocates an initial capacity of 8 up front.

items.name: vec_from_arr
items.doc:
Builds a `Vec` from a fixed-size array, inferring element type and length from the
argument shape (no turbofish). `vec!(1, 2, 3)` lowers to exactly this call.

items.name: vec_contains
items.doc:
Returns `true` if `v` contains an element equal to `val` — linear scan, requires `T: Eq`.
Free-fn form keeps the base `impl<T> Vec<T>` bound-free; see also `Vec::contains`.

items.name: iter_collect_result_vec
items.doc:
Collects an iterator of `Result<T, E>` into `Result<Vec<T>, E>`,
short-circuiting on the first `Err`.

items.name: iter_collect_vec
items.doc: Drains an iterator into a new `Vec<T>`.

items.name: iter_partition_vec
items.signature: fn iter_partition_vec(iter: I, pred: fn(T) -> bool) -> ZipPair<Vec<T>, Vec<T>>
items.doc:
Splits an iterator into two `Vec`s by a predicate: items where `pred` is true go to
`first`, the rest to `second`. Rust's `Iterator::partition::<Vec<T>>` in free-fn form.

items.name: raw
items.doc: Returns the raw 8-byte word: Pod → the tagged word, Ref → the absolute pointer bits.

items.name: null
items.doc: Creates the null `WAny` (`Ref(0)`; raw word 0).

items.name: pod
items.signature: fn pod(v: i64, code: u8) -> WAny
items.doc:
Creates an inline Pod: `code` = 7-bit Writ type code (1..=127); `v` must fit
56 signed bits (see `fits_i56`) — wider values box into a Ref instead.

items.name: pod_bool
items.signature: fn pod_bool(b: bool, code: u8) -> WAny
items.doc: Creates an inline boolean Pod (value 1/0) tagged with `code`.

items.name: ref_to
items.doc: Creates a `WAny` referencing the tagged zone object `obj` (absolute value form).

items.name: is_null
items.doc: Returns `true` iff the raw word is 0 (the null reference).

items.name: is_pod
items.doc: Returns `true` iff this holds an inline Pod (low bit 1).

items.name: is_ref
items.doc: Returns `true` iff this holds a non-null Ref (low bit 0, word != 0).

items.name: pod_code
items.doc: Returns the Pod's 7-bit type code (bits[7:1]); meaningful only when `is_pod()`.

items.name: as_i56
items.doc: Returns the inline Pod value: bits[63:8] sign-extended to `i64`.

items.name: as_bool
items.doc: Returns `true` iff the inline Pod value (bits[63:8]) is nonzero.

items.name: resolve
items.doc: Returns the Ref's absolute tagged-object pointer; safe to obtain, `unsafe` to dereference.

items.name: fits_i56
items.doc: Returns `true` iff `v` fits the 56-bit signed inline Pod range (else it must be boxed).

items.name: w_type_code
items.doc:
Reads the in-band type tag written immediately before a tagged arena object (legacy
datatag varint): `obj[-1]` in 1..=222 is the code directly; a header 223..=230 means
`header-222` little-endian code bytes follow at `obj[-2]`, `obj[-3]`, …; 0 or >230 = unset (0).

items.name: from
items.doc: Creates a `WA_I56` inline Pod; the `i56` parameter makes the inline range a type constraint.

items.name: from
items.doc: Creates a `WA_BOOL` inline Pod from `b`.

items.name: from
items.doc: Creates a `WT_I8`-tagged inline Pod (exact width preserved on read-back).

items.name: from
items.doc: Creates a `WT_U8`-tagged inline Pod.

items.name: from
items.doc: Creates a `WT_I16`-tagged inline Pod.

items.name: from
items.doc: Creates a `WT_I24`-tagged inline Pod.

items.name: from
items.doc: Creates a `WT_U16`-tagged inline Pod.

items.name: from
items.doc: Creates a `WT_U24`-tagged inline Pod.

items.name: as_i8
items.doc: Returns the inline value narrowed to `i8`; a wrong accessor truncates (pair with `is_i8`).

items.name: as_u8
items.doc: Returns the inline value narrowed to `u8`; truncates on mismatch (pair with `is_u8`).

items.name: as_i16
items.doc: Returns the inline value narrowed to `i16`; truncates on mismatch (pair with `is_i16`).

items.name: as_i24
items.doc: Returns the inline value narrowed to `i24`; truncates on mismatch (pair with `is_i24`).

items.name: as_u16
items.doc: Returns the inline value narrowed to `u16`; truncates on mismatch (pair with `is_u16`).

items.name: as_u24
items.doc: Returns the inline value narrowed to `u24`; truncates on mismatch (pair with `is_u24`).

items.name: is_i56
items.doc: Returns `true` iff an inline Pod tagged `WA_I56`.

items.name: is_bool
items.doc: Returns `true` iff an inline Pod tagged `WA_BOOL`.

items.name: is_i8
items.doc: Returns `true` iff an inline Pod tagged `WT_I8`.

items.name: is_u8
items.doc: Returns `true` iff an inline Pod tagged `WT_U8`.

items.name: is_i16
items.doc: Returns `true` iff an inline Pod tagged `WT_I16`.

items.name: is_i24
items.doc: Returns `true` iff an inline Pod tagged `WT_I24`.

items.name: is_u16
items.doc: Returns `true` iff an inline Pod tagged `WT_U16`.

items.name: is_u24
items.doc: Returns `true` iff an inline Pod tagged `WT_U24`.

items.name: type_code
items.doc:
Returns the unified type code: Pod → inline pod code, Ref → the pointee's
in-band tag, null → 0.

items.name: is_string
items.doc: Returns `true` iff a Ref to a `W_STRING` arena object.

items.name: is_array
items.doc: Returns `true` iff a Ref to a `W_ARRAY` arena object.

items.name: is_map
items.doc: Returns `true` iff a Ref to a `W_MAP` arena object.

items.name: is_tinymap
items.doc: Returns `true` iff a Ref to a `W_TINYMAP` (bitmap-indexed tiny map) object.

items.name: is_int
items.doc:
Returns `true` iff an integer in any form: inline Pod (`WA_I56` or exact-width)
or boxed `W_I64`/`W_U64`.

items.name: is_u64
items.doc: Returns `true` iff a Ref to a boxed `W_U64` object (inline Pods never carry u64).

items.name: is_f32
items.doc: Returns `true` iff a Ref to a boxed `W_F32` object.

items.name: is_float
items.doc: Returns `true` iff a Ref to a boxed float (`W_F32` or `W_F64`); floats never inline.

items.name: as_i64
items.doc:
Returns the integer value: inline Pod → sign-extended i56, boxed Ref → the
64-bit pointee, null → 0 (ADR 0011 absent-key default).

items.name: as_u64
items.doc:
Returns the unsigned value: inline Pod → `as_i56() as u64`, boxed Ref → the
64-bit pointee, null → 0.

items.name: as_f32
items.doc: Returns the boxed `f32` value; null or Pod → `0.0` (floats are never inline).

items.name: as_f64
items.doc: Returns the boxed `f64` value; null or Pod → `0.0`.

items.name: get
items.doc: Reads the element value (materialises the at-rest slot to an absolute `WAny`).

items.name: set
items.doc: Replaces the element in place (lowers the new value into the slot).

items.name: type_code
items.doc: Returns the materialised element's unified type code (see `WAny::type_code`).

items.name: is_pod
items.doc: Returns `true` iff the element is an inline Pod.

items.name: is_ref
items.doc: Returns `true` iff the element is a non-null Ref.

items.name: is_string
items.doc: Returns `true` iff the element is a `W_STRING` object.

items.name: is_array
items.doc: Returns `true` iff the element is a `W_ARRAY` object.

items.name: is_map
items.doc: Returns `true` iff the element is a `W_MAP` object.

items.name: as_i56
items.doc: Returns the element's inline Pod value, sign-extended from 56 bits.

items.name: as_any
items.doc: Returns the materialised element value at this cursor.

items.name: dv_space
items.doc: Returns the code-space selector (high 8 bits) of a `DView` tag.

items.name: dv_code
items.doc: Returns the within-space code (low 56 bits) of a `DView` tag.

items.name: dv_make_tag
items.signature: fn dv_make_tag(space: u8, code: u64) -> u64
items.doc: Packs `(space, code)` into a `DView` tag; `code` is truncated to its low 56 bits.

items.name: from_datum
items.signature: fn from_datum(self: *const i64, d: DView) -> i64

items.name: from_datum
items.signature: fn from_datum(self: *const u64, d: DView) -> u64

items.name: from_datum
items.signature: fn from_datum(self: *const bool, d: DView) -> bool

items.name: from
items.doc:
Type-erases a concrete value into a non-owning view (borrows `v`) by asking the type to
present its storage form. `T` is inferred (`DView::from(&v)`).

items.name: from_parts
items.signature: fn from_parts(obj: *const u8, len: u64, tag: u64, meta: u64) -> DView
items.doc: Creates a view from explicit parts (for in-container views: a stored object + tag).

items.name: from_str
items.doc:
Type-erases a string into a `W_STRING` view over its UTF-8 bytes (borrows `s`).
Dedicated because `str` is unsized (a `{ptr,len}` fat value) and can't ride the generic
`from<T>`; the byte block IS the Writ String content, so it stays in the Writ space.

items.name: as_str
items.doc:
Materializes a `str` over the view's bytes; borrows the same bytes the view does
(valid for the view's lifetime). No tag check.

items.name: type_code
items.doc: Returns the full tag word (code-space selector + code).

items.name: obj_ptr
items.doc: Returns the borrowed pointer to the object bytes.

items.name: obj_len
items.doc: Returns the object byte length.

items.name: meta_word
items.doc: Returns the packed per-value metadata word (0 if none).

items.name: is_a
items.doc: Returns whether the view's tag equals `T`'s `Datum` tag.

items.name: try_to
items.doc: Materializes a `T` from the view if the tag matches; `None` on a tag mismatch.

items.name: to
items.doc: Materializes a `T` from the view without a tag check; a mismatched tag reads garbage.

items.name: compare
items.doc:
Compares two views' object bytes via the shared tag-keyed layer (`htag_cmp`).
Dispatches on `self.tag`; both views must carry the same tag.

items.name: load_le
items.signature: fn load_le(p: *const u8, n: u64) -> u64

items.name: htag_cmp
items.signature: fn htag_cmp(tag: u64, meta: u64, a: *const u8, alen: u64, b: *const u8, blen: u64) -> Ordering
items.doc:
Compares two same-tagged object-byte runs (total order), dispatched on `(space, code)`.
Only the Writ space (0) is wired today; unrecognized codes fall back to unsigned LE compare.
Reads via LE byte assembly, so possibly-unaligned borrowed offsets are fine.

items.name: htag_size
items.signature: fn htag_size(tag: u64, meta: u64) -> u64
items.doc: Returns the object byte stride for a tag (the at-rest/store path); 0 = unknown.

items.name: htag_align
items.signature: fn htag_align(tag: u64, meta: u64) -> u64
items.doc:
Returns the required alignment of a tag's object bytes (a power of two).
The Buffer aligns every element's entry to this so in-place reads (`to::<T>` pointer-casts,
a byte-copied compactified Writ object) are valid.

items.name: align_up
items.signature: fn align_up(x: u64, a: u64) -> u64

items.name: dvs_declare
items.signature: fn dvs_declare(s: *mut DViewStore, v: DView) -> void

items.name: dvs_accepts
items.signature: fn dvs_accepts(s: *const DViewStore, v: DView) -> bool

items.name: storage_get
items.signature: fn storage_get(s: *const DViewStore, idx: u64) -> DView

items.name: storage_push
items.signature: fn storage_push(s: *mut DViewStore, v: DView) -> void

items.name: storage_set
items.signature: fn storage_set(s: *mut DViewStore, idx: u64, v: DView) -> void

items.name: storage_insert_at
items.signature: fn storage_insert_at(s: *mut DViewStore, idx: u64, v: DView) -> void

items.name: storage_remove_at
items.signature: fn storage_remove_at(s: *mut DViewStore, idx: u64) -> void

items.name: store_push
items.signature: fn store_push(s: *mut DViewStore, v: DView) -> void

items.name: store_get
items.signature: fn store_get(s: *const DViewStore, idx: u64) -> DView

items.name: store_set
items.signature: fn store_set(s: *mut DViewStore, idx: u64, v: DView) -> void

items.name: store_insert_at
items.signature: fn store_insert_at(s: *mut DViewStore, idx: u64, v: DView) -> void

items.name: store_remove_at
items.signature: fn store_remove_at(s: *mut DViewStore, idx: u64) -> void

items.name: store_accepts
items.signature: fn store_accepts(s: *const DViewStore, v: DView) -> bool

items.name: cmp_views
items.signature: fn cmp_views(self: *const DView, a: DView, b: DView) -> Ordering

items.name: new
items.doc: Creates an empty `String` with no heap allocation.

items.name: with_capacity
items.doc:
Creates an empty `String` with capacity for `cap` content bytes.
On allocation failure, returns the unallocated `String::new()` state.

items.name: from_cstr
items.doc: Creates a `String` by copying a null-terminated C string.

items.name: from_bytes
items.signature: fn from_bytes(s: *const u8, n: i64) -> String
items.doc: Creates a `String` by copying `n` bytes from `s`.

items.name: from
items.doc: Creates a `String` by copying a `str` slice (Rust-style `String::from("...")`).

items.name: grow
items.signature: fn grow(self: &mut String, needed: i64) -> void
items.doc:
Ensures capacity for `needed` additional content bytes plus the null terminator.
No-op (buffer and length preserved) on `needed < 0`, overflow, or allocation failure.

items.name: push_u8
items.signature: fn push_u8(self: &mut String, c: u8) -> void
items.doc: Appends a single byte.

items.name: push_bytes
items.signature: fn push_bytes(self: &mut String, s: *const u8, n: i64) -> void
items.doc: Appends `n` bytes from `s`; no-op if `n <= 0`.

items.name: push_cstr
items.signature: fn push_cstr(self: &mut String, s: *const u8) -> void
items.doc: Appends a null-terminated C string.

items.name: push_str
items.doc: Appends a `str` slice.

items.name: as_ptr
items.doc: Returns a read-only pointer to the null-terminated bytes; null if never allocated.

items.name: as_str
items.doc: Returns a read-only `str` view of the content bytes.

items.name: len
items.doc: Returns the number of content bytes, excluding the null terminator.

items.name: capacity
items.doc: Returns the allocated capacity in bytes, excluding the null-terminator slot.

items.name: is_empty
items.doc: Returns `true` if the length is zero.

items.name: clear
items.doc: Resets the length to zero, keeping the allocation.

items.name: truncate
items.signature: fn truncate(self: &mut String, new_len: i64) -> void
items.doc:
Truncates the length to `new_len` bytes; no-op if `new_len >= len()`.
Negative values clamp to 0. The allocation is preserved.

items.name: spare_ptr
items.signature: fn spare_ptr(self: &mut String, reserve: i64) -> *mut u8
items.doc:
Ensures `reserve` spare bytes and returns a pointer to the first spare byte.
Write directly into the buffer, then call `advance_len`.

items.name: advance_len
items.signature: fn advance_len(self: &mut String, n: i64) -> void
items.doc:
