Advances the content length by `n` bytes and writes the null terminator.
Call after writing into the buffer returned by `spare_ptr`.

items.name: push_i64
items.signature: fn push_i64(self: &mut String, v: i64) -> void
items.doc: Appends the decimal representation of a signed 64-bit integer.

items.name: push_u64
items.signature: fn push_u64(self: &mut String, v: u64) -> void
items.doc: Appends the decimal representation of an unsigned 64-bit integer.

items.name: eq_string
items.doc: Returns `true` if the content bytes equal `other`'s.

items.name: eq_str
items.doc: Returns `true` if the content equals the `str` slice `s`.

items.name: eq_cstr
items.doc: Returns `true` if the content equals the null-terminated C string `s`.

items.name: push_char
items.signature: fn push_char(self: &mut String, cp: u32) -> void
items.doc:
Encodes the Unicode codepoint `cp` as UTF-8 and appends it.
Invalid codepoints (surrogates, above U+10FFFF) append U+FFFD.

items.name: std_utf8_encode
items.signature: fn std_utf8_encode(cp: u32, buf: *mut u8) -> i64

items.name: as_datum
items.doc: Captures the UTF-8 content bytes (not the `{ptr,len,cap}` struct) as a `W_STRING` view.

items.name: from_datum
items.signature: fn from_datum(self: *const String, d: DView) -> String
items.doc: Materializes an owned `String` by copying the content bytes out of `d`.

items.name: span_unknown
items.doc: Returns the sentinel `Span` for tokens with no source location (`file_id == -1`).

items.name: token_stream_new
items.doc: Creates an empty `TokenStream` with an unknown span.

items.name: push
items.signature: fn push(self: &mut TokenStream, t: TokenTree) -> void
items.doc: Appends a tree to the end of the stream.

items.name: length
items.doc: Returns the number of top-level trees in the stream.

items.name: is_empty
items.doc: Returns `true` if the stream contains no trees.

items.name: sha256_process_block
items.signature: fn sha256_process_block(block: *const u8, h: *mut u32) -> void

items.name: rotr32
items.signature: fn rotr32(x: u32, n: u32) -> u32

items.name: sha256
items.signature: fn sha256(src: *const u8, len: i64, out: &mut String) -> void
items.doc:
Computes SHA-256 of `[src, src+len)`, appending the 32-byte digest (big-endian) to `out`.
`src` must be valid for `len` reads.

items.name: sha256_str
items.doc: Computes SHA-256 of `s`, appending the 32-byte digest (big-endian) to `out`.

items.name: sha256_hex
items.signature: fn sha256_hex(src: *const u8, len: i64, out: &mut String) -> void
items.doc:
Computes SHA-256 of `[src, src+len)`, appending the 64-char lowercase hex digest to `out`.
`src` must be valid for `len` reads.

items.name: sha256_hex_str
items.doc: Computes SHA-256 of `s`, appending the 64-char lowercase hex digest to `out`.

items.name: rotl32
items.signature: fn rotl32(x: u32, n: u32) -> u32

items.name: md5_process_block
items.signature: fn md5_process_block(block: *const u8, h: *mut u32) -> void

items.name: md5
items.signature: fn md5(src: *const u8, len: i64, out: &mut String) -> void
items.doc:
Computes MD5 of `[src, src+len)`, appending the 16-byte digest (RFC 1321 order) to `out`.
`src` must be valid for `len` reads. MD5 is cryptographically broken; legacy formats only.

items.name: md5_str
items.doc: Computes MD5 of `s`, appending the 16-byte digest to `out`.

items.name: md5_hex
items.signature: fn md5_hex(src: *const u8, len: i64, out: &mut String) -> void
items.doc:
Computes MD5 of `[src, src+len)`, appending the 32-char lowercase hex digest to `out`.
`src` must be valid for `len` reads.

items.name: md5_hex_str
items.doc: Computes MD5 of `s`, appending the 32-char lowercase hex digest to `out`.

items.name: arc_new
items.doc: Creates a new `Arc<T>` owning `val`, with a single strong share.

items.name: arc_new_uninit
items.doc:
Allocates an `Arc<T>` control block (strong = weak = 1) with the value uninitialised.
Caller must initialise the value in place (`data_u8`/`DerefMut`) before the `Arc` is
read or dropped.

items.name: arc_from_raw_inner
items.doc:
Reconstructs an owning `Arc<T>` from a raw control-block pointer, taking a new strong share.
`inner` must point at a live `ArcInner<T>`; for unsized `T` the fat pointer carries the
metadata (vtable/len).

items.name: node_arc_data_ptr
items.doc:
Returns the node's base address (where its `NodeBase` header sits).
The data half of the fat `Arc` pointer, reinterpretable as the concrete node type.

items.name: node_arc_from_parts
items.signature: fn node_arc_from_parts(data: *mut u8, vtable: *const u8) -> Arc<dyn BTreeNode>
items.doc:
Reconstructs an owning `NodeARC` from a raw block pointer and its resolved vtable.
Takes a new strong share (rc++); existing handles over the same block stay valid.

items.name: node_arc_same
items.signature: fn node_arc_same(a: &Arc<dyn BTreeNode>, b: &Arc<dyn BTreeNode>) -> bool
items.doc:
Returns whether `a` and `b` point at the same underlying block.
Used by writeback to skip a no-op slot update.

items.name: for_each_block_rec
items.signature: fn for_each_block_rec(arc: &Arc<dyn BTreeNode>, counter: *mut u64) -> void
items.doc:
Visits every `NodeARC` in the tree rooted at `arc` exactly once, post-order.
Accumulates the total count visited (including `arc` itself) into `*counter`.
Doesn't touch refcounts — caller's `NodeARC` keeps the tree alive for the walk.

items.name: arc_release_in_slot
items.doc:
Drops the share held by `*slot` in place (atomic rc--, recursive release/free if last).
Leaves `*slot` a null/moved-from handle so a later auto-drop on it is a no-op.

items.name: arc_replace_in_slot
items.signature: fn arc_replace_in_slot(slot: *mut Arc<dyn BTreeNode>, new_arc: Arc<dyn BTreeNode>) -> void
items.doc:
Replaces the `NodeARC` at `*slot` with `new_arc`, releasing the old share.
The old share is dropped via `Arc`'s own drop (atomic rc-- + recursive release if last).

items.name: make_unique
items.signature: fn make_unique(arc: &mut Arc<dyn BTreeNode>, cur_snapshot_id: <cfg-slot-type>, id_ctr_p: *mut <cfg-slot-type>) -> void
items.doc:
Ensures `*arc` uniquely owns its block for `cur_snapshot_id`, CoW-cloning in place
(via `cow_clone` + a fresh id from `*id_ctr_p`) if the block belongs to an older snapshot.
No-op if the block's `snapshot_id` already matches; caller may then mutate in place.

items.name: shuttle_sum_u64
items.signature: fn shuttle_sum_u64(slots: *const PrimVec<u64>, target: u64) -> ShuttleResult
items.doc:
Finds the child whose prefix-sum range brackets `target` in a SUM-dimension branch.
Assumes `target` is in-range; caller must check `len == 0` and the total beforehand.
Out-of-range `target` clamps to the last child with full remainder.

items.name: shuttle_max_u64
items.signature: fn shuttle_max_u64(slots: *const PrimVec<u64>, target: u64) -> ShuttleResult
items.doc:
Finds the first child whose max-key slot is `>= target` in a MAX-dimension branch.
If none qualifies, `target` is past the last subtree — clamps to the last child.

items.name: shuttle_max_k
items.signature: fn shuttle_max_k(slots: *const Buffer<K>, target: K::ViewInStore) -> ShuttleResult
items.doc:
Generic MAX-dimension shuttle over `K`: returns the smallest `i` with `slots[i] >= target`.
`slots` is the `keys` column of a `BranchNode` (i-th slot = max key in `children[i]`'s
subtree); comparison goes through `cmp_views`. Out-of-range `target` clamps to the last
child; `remainder` is always 0 (kept only for ABI parity with the SUM/MAX u64 shuttles).

items.name: allocator_new
items.doc:
Creates an allocator with an initial segment of `seg_size` data bytes.
On OOM the returned allocator has a null `head` (`allocator_alloc` then returns null).

items.name: allocator_append
items.signature: fn allocator_append(a: *mut Allocator, min_cap: i64) -> *mut Segment

items.name: allocator_alloc
items.signature: fn allocator_alloc(a: *mut Allocator, size: i64, align: i64) -> *mut u8
items.doc:
Bump-allocates `size` bytes at `align` from the allocator, appending a new
segment if the current one is full. `align` must be a power of two; the
allocator floors it to 2 (a Ref'd object's low bit must be 0). Returns a
pointer into a never-moved segment (stable for the allocator's lifetime), or
null on OOM.

items.name: allocator_free
items.doc:
Frees all segments en masse. The allocator must not be used afterwards.
Idempotent: nulls `head`/`current`, so a second call (or the `Drop` impl after
an explicit free) walks a null list and is a no-op — no double-free.

items.name: drop
items.doc: Frees all segments en masse; a no-op if `allocator_free` already ran (idempotent).

items.name: writ_new
items.doc:
Creates a container whose segments default to `seg_size` data bytes.

Safe: the returned container is a sound owned root, RAII-freed on drop.

items.name: set_root
items.doc: Sets the document root to `v` (interior-mutable: `&self`; stores the raw word).

items.name: root
items.doc: Returns the document root `WAny` reconstructed from the stored word.

items.name: writ_free
items.doc: Frees the container and all its segments en masse; `h` must not be used afterwards.

items.name: writ_alloc
items.doc:
Places one uninitialised `T` into a never-move segment and returns a pointer to it.

The caller initialises the storage. The pointer is stable for the container's
lifetime. Returns null on OOM.

items.name: writ_alloc_n
items.signature: fn writ_alloc_n(h: *mut Writ, n: i64) -> *mut T
items.doc:
Places `n` contiguous uninitialised `T` (array backing) in one segment.

Returns a pointer to element 0, stable for the container's lifetime. Null on OOM.

items.name: writ_alloc_bytes
items.signature: fn writ_alloc_bytes(h: *mut Writ, n: i64) -> *mut u8
items.doc:
Places `n` raw uninitialised bytes (8-aligned) and returns a pointer to them.

For self-describing blobs — Varchar payloads, etc. Stable for the container's
lifetime. Null on OOM.

items.name: w_write_tag
items.signature: fn w_write_tag(obj: *mut u8, code: u64) -> void
items.doc:
Writes the in-band varint type tag `code` into the bytes immediately before `obj`.

Codes 1..=222 take one byte at `obj[-1]`; larger codes take a header byte at `obj[-1]`
plus little-endian code bytes below it. `code == 0` writes nothing.

items.name: alloc_tagged
items.signature: fn alloc_tagged(a: *mut Allocator, size: i64, code: u64) -> *mut u8

items.name: box_i64
items.signature: fn box_i64(a: *mut Allocator, v: i64) -> WAny
items.doc: Boxes `v` into its own `W_I64`-tagged 8-byte arena object; returns a Ref `WAny` to it.

items.name: box_f64
items.signature: fn box_f64(a: *mut Allocator, v: f64) -> WAny
items.doc: Boxes `v` into its own `W_F64`-tagged 8-byte arena object; returns a Ref `WAny` to it.

items.name: box_u64
items.signature: fn box_u64(a: *mut Allocator, v: u64) -> WAny
items.doc:
Boxes `v` into its own `W_U64`-tagged 8-byte arena object; returns a Ref `WAny` to it.

`u64` is ALWAYS boxed (no inline unsigned-56 tag — keeps `is_u64`/`as_u64` exact).

items.name: box_f32
items.signature: fn box_f32(a: *mut Allocator, v: f32) -> WAny
items.doc:
Boxes `v` into a `W_F32`-tagged 8-byte arena slot (4 used); returns a Ref `WAny` to it.

`f32` never fits the inline integer Pod, so it is always boxed.

items.name: make_int
items.signature: fn make_int(a: *mut Allocator, v: i64) -> WAny
items.doc: Makes an integer `WAny`: inline Pod when `v` fits i56, else boxed via `box_i64`.

items.name: int
items.doc: Makes an integer `WAny` in this arena: inline Pod when `v` fits i56, else boxed.

items.name: float
items.doc: Boxes `v` into a `W_F64`-tagged object in this arena; returns a Ref `WAny` to it.

items.name: put
items.signature: fn put(self: &mut Writ, v: T) -> Slot<T>
items.doc: Places `v` into a never-move segment and returns an opaque `Slot` handle to it.

items.name: get
items.signature: fn get(self: &'a Writ, s: Slot<T>) -> &'a T
items.doc: Borrows the slot's value; the `&'a T` is tied to `&'a self`, shared views may coexist.

items.name: get_mut
items.signature: fn get_mut(self: &'a mut Writ, s: Slot<T>) -> &'a mut T
items.doc: Exclusively borrows the slot's value; excludes every other view of the container.

items.name: writ_rc
items.doc: Creates a refcounted (held) container; when the last holder drops, all segments free.

items.name: hold
items.doc: Pairs `r` with a clone of the container's residency holder — the escape form of a reference.

items.name: hold_any
items.signature: fn hold_any(h: &mut Rc<Writ>, v: WAny) -> HeldAny
items.doc: Pairs `v` with a clone of the container's residency holder — the escape form of a `WAny`.

items.name: val
items.doc: Returns the held value; valid while this `HeldAny` (hence its Writ holder) lives.

items.name: resolve
items.doc: Returns the held value's raw word as an absolute pointer (tagged object for a Ref).

items.name: wstring_in_alloc
items.doc:
Interns `s` through a raw `Allocator`: allocates `vlen_encode_size(n) + n` bytes in
one arena chunk, writes the vlen length prefix, then copies the payload after it.
Taking `*mut Allocator` (not the whole `Writ`) lets a container that holds only its
allocator back-pointer — e.g. `WArray<WAny>` — intern strings without the full handle.

items.name: wstring
items.doc:
Interns `s` into the arena; returns a borrowed `&WString` into never-move storage,
valid for the container's lifetime. Read it back with `as_str`.

items.name: wstring_of
items.doc:
Resolves a Ref `WAny` back to the `WString` it points at.
`e` must be a Ref to an interned string — no type check is performed.

items.name: len
items.doc:
Returns the logical string length in bytes — the vlen-decoded payload count.
NOT `dst_len`, which is the whole tail including the vlen prefix (`self.bytes`
materializes the tail; its data pointer is the vlen prefix).

items.name: as_str
items.doc:
Returns the inline UTF-8 payload — the tail past its vlen prefix.
The `str` borrows arena storage and stays valid while the container lives.

items.name: from
items.doc: Wraps an interned string as a heterogeneous `WAny` — a Ref to its header.

items.name: as_string
items.doc:
Resolves a Ref `WAny` to a borrowed `&WString` (read-only); check `is_string()` first.
Encapsulates the `resolve()`+cast so callers stay `unsafe`-free.

items.name: as_wstr
items.doc:
Returns a null-safe `str` view of a `WAny` holding an interned `WString`.
Null, inline Pod, and non-string Ref values (guard: `!is_string()`) all yield
the empty `str` — the same default as an absent schema key.

items.name: array_new
items.signature: fn array_new(h: *mut Writ, cap: i64) -> *mut WArray<WAny>

items.name: array_push
items.signature: fn array_push(a: *mut WArray<WAny>, alloc: *mut Allocator, v: WAny) -> void

items.name: array_get
items.signature: fn array_get(a: *const WArray<WAny>, i: i64) -> WAny

items.name: array
items.signature: fn array(self: &'a Writ, cap: i64) -> &'a mut WArray<WAny>
items.doc:
Creates an empty heterogeneous array in this container; returns a fresh fat `&mut` to it.
Interior-mut `&self` allocation into never-move storage: multiple `array` calls coexist.

items.name: array_of
items.signature: fn array_of(self: &'a Writ, e: WAny) -> &'a WArray<WAny>
items.doc:
Resolves a Ref element back to the array it points at, tied to this container.
The returned `&WArray<WAny>` may not outlive the container.

items.name: push
items.signature: fn push(self: &mut WArray<WAny>, v: WAny) -> void
items.doc: Appends a `WAny` element, growing the buffer 2× via the zone carried by the fat `&mut`.

items.name: push
items.signature: fn push(self: &mut WArray<WAny>, v: i64) -> void
items.doc: Appends an `i64` — inline Pod when it fits i56, else boxed (`W_I64`) into the arena.

items.name: push
items.signature: fn push(self: &mut WArray<WAny>, v: u64) -> void
items.doc: Appends a `u64` — always boxed (`W_U64`); there is no inline unsigned Pod form.

items.name: push
items.signature: fn push(self: &mut WArray<WAny>, v: f32) -> void
items.doc: Appends an `f32` — boxed (`W_F32`) into the arena.

items.name: push
items.signature: fn push(self: &mut WArray<WAny>, v: f64) -> void
items.doc: Appends an `f64` — boxed (`W_F64`) into the arena; never fits the inline Pod.

items.name: push
items.signature: fn push(self: &mut WArray<WAny>, v: bool) -> void
items.doc: Appends a `bool` as an inline Pod.

items.name: push
items.signature: fn push(self: &mut WArray<WAny>, v: i8) -> void
items.doc: Appends an `i8` as an exact-width inline Pod (no boxing).

items.name: push
items.signature: fn push(self: &mut WArray<WAny>, v: u8) -> void
items.doc: Appends a `u8` as an exact-width inline Pod (no boxing).

items.name: push
items.signature: fn push(self: &mut WArray<WAny>, v: i16) -> void
items.doc: Appends an `i16` as an exact-width inline Pod (no boxing).

items.name: push
items.signature: fn push(self: &mut WArray<WAny>, v: u16) -> void
items.doc: Appends a `u16` as an exact-width inline Pod (no boxing).

items.name: push
items.signature: fn push(self: &mut WArray<WAny>, v: i24) -> void
items.doc: Appends an `i24` as an exact-width inline Pod (no boxing).

items.name: push
items.signature: fn push(self: &mut WArray<WAny>, v: u24) -> void
items.doc: Appends a `u24` as an exact-width inline Pod (no boxing).

items.name: push
items.doc: Appends a Ref to an already-interned arena `WString` (no copy).

items.name: push
items.doc: Appends a Ref to an arena-resident nested array (no copy).

items.name: push
items.doc:
Appends a `str` by interning its bytes into this array's zone as a `WString`, pushing a Ref.
The interned copy lives as long as the container.

items.name: get
items.doc: Returns element `i` by value. No bounds check — `i` must be less than `len()`.

items.name: len
items.doc: Returns the number of elements.

items.name: get_mut
items.signature: fn get_mut(self: &mut WArray<WAny>, i: i64) -> WAnyMut
items.doc:
Returns a mutable cursor onto element `i`: the element slot plus this array's zone,
so a nested child can grow in the same arena and a Pod reads/replaces in place.
No bounds check.

items.name: as_array_mut
items.doc:
Resolves a Ref cursor to a fat `&mut WArray<WAny>` carrying its zone.
Ready to push/grow in the same arena; check `is_array()` first.

items.name: from
items.doc: Wraps a nested array as a heterogeneous value: a Ref to the child's header (no copy).

items.name: as_array
items.doc:
Resolves a Ref to a borrowed read-only `&WArray<WAny>`; check `is_array()` first.
Encapsulates the `resolve()`+cast so callers stay safe.

items.name: warr_new
items.signature: fn warr_new(h: *mut Writ, cap: i64) -> *mut WArray<T>

items.name: warr_push
items.signature: fn warr_push(a: *mut WArray<T>, alloc: *mut Allocator, v: T) -> void

items.name: tarray
items.signature: fn tarray(self: &'a Writ, cap: i64) -> &'a mut WArray<T>
items.doc:
Creates an empty typed array; returns a fresh fat `&mut` carrying this container's zone.
Interior-mut `&self` allocation, like `array`.

items.name: om_zero
items.signature: fn om_zero(buf: *mut WMapEntry, cap: i64) -> void

items.name: om_new
items.signature: fn om_new(h: *mut Writ, cap: i64) -> *mut WMap<WString, WAny>

items.name: om_grow
items.signature: fn om_grow(m: *mut WMap<WString, WAny>, alloc: *mut Allocator) -> void

items.name: om_set
items.signature: fn om_set(m: *mut WMap<WString, WAny>, alloc: *mut Allocator, key: &[u8], val: WAny) -> void

items.name: om_get
items.signature: fn om_get(m: *const WMap<WString, WAny>, key: &[u8]) -> WAny

items.name: om_get_slot
items.signature: fn om_get_slot(m: *mut WMap<WString, WAny>, key: &[u8]) -> *zoned mut WAny

items.name: map
items.signature: fn map(self: &'a Writ, cap: i64) -> &'a mut WMap<WString, WAny>
items.doc:
Creates an empty string-keyed map with at least `cap` slots (power-of-two rounded, min 8).
`&self` interior-mut allocation into never-move storage; the fat `&mut` carries the zone.

items.name: set
items.signature: fn set(self: &mut WMap<WString, WAny>, key: &[u8], val: WAny) -> void
items.doc:
Inserts or updates `key → val`; a new key's `str` is interned into this map's arena.
Grows (2×, rehash) when load would exceed 0.75.

items.name: get
items.doc: Returns the value at `key`, or a null `WAny` if absent.

items.name: contains
items.doc: Returns `true` if `key` is present.

items.name: len
items.doc: Returns the number of entries.

items.name: get_mut
items.signature: fn get_mut(self: &mut WMap<WString, WAny>, key: &[u8]) -> WAnyMut
items.doc:
Returns a mutable cursor onto the value at `key`, carrying this map's zone.
The cursor's slot is null when `key` is absent — callers must ensure the key exists.

items.name: slots
items.doc: Returns the slot count (`cap`) for raw slot iteration over `0..slots()`.

items.name: key_at
items.signature: fn key_at(self: &WMap<WString, WAny>, i: i64) -> WAny
items.doc: Returns the key at slot `i` (interned `WString` Ref); null `WAny` if the slot is empty.

items.name: val_at
items.signature: fn val_at(self: &WMap<WString, WAny>, i: i64) -> WAny
items.doc: Returns the value at slot `i`; null `WAny` if the slot is empty.

items.name: set
items.signature: fn set(self: &mut WMap<WString, WAny>, key: &[u8], v: i64) -> void
items.doc: Inserts or updates `key` with an integer value (inline i56 Pod, boxed when wider).

items.name: set
items.signature: fn set(self: &mut WMap<WString, WAny>, key: &[u8], v: f64) -> void
items.doc: Inserts or updates `key` with an `f64` value (boxed into this map's arena).

items.name: set
items.signature: fn set(self: &mut WMap<WString, WAny>, key: &[u8], v: bool) -> void
items.doc: Inserts or updates `key` with a `bool` value.

items.name: set
items.signature: fn set(self: &mut WMap<WString, WAny>, key: &[u8], v: &WString) -> void
items.doc: Inserts or updates `key` with a Ref to an existing `WString`.

items.name: set
items.signature: fn set(self: &mut WMap<WString, WAny>, key: &[u8], v: &WArray<WAny>) -> void
items.doc: Inserts or updates `key` with a Ref to a nested `WArray<WAny>`.

items.name: set
items.signature: fn set(self: &mut WMap<WString, WAny>, key: &[u8], v: &WMap<WString, WAny>) -> void
items.doc: Inserts or updates `key` with a Ref to a nested map.

items.name: set
items.signature: fn set(self: &mut WMap<WString, WAny>, key: &[u8], v: &[u8]) -> void
items.doc: Inserts or updates `key` with a `str` value, interning it into this map's arena.

items.name: as_map_mut
items.doc:
Resolves this cursor to a fat `&mut WMap<WString, WAny>` carrying its zone.
Check `is_map()` first; the child can then set/grow in the same arena.

items.name: from
items.doc: Wraps a map as a heterogeneous `WAny` value (a Ref to its header).

items.name: as_map
items.doc:
Resolves a Ref `WAny` to a borrowed read-only `&WMap<WString, WAny>`.
Check `is_map()` first; encapsulates the resolve()+cast so callers stay unsafe-free.

items.name: wtv_new
items.signature: fn wtv_new(h: *mut Writ, cap: i64) -> *mut WMap<Wu6, WAny>

items.name: size
items.doc: Returns the number of present entries.

items.name: cap
items.doc: Returns the fixed slot capacity (max 52).

items.name: schema_type_code
items.doc: Returns the node-class discriminator (`0` = none); byte-shared with the C++ TinyObjectMap.

items.name: set_schema_type_code
items.signature: fn set_schema_type_code(self: &mut WMap<Wu6, WAny>, code: u64) -> void
items.doc: Sets the node-class discriminator.

items.name: contains
items.signature: fn contains(self: &WMap<Wu6, WAny>, key: u8) -> bool
items.doc: Returns `true` if `key` is present.

items.name: get
items.signature: fn get(self: &WMap<Wu6, WAny>, key: u8) -> WAny
items.doc: Returns the value at `key`, or a null `WAny` if absent or `key >= 52`.

items.name: set
items.signature: fn set(self: &mut WMap<Wu6, WAny>, key: u8, val: WAny) -> void
items.doc:
Inserts or updates `key → val`; `key >= 52` is a silent no-op.
Panics when a NEW key would exceed the fixed capacity (over-capacity insert = bounds error).

items.name: tinymap
items.signature: fn tinymap(self: &'a Writ, cap: i64) -> &'a mut WMap<Wu6, WAny>
items.doc:
Creates an empty bitmap-indexed tiny map; thin `&mut` (fixed capacity → no carried zone).
`cap` is clamped to `0..=52`.

items.name: make_schema
items.signature: fn make_schema(self: &Writ, cap: i64, code: u64) -> *const WMap<Wu6, WAny>
items.doc: Allocates a schema-stamped TOM; returns the raw view pointer (ADR 0011).

items.name: make_schema_h
items.signature: fn make_schema_h(self: &Writ, cap: i64, code: u64) -> WSchemaH
items.doc:
Allocates a schema-stamped TOM as a `WSchemaH` carrying the arena allocator.
`make::<S>()` retypes the result to the typed view `S` (identical layout).

items.name: to_wany
items.signature: fn to_wany(self: bool, z: *mut Allocator) -> WAny

items.name: to_wany
items.signature: fn to_wany(self: i64, z: *mut Allocator) -> WAny

items.name: to_wany
items.signature: fn to_wany(self: WAny, z: *mut Allocator) -> WAny

items.name: to_wany
items.signature: fn to_wany(self: i8, z: *mut Allocator) -> WAny

items.name: to_wany
items.signature: fn to_wany(self: u8, z: *mut Allocator) -> WAny

items.name: to_wany
items.signature: fn to_wany(self: i16, z: *mut Allocator) -> WAny

items.name: to_wany
items.signature: fn to_wany(self: u16, z: *mut Allocator) -> WAny

items.name: to_wany
items.signature: fn to_wany(self: i24, z: *mut Allocator) -> WAny

items.name: to_wany
items.signature: fn to_wany(self: u24, z: *mut Allocator) -> WAny

items.name: to_wany
items.signature: fn to_wany(self: i56, z: *mut Allocator) -> WAny

items.name: to_wany
items.signature: fn to_wany(self: i32, z: *mut Allocator) -> WAny

items.name: to_wany
items.signature: fn to_wany(self: isize, z: *mut Allocator) -> WAny

items.name: to_wany
items.signature: fn to_wany(self: u32, z: *mut Allocator) -> WAny

items.name: to_wany
items.signature: fn to_wany(self: u56, z: *mut Allocator) -> WAny

items.name: to_wany
items.signature: fn to_wany(self: u64, z: *mut Allocator) -> WAny

items.name: to_wany
items.signature: fn to_wany(self: usize, z: *mut Allocator) -> WAny

items.name: to_wany
items.signature: fn to_wany(self: f32, z: *mut Allocator) -> WAny

items.name: to_wany
items.signature: fn to_wany(self: f64, z: *mut Allocator) -> WAny

items.name: from
items.doc: Wraps a tiny map as a heterogeneous `WAny` value (a Ref to its header).

items.name: wim_new
items.signature: fn wim_new(h: *mut Writ, cap: i64) -> *mut WMap<K, WAny>

items.name: intmap
items.signature: fn intmap(self: &'a Writ, cap: i64) -> &'a mut WMap<K, WAny>
items.doc: Creates an empty dense int-keyed map with fixed `cap`; thin `&mut` (no carried zone).

items.name: ok
items.doc: Returns `Ok(())`, the success value of a formatter operation.

items.name: err
items.doc: Returns `Err(Error {})`, signalling formatter failure.

items.name: write_char
items.signature: fn write_char(self: &mut String, c: u8) -> Result

items.name: formatter_for
items.doc: Creates a default-spec `Formatter` writing into `buf`.

items.name: formatter_for_sink
items.doc: Creates a default-spec `Formatter` streaming into an arbitrary `FmtWrite` sink.

items.name: new
items.doc: Creates a default-spec `Formatter` over `buf`; static-call twin of `formatter_for`.

items.name: reset_spec
items.doc: Resets spec fields to no-format defaults; `indent` (nesting state) is preserved.

items.name: write_indent
items.doc: Writes a newline followed by `indent * 4` spaces.

items.name: indent_inc
items.doc: Increments the pretty-print nesting depth.

items.name: indent_dec
items.doc: Decrements the pretty-print nesting depth.

items.name: write_char
items.signature: fn write_char(self: &mut Formatter, c: u8) -> Result

items.name: pad
items.signature: fn pad(self: &mut Formatter, content: &[u8], prefix: &[u8]) -> Result
items.doc:
Writes `prefix` then `content` applying the full spec: width, fill, alignment,
precision, `sign_plus`, and zero padding; precision truncates `content` bytewise.
Zero padding inserts `0`s between sign/prefix and content (`{:#05x}` → `0x0ff`).

items.name: write_int_dec_i64
items.signature: fn write_int_dec_i64(self: &mut Formatter, n: i64) -> Result
items.doc: Writes `n` in signed decimal through `pad` (spec applies).

items.name: write_int_dec_u64
items.signature: fn write_int_dec_u64(self: &mut Formatter, n: u64) -> Result
items.doc: Writes `n` in unsigned decimal through `pad` (spec applies).

items.name: write_bool
items.signature: fn write_bool(self: &mut Formatter, b: bool) -> Result
items.doc: Writes `"true"` or `"false"` through `pad` (spec applies).

items.name: write_int_radix_u64
items.signature: fn write_int_radix_u64(self: &mut Formatter, n: u64, radix: u64, upper: bool) -> Result
items.doc:
Writes `n` in the given radix (2/8/16) through `pad`; `upper` selects `A-F` digits.
Signless — callers mask signed values first. Alternate (`#`) adds `0x`/`0o`/`0b`.

items.name: fmt_display_to_string
items.doc: Renders `val` via `Display` into a new `String` using a default-spec `Formatter`.

items.name: fmt_debug_to_string
items.doc: Renders `val` via `Debug` into a new `String` using a default-spec `Formatter`.

items.name: fmt_display
items.doc: Dispatches `arg.fmt(f)` through the `Display` bound (resolves the shared `fmt` name).

items.name: fmt_debug
items.doc: Dispatches `arg.fmt(f)` through the `Debug` bound (resolves the shared `fmt` name).

items.name: fmt_lower_hex
items.doc: Dispatches `arg.fmt(f)` through the `LowerHex` bound (resolves the shared `fmt` name).

items.name: fmt_upper_hex
items.doc: Dispatches `arg.fmt(f)` through the `UpperHex` bound (resolves the shared `fmt` name).

items.name: fmt_octal
items.doc: Dispatches `arg.fmt(f)` through the `Octal` bound (resolves the shared `fmt` name).

items.name: fmt_binary
items.doc: Dispatches `arg.fmt(f)` through the `Binary` bound (resolves the shared `fmt` name).

items.name: fmt_lower_exp
items.doc: Dispatches `arg.fmt(f)` through the `LowerExp` bound (resolves the shared `fmt` name).

items.name: fmt_upper_exp
items.doc: Dispatches `arg.fmt(f)` through the `UpperExp` bound (resolves the shared `fmt` name).

items.name: fmt_seq
items.signature: fn fmt_seq(a: Result, b: Result) -> Result
items.doc: Returns `a` if it is `Err`, else `b`; short-circuit step in mono-expanded tuple `Debug` chains.

items.name: fmt_tuple_open
items.doc: Writes the tuple-`Debug` opener `(`.

items.name: fmt_tuple_sep
items.doc: Writes the tuple-`Debug` separator `, `.

items.name: fmt_tuple_close
items.doc: Writes the tuple-`Debug` closer `)`.

items.name: fmt_tuple_close1
items.doc: Writes the 1-tuple closer `,)`.

items.name: fmt
items.doc: Writes `self` in `"` quotes, escaping `"`, `\`, and control chars (Rust `escape_debug`).

items.name: fmt
items.doc: Renders the pointed-to NUL-terminated C string; walks memory unchecked to the terminator.

items.name: fmt
items.doc: Writes the content in `"` quotes, escaping `"`, `\`, and control chars (Rust `escape_debug`).

items.name: debug_struct
items.doc:
Creates a struct-shape `Debug` builder, writing `name` immediately.
Braces appear only once `.field()` is called (unit structs render as bare `name`).

items.name: debug_tuple
items.doc: Creates a tuple-shape `Debug` builder, writing `name` immediately.

items.name: debug_list
items.doc: Creates a list-shape `Debug` builder, writing the opening `[` immediately.

items.name: debug_set
items.doc: Creates a set-shape `Debug` builder, writing the opening `{` immediately.

items.name: debug_map
items.doc: Creates a map-shape `Debug` builder, writing the opening `{` immediately.

items.name: field
items.signature: fn field(self: &mut DebugStruct, name: &[u8], value: &T) -> &mut DebugStruct
items.doc: Appends one `name: value` field rendered via `value`'s `Debug`.

items.name: finish
items.doc: Closes the struct body (only if fields were written) and returns the running result.

items.name: finish_non_exhaustive
items.doc: Closes the body with a trailing `..` marker (`Foo { a: 1, .. }` / `Foo { .. }`).

items.name: field
items.doc: Appends one tuple field rendered via `value`'s `Debug`.

items.name: finish
items.doc: Closes with `)` (only if fields were written) and returns the running result.

items.name: entry
items.doc: Appends one entry rendered via `value`'s `Debug`.

items.name: finish
items.doc: Closes with `]` and returns the running result.

items.name: entry
items.doc: Appends one entry rendered via `value`'s `Debug`.

items.name: finish
items.doc: Closes with `}` and returns the running result.

items.name: entry
items.signature: fn entry(self: &mut DebugMap, k: &K, v: &V) -> &mut DebugMap
items.doc: Appends one `k: v` entry, rendering key and value via their `Debug` impls.

items.name: finish
items.doc: Closes with `}` and returns the running result.

items.name: is_void
items.doc: Returns `true` if the kind is `Void`.

items.name: is_bool
items.doc: Returns `true` if the kind is `Bool`.

items.name: is_integer
items.doc:
Returns `true` for fixed-width integer kinds (i8..i128, u8..u128, incl. 24/56-bit).

`usize`/`isize`/`char` are separate kinds and NOT included.

items.name: is_float
items.doc: Returns `true` if the kind is `F32` or `F64`.

items.name: is_primitive
items.doc:
Returns `true` for bool, float, and fixed-width integer kinds (kinds 1..=17).

Excludes `Void`, `usize`/`isize`, `char`, and all compound kinds.

items.name: is_struct
items.doc: Returns `true` if the kind is `Struct` or `ZonedStruct`.

items.name: is_enum
items.doc: Returns `true` if the kind is `Enum`.

items.name: is_array
items.doc: Returns `true` if the kind is `Array` (`[T; N]`).

items.name: is_pointer
items.doc: Returns `true` if the kind is `Ptr`, `Ref`, or `MutRef`.

items.name: ident
items.doc: Returns the type's name as a borrowed `Ident`.

items.name: template_of_at
items.doc:
Creates a `Template` from a module-AST arena byte offset.

Compiler-intrinsic target: `template_of::<X>()` lowers to a call of this with
the resolved item's arena offset (the hook-side `OView` supplies the base).

items.name: name
items.doc: Returns the declaration's name (NAME key) as borrowed identifier bytes.

items.name: type_param_count
items.doc: Returns the number of generic type parameters (0 if none).

items.name: type_param_name
items.signature: fn type_param_name(self: &Template, view: &OView, idx: u64) -> Ident
items.doc: Returns the name of the `idx`-th generic type parameter.

items.name: body
items.doc: Returns the same underlying node viewed as an `Item` (for field/variant inspection).

items.name: gensym
items.doc:
Returns a fresh hygienic identifier `<prefix>__hyg_<N>`, unique across the whole compile.

The bytes are host-owned and stay valid for the rest of the compilation.

items.name: name
items.doc: Returns the item's name (NAME key) as borrowed identifier bytes.

items.name: field_count
items.doc: Returns the number of struct fields (0 if the node has no FIELDS array).

items.name: field
items.signature: fn field(self: &Item, view: &OView, idx: u64) -> Item
items.doc: Returns the `idx`-th field-def node wrapped as an `Item`.

items.name: eq
items.doc: Returns `true` if the two identifiers have identical byte content.

items.name: type_from_anyval
items.doc:
Decodes a `<type:T>` node handle (`WAny` Ref to a schema-107 TOM) into a `Type`.

Returns a zero-filled `Type` if `val` is not a Ref to a schema-107 TinyObjectMap.
Only `kind` and `uid` are decoded; `name`/`size`/`align` stay empty/zero.

items.name: ast_node_name
items.doc: Returns the node's NAME (key 1) as a string view.

items.name: ast_node_code
items.doc:
Returns the node's AST code (`la::Code` — e.g. ARR_TYPE=64, SLICE_TYPE=111).

Read from the TOM's `schema_type_code`; 0 for a null/non-ref node.

items.name: ast_type_child
items.doc:
Returns the TYPE (key 3) child of a field-def / type node.

The element type of an array/slice, or the pointee of a ref; null `WAny` if absent.

items.name: ast_struct_field_count
items.doc: Returns the length of the node's FIELDS (key 22) array; 0 if absent.

items.name: ast_struct_field
items.signature: fn ast_struct_field(self: &OView, node: WAny, idx: u64) -> WAny
items.doc: Returns the `idx`-th field-def node from the node's FIELDS (key 22) array.

items.name: ast_field_name
items.doc: Returns a field-def node's NAME as a string view.

items.name: ast_field_type_name
items.doc: Returns the NAME of a field-def's TYPE child; empty `SView` if the field has no TYPE.

items.name: ast_type_param_count
items.doc: Returns the node's generic type-parameter count (TYPE_PARAMS key 27); 0 if absent.

items.name: ast_type_param_name
items.signature: fn ast_type_param_name(self: &OView, node: WAny, idx: u64) -> SView
items.doc: Returns the `idx`-th generic type-parameter name; empty `SView` if the list is absent.

items.name: ast_enum_variant_count
items.doc: Returns an enum node's variant count; 0 if the variant list is absent.

items.name: ast_enum_variant
items.signature: fn ast_enum_variant(self: &OView, node: WAny, idx: u64) -> WAny
items.doc: Returns the `idx`-th variant node of an enum node.

items.name: ast_variant_arity
items.doc:
Returns a variant node's payload arity.

0 for a unit variant; 1 for a single unwrapped payload; else the payload-list length.

items.name: find_template_decl
items.doc: Returns the first MODULE item whose NAME matches `name` byte-for-byte; null `WAny` if none.

items.name: ast_lit_str_view
items.doc: Returns a STRING-token literal's byte view minus the surrounding quotes; empty if absent.

items.name: as_type
items.doc: Decodes a `<type:T>` Writ value (schema-107 TOM) into a `Type` via `type_from_anyval`.

items.name: module_package_append
items.doc: Appends the user-root MODULE's dotted package name (e.g. `a.b.c`) to `buf`.

items.name: find_my_annotation
items.signature: fn find_my_annotation(view: &OView, target_offset: u32, trigger: &[u8]) -> WAny

items.name: annot_kv_value
items.signature: fn annot_kv_value(view: &OView, ann: WAny, key: &[u8]) -> WAny

items.name: writ_map_get
items.signature: fn writ_map_get(view: &OView, wmap: WAny, key: &[u8]) -> WAny

items.name: error
items.doc:
Reports `msg` as a metaprog-hook error; the host fails compilation after the hook returns.
A hook may report several errors per invocation; all surface together.

items.name: error_at
items.doc:
Reports `msg` like `error`, attributed to the user-AST node at `target_offset`.
The host prefixes the message with `<file>:<line>:` from the node's `SRC_LINE` field.

items.name: new
items.doc: Creates an `Emitter` with an empty buffer.

items.name: emit_raw
items.doc: Appends `src` to the buffer followed by a newline.

items.name: emit_into
items.doc: Begins a new chunk by writing `package <pkg>; ` to the buffer.

items.name: emit_target_pkg
items.doc: Begins a new chunk in the user's own package, read from `view`'s root MODULE node.

items.name: commit
items.doc: Flushes buffered source to the host compiler and clears the buffer; no-op when empty.

items.name: push_text
items.doc: Appends `s` to the buffer without a trailing newline (unlike `emit_raw`).

items.name: push_view
items.signature: fn push_view(self: &mut Emitter, v: SView) -> void
items.doc: Appends the bytes of `v` verbatim.

items.name: push_num
items.signature: fn push_num(self: &mut Emitter, n: u64) -> void
items.doc: Appends `n` in decimal.

items.name: tpl1
items.signature: fn tpl1(self: &mut Emitter, tmpl: &[u8], k1: &[u8], v1: SView) -> void
items.doc:
Renders `tmpl` into the buffer, replacing `$k1` with the bytes of `v1`.
`$$` emits a literal `$`; an unmatched `$ident` passes through unchanged.

items.name: tpl2
items.signature: fn tpl2(self: &mut Emitter, tmpl: &[u8], k1: &[u8], v1: SView, k2: &[u8], v2: SView) -> void
items.doc: Renders `tmpl` into the buffer, substituting two `$key` bindings; same rules as `tpl1`.

items.name: tpl3
items.signature: fn tpl3(self: &mut Emitter, tmpl: &[u8], k1: &[u8], v1: SView, k2: &[u8], v2: SView, k3: &[u8], v3: SView) -> void
items.doc: Renders `tmpl` into the buffer, substituting three `$key` bindings; same rules as `tpl1`.

items.name: view_of_str
items.doc: Wraps a `str` as an `SView`, e.g. to bind a literal as a `tpl*` value.

items.name: view_of_string
items.doc:
Wraps a `String`'s buffer as an `SView`.
Borrows: the view is valid only while the source `String` is alive and unmodified.

items.name: bytes_eq
items.signature: fn bytes_eq(a: *const u8, b: *const u8, n: i64) -> bool

items.name: match_key
items.signature: fn match_key(p: *const u8, n: i64, k: &[u8], v: SView, out_buf: &mut String) -> bool

items.name: tpl_render_into
items.signature: fn tpl_render_into(out: &mut String, tmpl: &[u8], k1: &[u8], v1: SView, k2: &[u8], v2: SView, k3: &[u8], v3: SView) -> void

items.name: item_list_new
items.doc: Creates an empty `ItemList` with no blobs.

items.name: kind_name
items.doc: Returns a short printable name for a `Type` kind tag; unknown tags yield `"?"`.

items.name: name_eq
items.doc: Returns `true` iff byte slices `a` and `b` have equal length and contents.

items.name: length
items.doc: Returns the pack length (compile-time constant; folds to a `lit_int`).

items.name: head
items.doc:
Returns the first pack element.
Calling on an empty pack hits the array out-of-range path of the B-side intrinsic.

items.name: transform
items.doc: Applies `f` to each pack member and returns the mapped `[Type; sizeof...(T)]` array.

items.name: filter
items.signature: fn filter(pred: fn(Type) -> bool, out: *mut Type) -> i64
items.doc:
Writes pack members satisfying `pred` front-packed into `out`; returns the accepted count.
`out` must have room for `sizeof...(T)` elements; slots past the returned count are untouched.

items.name: fold_to_type
items.signature: fn fold_to_type(init: Type, f: fn(Type, Type) -> Type) -> Type
items.doc: Left-folds `f` over the pack's `Type`s starting from `init`.

items.name: is_void
items.doc: Returns `true` iff `t` is the `Void` kind.

items.name: is_bool_kind
items.doc: Returns `true` iff `t` is the `bool` kind.

items.name: is_integer
items.doc: Returns `true` iff `t` is any integer kind (signed/unsigned, 8–128 bits incl. 24/56).

items.name: is_float_kind
items.doc: Returns `true` iff `t` is `f32` or `f64`.

items.name: is_primitive_kind
items.doc: Returns `true` iff `t` is a primitive scalar kind (integer, float, or `bool`; not `Void`).

items.name: is_struct
items.doc: Returns `true` iff `t` is a struct or zoned-struct kind.

items.name: is_enum
items.doc: Returns `true` iff `t` is the `Enum` kind.

items.name: is_pointer
items.doc: Returns `true` iff `t` is `Ptr`, `Ref`, or `MutRef`.

items.name: is_array
items.doc: Returns `true` iff `t` is the `Array` kind.

items.name: count_if
items.doc: Counts pack members for which `pred` returns `true`.

items.name: all
items.doc: Returns `true` iff every pack member satisfies `pred` (vacuously `true` on an empty pack).

items.name: any
items.doc: Returns `true` iff any pack member satisfies `pred` (`false` on an empty pack).

items.name: sum_by
items.doc: Returns the sum of `proj(t)` over the pack — handy for total size, alignment, etc.

items.name: fold_i64
items.signature: fn fold_i64(init: i64, step: fn(i64, Type) -> i64) -> i64
items.doc:
Folds the pack into an `i64` accumulator via `step`, starting from `init`.
Polymorphic-accumulator fold awaits closure-arg generics; `i64` covers the
common cases (counts, sums, max, layout offsets) directly.

items.name: max_by
items.doc: Returns the largest `proj(t)` over the pack (`i64::MIN` on an empty pack).

items.name: min_by
items.doc: Returns the smallest `proj(t)` over the pack (`i64::MAX` on an empty pack).

items.name: find_index
items.doc: Returns the index of the first pack member satisfying `pred`, or `-1` if none.

items.name: argmax_by
items.doc:
Returns the index of the pack member maximising `proj`, or `-1` on an empty pack.
Ties resolve to the lowest index.

items.name: argmin_by
items.doc:
Returns the index of the pack member minimising `proj`, or `-1` on an empty pack.
Ties resolve to the lowest index.

items.name: schema_mix
items.signature: fn schema_mix(h: u64, x: u64) -> u64

items.name: schema_pack
items.doc: Returns the FNV-1a offset basis — the empty-pack base case of the `schema_pack` fold.

items.name: schema_pack
items.doc: Mixes `schema_code::<_H>()` into the fold over the tail pack (head/tail recursion).

items.name: schema_code
items.doc:
Returns a layout-faithful structural hash of `_T`'s in-memory schema.
Mixes kind/size/align, recursing into struct field types; structurally identical
layouts hash equal regardless of names. Pointers are opaque leaves — recursion
stops there, breaking cycles on self-referential types.

items.name: oview_module_ast
items.doc:
Acquires a retained view of the trigger's AST document.

`holder` is null when no document is loaded — branch on that before using `base`.

items.name: base_ptr
items.doc: Returns the document segment's absolute base pointer.

items.name: node_at
items.doc:
Wraps a flat arena offset into a node handle (`WAny::Ref(base + offset)`).

The only `base + offset` step; all further navigation is `WAny::resolve()`.

items.name: root
items.doc:
Returns the document root `WAny`.

DocumentHeader = `{ WAny root }` at base+0; the at-rest root word is
self-relative to base, so it is materialized: Ref → base + delta,
Pod → verbatim, 0 → null Ref.

items.name: tiny_map_get
items.signature: fn tiny_map_get(self: &OView, node: WAny, key: u8) -> WAny
items.doc:
Reads field `key` of a TinyObjectMap node (`node` = Ref to a `WMap<Wu6, WAny>`).

Null/non-ref `node`, or an absent key, yields a null `WAny`.

items.name: get_str_view
items.doc:
Returns the borrowed UTF-8 bytes of a string node (`v` = Ref to a `WString`).

Empty view if `v` is null/non-ref.

items.name: array_len
items.doc: Returns the length of an array node (`v` = Ref to a `WArray<WAny>`); null/non-ref → 0.

items.name: array_get
items.signature: fn array_get(self: &OView, v: WAny, idx: u64) -> WAny
items.doc:
Returns element `idx` of an array node; null/non-ref `v` → null `WAny`.

No bounds check — `idx` must be less than `array_len(v)`.

items.name: get_int
items.doc: Returns a Pod's inline integer (i56, sign-extended); non-pod → 0.

items.name: get_i64
items.doc: Returns a Pod's inline integer as `i64`; alias of `get_int`.

items.name: get_u64
items.doc: Returns a Pod's inline integer cast to `u64`; non-pod → 0.

items.name: get_bool
items.doc: Returns `true` iff `v` is a Pod with a nonzero inline value.

items.name: branch_alloc
items.doc:
Allocates a new `BranchNode` and returns it wrapped as a `NodeARC`.
`BranchNode` is macro-derived with no struct-literal form, so this builds the control
block uninitialised and fills the node fields in place through a raw pointer.

items.name: leaf_alloc
items.doc: Builds a `LeafNode` struct literal and moves it into a fresh `NodeARC`.

items.name: leaf_dir_alloc
items.doc: Builds a `LeafDirNode` struct literal and moves it into a fresh `NodeARC`.

items.name: wview2_from_ptr
items.doc: Builds a reader from a rodata blob pointer (`WritStatic.ptr`).

items.name: wany_from_word
items.doc:
Reconstructs a value-form `WAny` from its raw word (`WAny::raw()`).
Lets callers stash a node handle as a plain `i64` field (sidestepping the borrow-checker's
conservative treatment of the Ref raw-ptr) and rebuild it on use.
A value-form Ref's word IS its absolute ptr (low bit 0); Pod has low bit 1; word 0 = null.

items.name: root
items.doc:
Returns the document root `WAny`.
The at-rest root word at `base+0` is self-relative; this materializes it to value form.

items.name: map_get
items.signature: fn map_get(self: WView2, node: WAny, key: &[u8]) -> WAny
items.doc:
Reads a string-keyed ObjectMap field: `node` (Ref to `WMap<WString,WAny>`) by `key`.
A null/non-ref `node`, or an absent key, yields a null `WAny`.

items.name: tiny_map_get
items.signature: fn tiny_map_get(self: WView2, node: WAny, key: u8) -> WAny
items.doc:
Reads a u8-keyed TinyObjectMap field (the AST/schema flavour: `WMap<Wu6,WAny>`).
A null/non-ref `node`, or an absent key, yields a null `WAny`.

items.name: get_int
items.signature: fn get_int(self: WView2, v: WAny) -> i64
items.doc:
Returns `v` as `i64` — reads either an inline Pod int or a boxed zone `W_I64`/`W_U64`.
Non-int → `0`.

items.name: get_i64
items.signature: fn get_i64(self: WView2, v: WAny) -> i64
items.doc: Returns `v` as `i64`; alias for `get_int`.

items.name: get_u64
items.signature: fn get_u64(self: WView2, v: WAny) -> u64
items.doc: Returns `v` as `u64` (inline Pod int or boxed zone int). Non-int → `0`.

items.name: get_bool
items.signature: fn get_bool(self: WView2, v: WAny) -> bool
items.doc: Returns `v` as `bool`. Non-Pod → `false`.

items.name: get_str_view
items.signature: fn get_str_view(self: WView2, v: WAny) -> SView2
items.doc: Returns borrowed UTF-8 bytes of a string node (Ref to `WString`); empty if null/non-ref.

items.name: array_len
items.signature: fn array_len(self: WView2, v: WAny) -> u64
items.doc: Returns the length of an array node (`v` = Ref to `WArray<WAny>`). Null/non-ref → `0`.

items.name: array_get
items.signature: fn array_get(self: WView2, v: WAny, idx: u64) -> WAny
items.doc: Returns element `idx` of an array node; null/non-ref → null `WAny`.

items.name: max_keys_branch
items.doc: Returns the max key count for branch nodes: CFG `fanout_branch`, else `fanout`, else 4.

items.name: max_keys_leaf
items.doc: Returns the max key count for leaf nodes: CFG `fanout_leaf`, else `fanout`, else 4.

items.name: min_keys_branch
items.doc:
Returns the min key count for a branch node (`max/2`, at least 1); fewer keys means the node
underflowed and must borrow from a sibling or merge during remove rebalance.

items.name: min_keys_leaf
items.doc:
Returns the min key count for a leaf node (`max/2`, at least 1); fewer keys means the node
underflowed and must borrow from a sibling or merge during remove rebalance.

items.name: arc_is_leaf
items.doc: Returns `true` if the node behind `arc` is a leaf, via a layout-driven `NodeBase` header read.

items.name: as_leaf
items.signature: fn as_leaf(arc: &Arc<dyn BTreeNode>) -> *mut LeafNode<K, V, STORE_CFG, CFG>
items.doc:
Casts `arc`'s node data pointer to a typed `LeafNode` pointer; caller must check
`arc_is_leaf` first — no validation is performed.

items.name: as_branch
items.signature: fn as_branch(arc: &Arc<dyn BTreeNode>) -> *mut BranchNode<K, STORE_CFG, CFG>
items.doc:
Casts `arc`'s node data pointer to a typed `BranchNode` pointer; caller must check
`arc_is_leaf` first — no validation is performed.

items.name: leaf_find
items.signature: fn leaf_find(leaf: *mut LeafNode<K, V, STORE_CFG, CFG>, key: K::ViewInStore) -> i64
items.doc: Returns the index of `key` in `leaf`, or `-1` if absent (linear scan).

items.name: leaf_lower_bound
items.signature: fn leaf_lower_bound(leaf: *mut LeafNode<K, V, STORE_CFG, CFG>, key: K::ViewInStore) -> u64
items.doc: Returns the first in-leaf index with `keys[i] >= key`, or the key count if no such index.

items.name: leaf_upper_bound
items.signature: fn leaf_upper_bound(leaf: *mut LeafNode<K, V, STORE_CFG, CFG>, key: K::ViewInStore) -> u64
items.doc:
Returns the first in-leaf index with `keys[i] > key` (strict), or the key count if none.
Mirrors `leaf_lower_bound` with a strict-greater predicate.

items.name: branch_descend_idx
items.signature: fn branch_descend_idx(branch: *mut BranchNode<K, STORE_CFG, CFG>, key: K::ViewInStore) -> u64

items.name: bt_contains_rec
items.signature: fn bt_contains_rec(arc: &Arc<dyn BTreeNode>, key: K::ViewInStore) -> bool
items.doc:
Returns `true` if `key` is present in the subtree rooted at `arc` (read-only recursive
descent; no rc bumps).

items.name: bt_get_rec
items.signature: fn bt_get_rec(arc: &Arc<dyn BTreeNode>, key: K::ViewInStore) -> Option
items.doc: Returns the stored value view for `key` in the subtree rooted at `arc`, or `None` if absent.

items.name: bt_descend_to_n
items.signature: fn bt_descend_to_n(arc: &Arc<dyn BTreeNode>, n: u64, out_idx: *mut u64) -> Option

items.name: bt_lower_bound
items.signature: fn bt_lower_bound(arc: &Arc<dyn BTreeNode>, key: K::ViewInStore) -> u64
items.doc:
Returns the smallest global sorted position whose key is `>= key`; the tree size if `key`
exceeds every key in the tree.

items.name: bt_upper_bound
items.signature: fn bt_upper_bound(arc: &Arc<dyn BTreeNode>, key: K::ViewInStore) -> u64
items.doc:
Returns the smallest global sorted position whose key is `> key` (strict); the tree size if
no key exceeds `key`.

items.name: bt_size_rec
items.doc:
Returns the total key count of the subtree rooted at `arc`.
Sums the `subtree_size` SUM column when populated (no descent); otherwise falls back to a
recursive walk over children.

items.name: descend_leftmost
items.signature: fn descend_leftmost(start: &Arc<dyn BTreeNode>, path: *mut Vec<IterFrame<K, STORE_CFG, CFG>>) -> Arc<dyn BTreeNode>

items.name: descend_rightmost
items.signature: fn descend_rightmost(start: &Arc<dyn BTreeNode>, path: *mut Vec<IterFrame<K, STORE_CFG, CFG>>) -> Arc<dyn BTreeNode>

items.name: bt_iter_open_at
items.signature: fn bt_iter_open_at(arc: &Arc<dyn BTreeNode>, n: u64) -> BtIter<K, V, STORE_CFG, CFG>
items.doc:
Opens a forward iter positioned at the n-th entry (0-based) in sorted order.

Returns an invalid iter (`valid()==false`) when `n >= total` size of the tree.

items.name: bt_iter_open
items.signature: fn bt_iter_open(arc: &Arc<dyn BTreeNode>) -> BtIter<K, V, STORE_CFG, CFG>
items.doc: Opens a forward iter at the leftmost (smallest) entry of the BTree rooted at `arc`.

items.name: bt_iter_open_back
items.signature: fn bt_iter_open_back(arc: &Arc<dyn BTreeNode>) -> BtIter<K, V, STORE_CFG, CFG>
items.doc:
Opens a reverse iter at the rightmost (largest) entry of the BTree rooted at `arc`.

Walk backward with `step_back()`; an empty tree yields an invalid iter (`valid()==false`).

items.name: vec_arc_insert_at
items.signature: fn vec_arc_insert_at(v: *mut Vec<Arc<dyn BTreeNode>>, idx: i64, new_arc: Arc<dyn BTreeNode>) -> void
items.doc:
Inserts `new_arc` at index `idx` in `v`, shifting later elements right.
Caller must ensure `idx <= v.len()`; the shift is whole-handle swaps (no refcount churn).

items.name: node_max_key
items.doc: Returns the max key of `arc`'s subtree, relying on the leaf/branch max-key invariant.

items.name: split_leaf
items.signature: fn split_leaf(leaf: *mut LeafNode<K, V, STORE_CFG, CFG>, new_id: <cfg-slot-type>, snapshot_id: <cfg-slot-type>) -> Arc<dyn BTreeNode>
items.doc:
Splits `leaf` in half, moving the upper half of entries into a freshly allocated
right leaf; `leaf` keeps the lower half. Panics if `keys.len() != vals.len()`.

items.name: split_branch
items.signature: fn split_branch(branch: *mut BranchNode<K, STORE_CFG, CFG>, new_id: <cfg-slot-type>, snapshot_id: <cfg-slot-type>) -> Arc<dyn BTreeNode>
items.doc:
Splits `branch` in half, moving the upper half of keys/children/subtree_size into
a freshly allocated right branch; `branch` keeps the lower half.

items.name: insert_rec
items.signature: fn insert_rec(arc: &mut Arc<dyn BTreeNode>, key: K::ViewInStore, val: V::ViewInStore, qkey: K::ViewInStore, cur_snapshot_id: <cfg-slot-type>, id_ctr_p: *mut <cfg-slot-type>) -> InsertOutcome<K, STORE_CFG, CFG>
items.doc:
Recursively inserts `key`/`val` (positioned by `qkey`) into the subtree rooted at
`arc`, CoW-cloning any node not owned by `cur_snapshot_id` and splitting on overflow.
Returns an `InsertOutcome` reporting whether an existing key was replaced and
whether `arc` split.

items.name: bt_insert
items.signature: fn bt_insert(root: &mut Arc<dyn BTreeNode>, key: K::ViewInStore, val: V::ViewInStore, qkey: K::ViewInStore, cur_snapshot_id: <cfg-slot-type>, id_ctr_p: *mut <cfg-slot-type>) -> Option
items.doc:
Inserts `key`/`val` into the tree rooted at `root`, growing a new root when the
old root splits. Returns the previous value if `key` already existed (mirrors
Rust `BTreeMap::insert`).

items.name: rebalance_leaf_child
items.signature: fn rebalance_leaf_child(parent_branch: *mut BranchNode<K, STORE_CFG, CFG>, i: i64, cur_snapshot_id: <cfg-slot-type>, id_ctr_p: *mut <cfg-slot-type>) -> void
items.doc:
Rebalances leaf child `i` of `parent_branch` after underflow: merges with a
sibling when the combined length fits `max_keys`, otherwise borrows one entry
from whichever sibling has more keys.

items.name: rebalance_branch_child
items.signature: fn rebalance_branch_child(parent_branch: *mut BranchNode<K, STORE_CFG, CFG>, i: i64, cur_snapshot_id: <cfg-slot-type>, id_ctr_p: *mut <cfg-slot-type>) -> void
items.doc:
Rebalances branch child `i` of `parent_branch` after underflow: merges with a
sibling when the combined length fits `max_keys`, otherwise borrows one
(key, child) pair from whichever sibling has more keys.

items.name: remove_rec
items.signature: fn remove_rec(arc: &mut Arc<dyn BTreeNode>, qkey: K::ViewInStore, cur_snapshot_id: <cfg-slot-type>, id_ctr_p: *mut <cfg-slot-type>) -> RemoveOutcome
items.doc:
Recursively removes `qkey` from the subtree rooted at `arc`, CoW-cloning any node
not owned by `cur_snapshot_id` and rebalancing children that underflow.
Returns a `RemoveOutcome` reporting whether the key was found and whether `arc`
itself underflowed.

items.name: bt_remove
items.signature: fn bt_remove(root: &mut Arc<dyn BTreeNode>, qkey: K::ViewInStore, cur_snapshot_id: <cfg-slot-type>, id_ctr_p: *mut <cfg-slot-type>) -> bool
items.doc:
Removes `qkey` from the tree rooted at `root`, collapsing the root while it's a
branch with a single remaining child. Returns `true` if the key was found and removed.

items.name: mutex_new
items.doc: Creates a new `Mutex` wrapping `data`, initializing the underlying `pthread_mutex_t`.

items.name: mutex_destroy
items.doc: Destroys the underlying `pthread_mutex_t` and frees the raw heap block.

items.name: rwlock_new
items.doc: Creates a new `RwLock` wrapping `data`, initializing the underlying `pthread_rwlock_t`.

items.name: rwlock_destroy
items.doc: Destroys the underlying `pthread_rwlock_t` and frees the raw heap block.

items.name: locking_store_new
items.doc: Creates an empty `LockingStore` with block/snapshot id counters both at 0.

items.name: dir_split_leaf
items.signature: fn dir_split_leaf(leaf: *mut LeafDirNode<u64, STORE_CFG, @hs_e04e579d2c32aa01>, new_id: <cfg-slot-type>, snapshot_id: <cfg-slot-type>) -> Arc<dyn BTreeNode>

items.name: dir_leaf_lower_bound
items.signature: fn dir_leaf_lower_bound(leaf: *mut LeafDirNode<u64, STORE_CFG, @hs_e04e579d2c32aa01>, ctr_id: u64) -> u64

items.name: dir_branch_descend_idx
items.signature: fn dir_branch_descend_idx(br: *mut BranchNode<u64, STORE_CFG, @hs_e04e579d2c32aa01>, ctr_id: u64) -> u64

items.name: dir_insert_rec
items.signature: fn dir_insert_rec(arc: &mut Arc<dyn BTreeNode>, ctr_id: u64, val_arc: Arc<dyn BTreeNode>, cur_snapshot_id: <cfg-slot-type>, id_ctr_p: *mut <cfg-slot-type>) -> DirInsertOutcome<STORE_CFG>

items.name: dir_insert
items.signature: fn dir_insert(snap_dir: &mut Arc<dyn BTreeNode>, ctr_id: u64, val_arc: Arc<dyn BTreeNode>, cur_snapshot_id: <cfg-slot-type>, id_ctr_p: *mut <cfg-slot-type>) -> void
items.doc:
Inserts `ctr_id` → `val_arc` into the dir tree, growing a new root on split.
Takes ownership of `val_arc`; an existing `ctr_id` has its value replaced.

items.name: dir_lookup
items.signature: fn dir_lookup(snap_dir: &Arc<dyn BTreeNode>, ctr_id: u64) -> *mut Arc<dyn BTreeNode>
items.doc: Finds `ctr_id` in the dir tree; returns its `NodeARC` slot pointer, or null when absent.

items.name: store_open
items.doc: Creates a store seeded with an empty genesis commit at id `GENESIS`.

items.name: snap_fork
items.doc: Starts a new mutable snapshot from committed `parent_commit` with a fresh snapshot id.

items.name: create_ctr
items.signature: fn create_ctr(snap: &mut Snap<STORE_CFG>, ctr_id: u64) -> ContainerRef<STORE_CFG, CFG>
items.doc: Creates an empty container under `ctr_id` in the snap's dir and returns its open handle.

items.name: open_ctr
items.signature: fn open_ctr(snap: &mut Snap<STORE_CFG>, ctr_id: u64) -> Option
items.doc: Opens the container at `ctr_id`: `Some(ContainerRef)` sharing the dir slot's rc, else `None`.

items.name: hashmap_new
items.doc: Creates an empty `HashMap` with capacity 16.

items.name: hashmap_with_capacity
items.doc: Creates an empty `HashMap` with `cap_hint` rounded up to a power of two (minimum 8).

items.name: message
items.doc: Returns the error text as a borrowed `str` view, valid while the `QError` lives.

items.name: qfail
items.signature: fn qfail(a: &[u8], b: &[u8], c: &[u8], d: &[u8], e: &[u8]) -> QError

items.name: fidx
items.signature: fn fidx(self: &SchemaCatalog, si: i64, fname: &[u8]) -> i64

items.name: add_schema
items.signature: fn add_schema(self: &mut SchemaCatalog, name: &[u8], code: u64) -> void

items.name: add_field
items.signature: fn add_field(self: &mut SchemaCatalog, sname: &[u8], field: &[u8], key: i64, ty: &[u8]) -> void

items.name: add_edge
items.signature: fn add_edge(self: &mut SchemaCatalog, sname: &[u8], field: &[u8], key: i64, target: &[u8]) -> void

items.name: merge_static
items.signature: fn merge_static(self: &mut SchemaCatalog, blob: WritStatic) -> void

items.name: new
items.doc: Creates an empty environment with no bindings and no registered UDFs/UDAs.

items.name: bind_node
items.signature: fn bind_node(self: &mut QEnv, name: &[u8], node: WAny) -> void
items.doc:
Binds `name` to a schema'd Writ object (a `WAny` ref to a schema-stamped TOM).
The schema code is read off the object; a non-schema value binds with code 0 and is
diagnosed by the strict checker on first use.

items.name: bind_i64
items.signature: fn bind_i64(self: &mut QEnv, name: &[u8], v: i64) -> void
items.doc: Binds `name` to an `i64` query/template parameter.

items.name: bind_f64
items.signature: fn bind_f64(self: &mut QEnv, name: &[u8], v: f64) -> void
items.doc: Binds `name` to an `f64` query/template parameter.

items.name: bind_bool
items.signature: fn bind_bool(self: &mut QEnv, name: &[u8], v: bool) -> void
items.doc: Binds `name` to a `bool` query/template parameter.

items.name: bind_str
items.signature: fn bind_str(self: &mut QEnv, name: &[u8], v: &[u8]) -> void
items.doc: Binds `name` to a `str` parameter; the view must stay valid across `render`/`run`.

items.name: bind_source
items.signature: fn bind_source(self: &mut QEnv, name: &[u8], arr: WAny) -> void
items.doc:
Binds `name` to a query source: a Writ array of schema'd rows (`from <name> v` scans it).
The element schema resolves live at `run` (off the first element's stamped code) — an
empty array binds fine and yields no rows.
The caller keeps the backing doc alive across `run`.

items.name: bind_node_erased
items.signature: fn bind_node_erased(self: &mut QEnv, name: &[u8], node: WAny) -> void
items.doc:
Binds `name` to an erased (lenient) node: `name.field` resolves at runtime by name.
A string-keyed Writ map gets by key; a schema-stamped TOM whose schema is in the catalog
gets by the cataloged key; anything else (missing key, unknown schema, non-object) reads
as `RtVal::Null`, which propagates CEL-style through operators. No catalog entry needed.

items.name: bind_source_erased
items.signature: fn bind_source_erased(self: &mut QEnv, name: &[u8], arr: WAny) -> void
items.doc:
Binds `name` to an erased source: a Writ array of lenient rows (string-keyed maps /
arbitrary Writ values). Scans bind the row var leniently; field types are unknown at
compile, so lenient rows never serve as hash-join keys (such joins take the loop tier).

items.name: register_fn
items.signature: fn register_fn(self: &mut QEnv, name: &[u8], f: fn(&[RtVal]) -> RtVal, args: &[&[u8]], ret: &[u8]) -> bool
items.doc:
Registers a scalar UDF with its declared signature: `args` are EL type names
(`"i64"`/`"f64"`/`"bool"`/`"str"`, max 4), `ret` likewise. The checker resolves calls
against builtins first, then this registry; re-registering a name overwrites it.
Returns `false` (registers nothing) on a bad type name, >4 args, or a full registry.

items.name: register_agg
items.signature: fn register_agg(self: &mut QEnv, name: &[u8], init: fn() -> RtVal, step: fn(RtVal, RtVal) -> RtVal, fin: fn(RtVal) -> RtVal, arg_ty: &[u8], ret_ty: &[u8]) -> bool
items.doc:
Registers a UDA: `init() -> acc`, `step(acc, arg) -> acc`, `fin(acc) -> result`.
`arg_ty`/`ret_ty` are the declared EL types of the aggregate argument and the finalized
result. Builtin aggregate names shadow UDAs. Returns `false` (nothing registered) on a
bad type name or a full registry (same contract as `register_fn`).

items.name: compile
items.doc:
Compiles template text against the catalog into a reusable `Tpl`.
Runs parse, the shared IR optimizer, and the env-independent checks; unknown function
names defer to the render-time strict check (they may be env-registered UDFs).
Errors are values (`Result`), never compile diagnostics.

items.name: render
items.doc:
Renders the template against `env`, returning the output string.
Runs the full strict check (var→schema resolution, field types) against this env first;
a math error in an embedded expression aborts the render as a `QError`.
Re-entrant over different envs (the check is per-env by design).

items.doc:

items.name: get_node
items.signature: fn get_node(self: &QRows, r: i64, c: i64) -> WAny
items.doc:
Returns cell (`r`,`c`) as a row-object handle (identity / `find` projections) into the

items.name: is_null

items.doc:
Returns whether cell (`r`,`c`) is Null. Lenient projections may carry Null (the typed
getters read zero-values for it) — this is the distinguishing probe. Out-of-range
reads also report `true`.

items.name: col_ty
items.doc:
Returns column `c`'s checked type name — `"i64"`/`"str"`/`"bool"`/`"f64"`/`"node"`/
`"dyn"` (lenient, runtime-typed) — or `""` out of range.

Compiles query text against the catalog into a reusable `Query`.
Parses, registers/stratifies `rel` blocks, lowers and simplifies the algebra, and runs
the env-independent checks. Errors are values (`Result`), never compile diagnostics.

items.name: run
items.doc:
Executes the query against `env` and materializes the result as a self-contained `QRows`.
Runs the full strict check against this env first, then materializes the `rel`s (topo
order, semi-naive where recursive) and executes the entry pipeline. Re-entrant over
different envs (sources, params and the UDF/UDA registry live on the env).

items.name: args_count
items.doc:
Returns the number of command-line arguments, including `argv[0]`.

Returns `0` if `/proc/self/cmdline` could not be read at startup (e.g. in a sandbox).

items.name: arg
items.doc: Returns command-line argument `i` as a `str`, or `""` if `i` is out of range.

items.name: var
items.doc:
Returns the value of the environment variable `name` as an owned copy, or `None` if unset.

The C environ string is copied, so the result survives a later `setenv`.

items.name: var_is_set
items.doc: Returns `true` if the environment variable `name` is set.

items.name: current_exe
items.doc:
Returns the path of the running executable, or `None` if it cannot be determined.

Resolved through `/proc/self/exe`, following the symlink chain to the real file.

items.name: current_dir
items.doc: Returns the current working directory as an owned `String`, or `None` on error.

items.name: set_current_dir
items.doc:
Changes the process working directory to `path`.

Returns the raw libc `chdir` result: `0` on success, `-1` on failure.

items.name: var
items.doc:
Returns the value of the environment variable named by NUL-terminated C string `name`,
or `None` if unset.

The returned `str` borrows libc environ storage; a later `setenv` may invalidate it.

items.name: var_is_set
items.doc: Returns `true` if the environment variable named by NUL-terminated C string `name` is set.

items.name: has_flag
items.doc:
Returns true if `--long_name` or `-short_name` appears as a bare flag in argv.
Stops scanning at the `--` terminator; `short_name` may be `""` to disable short-flag matching.

items.name: get_option
items.doc:
Looks up an option value: `--long=VALUE`, `--long VALUE`, or `-s VALUE` (short form
only when `short_name.len() == 1`).
Returns `Option::None` if not found, or if scanning reaches the `--` terminator first.

items.name: positional_count
items.doc: Returns the count of positional arguments (non-flags; everything after `--` counts too).

items.name: positional
items.doc: Returns the `idx`-th positional argument (0-indexed), or `""` if out of range.

items.name: program_name
items.doc: Returns the program name (`argv[0]`); useful for usage messages.

items.name: print
items.doc:
Writes `s` to standard output, without a trailing newline.

Unbuffered; write errors are silently ignored.

items.name: println
items.doc:
Writes `s` followed by a newline to standard output.

Unbuffered; write errors are silently ignored.

items.name: eprint
items.doc:
Writes `s` to standard error, without a trailing newline.

Unbuffered; write errors are silently ignored.

items.name: eprintln
items.doc:
Writes `s` followed by a newline to standard error.

Unbuffered; write errors are silently ignored.

items.name: print_string
items.doc:
Writes `s` to standard output, without a trailing newline.

`String` counterpart of `print`. Unbuffered; write errors are silently ignored.

items.name: println_string
items.doc:
Writes `s` followed by a newline to standard output.

`String` counterpart of `println`. Unbuffered; write errors are silently ignored.

items.name: eprint_string
items.doc:
Writes `s` to standard error, without a trailing newline.

`String` counterpart of `eprint`. Unbuffered; write errors are silently ignored.

items.name: eprintln_string
items.doc:
Writes `s` followed by a newline to standard error.

`String` counterpart of `eprintln`. Unbuffered; write errors are silently ignored.

items.name: fmt_into
items.signature: fn fmt_into(pat: &[u8], fi: i64, flen: i64, f: &mut Formatter) -> void

items.name: fmt_into
items.signature: fn fmt_into(pat: &[u8], fi: i64, flen: i64, f: &mut Formatter, first: H, rest: T) -> void

items.name: format_args_str
items.doc:
Formats `args` into a new `String` per the `{}`/`{:?}` placeholders in `pat`.
Runtime fallback for dynamic (non-literal) patterns; handles only `{}` (Display),
`{:?}` (Debug), and `{{`/`}}` escapes — no format-spec parsing.

items.name: format
items.doc: Returns `format_args_str(pat, args...)`; back-compat alias for plain fn-call sites.

items.name: format
items.doc:
Implements the `format!` macro: expands to a `format_args_str(...)` call yielding `String`.
Invocation: `let s: String = format!("n={}, ok={}", 42, true);`.

items.name: __fmt_print
items.doc: Writes `s` to stdout; `pub` only so `print!` expansions resolve it.

items.name: __fmt_println
items.doc: Writes `s` and a newline to stdout; `pub` only so `println!` expansions resolve it.

items.name: __fmt_eprint
items.doc: Writes `s` to stderr; `pub` only so `eprint!` expansions resolve it.

items.name: __fmt_eprintln
items.doc: Writes `s` and a newline to stderr; `pub` only so `eprintln!` expansions resolve it.

items.name: print
items.doc:
Implements the `print!` macro: formats and writes to stdout, no trailing newline.
Invocation: `print!("n={}", 42);`.

items.name: println
items.doc:
Implements the `println!` macro: formats and writes a newline-terminated line to stdout.
Invocation: `println!("n={}", 42);`.

items.name: eprint
items.doc:
Implements the `eprint!` macro: formats and writes to stderr, no trailing newline.
Invocation: `eprint!("n={}", 42);`.

items.name: eprintln
items.doc:
Implements the `eprintln!` macro: formats and writes a newline-terminated line to stderr.
Invocation: `eprintln!("n={}", 42);`.

items.name: write
items.doc:
Implements the `write!` macro: formats and writes to a sink via `write_str`, yielding its
`Result<(), Error>`. Invocation: `write!(f, "({}, {})", self.x, self.y)`.

items.name: writeln
items.doc:
Implements the `writeln!` macro: like `write!` with a trailing newline appended.
Invocation: `writeln!(sink, "m={}", 7)`.

items.name: __fmt_panic
items.doc: Panics with `msg`; `-> !` makes `panic!` expansions type as `!` in expression position.

items.name: panic
items.doc:
Implements the `panic!` macro: formats a message and aborts; the expansion types as `!`.
Invocation: `panic!("bad state: {}", x);`.

items.name: assert
items.doc:
Implements the `assert!` macro: panics if the condition is false.
Invocation: `assert!(a < b)` or `assert!(ok, "bad n={}", n)` (custom formatted message).

items.name: assert_eq
items.doc:
Implements the `assert_eq!` macro: panics with both values Debug-rendered when `a != b`.
Invocation: `assert_eq!(2 + 2, 4);`.
The RHS is re-evaluated for the message on failure (at most one extra eval, failure-only).

items.name: assert_ne
items.doc:
Implements the `assert_ne!` macro: panics with both values Debug-rendered when `a == b`.
Invocation: `assert_ne!(id, 0);`.
The RHS is re-evaluated for the message on failure (at most one extra eval, failure-only).

items.name: read_exact
items.signature: fn read_exact(r: &mut R, buf: *mut u8, len: i64) -> i64
items.doc:
Reads exactly `len` bytes into `buf`, looping over partial reads.
Returns 0 on success, -1 on early EOF, negative errno on error.

items.name: io_slice
items.signature: fn io_slice(ptr: *const u8, len: i64) -> IoSlice
items.doc: Builds an `IoSlice` from a raw pointer and length.

items.name: write_all
items.signature: fn write_all(w: &mut W, buf: *const u8, len: i64) -> i64
items.doc:
Writes all `len` bytes from `buf`, looping over partial writes.
Returns `0` on success, or the negative errno from the first failing write.

items.name: fd_reader
items.doc: Creates an `FdReader` over `fd`.

items.name: fd_writer
items.doc: Creates an `FdWriter` over `fd`.

items.name: read
items.signature: fn read(self: &mut FdReader, buf: *mut u8, len: i64) -> i64

items.name: write
items.signature: fn write(self: &mut FdWriter, buf: *const u8, len: i64) -> i64

items.name: buf_writer_wrap
items.signature: fn buf_writer_wrap(w: W, cap: i64) -> BufWriter<W>
items.doc: Wraps any `Write` sink in a `BufWriter` with a `cap`-byte buffer.

items.name: buf_writer_new
items.signature: fn buf_writer_new(fd: i32, cap: i64) -> BufWriter<FdWriter>
items.doc: Creates a `BufWriter` over a raw fd with a `cap`-byte buffer.

items.name: buf_writer_default
items.doc: Creates a `BufWriter` over a raw fd with the default 8 KiB buffer.

items.name: buf_reader_wrap
items.signature: fn buf_reader_wrap(r: R, cap: i64) -> BufReader<R>
items.doc: Wraps any `Read` source in a `BufReader` with a `cap`-byte buffer.

items.name: buf_reader_new
items.signature: fn buf_reader_new(fd: i32, cap: i64) -> BufReader<FdReader>
items.doc: Creates a `BufReader` over a raw fd with a `cap`-byte buffer.

items.name: buf_reader_default
items.doc: Creates a `BufReader` over a raw fd with the default 8 KiB buffer.

items.name: buf_reader_inner_mut
items.doc:
Borrows the inner reader mutably.
Useful when the caller must also write to the same underlying stream (e.g.
an HTTP server reading via `BufReader<TcpStream>` and replying directly).
Direct reads on the inner reader skip any bytes already buffered.

items.name: buf_writer_inner_mut
items.doc:
Borrows the inner writer mutably.
Direct writes to it land before any still-buffered bytes — flush first.

items.name: bytes_empty
items.doc: Returns an empty `Bytes` that owns no buffer.

items.name: bytes_from_slice
items.signature: fn bytes_from_slice(src: *const u8, len: i64) -> Bytes
items.doc:
Creates a `Bytes` by copying `len` bytes from `src` into a fresh buffer.
`len <= 0` yields the empty `Bytes` without reading `src`.

items.name: bytes_from_string
items.doc: Creates a `Bytes` by copying the contents of `s`.

items.name: bytes_from_str
items.doc: Creates a `Bytes` by copying the contents of `s`.

items.name: len
items.doc: Returns the length of this view in bytes.

items.name: is_empty
items.doc: Returns `true` if this view has zero length.

items.name: as_ptr
items.doc: Returns a pointer to the first byte of the view; null when the handle owns no buffer.

items.name: clone_bytes
items.doc: Returns a new handle to the same buffer and range (refcount bump; no copy).

items.name: slice
items.signature: fn slice(self: *mut Bytes, start: i64, end: i64) -> Bytes
items.doc: Returns a zero-copy sub-view over `[start, end)`; indices are clamped to the slice.

items.name: drop_bytes
items.doc:
Releases this handle and resets it to empty; frees the buffer when the last handle drops.
Idempotent: calling again on the emptied handle is a no-op.

items.name: get
items.signature: fn get(self: *mut Bytes, i: i64) -> u8
items.doc:
Returns the byte at index `i`. No bounds check: `i` outside `[0, len)` or an
empty handle is undefined behavior.

items.name: bytes_mut_with_capacity
items.doc: Creates an empty `BytesMut` with at least `cap` bytes of capacity preallocated.

items.name: bytes_mut_new
items.doc: Creates an empty `BytesMut` with no preallocated storage.

items.name: len
items.doc: Returns the number of bytes written so far.

items.name: capacity
items.doc: Returns the current allocated capacity in bytes.

items.name: as_ptr
items.doc: Returns a read pointer to the buffer start; null when nothing is allocated.

items.name: as_mut_ptr
items.doc:
Returns a write pointer to the buffer start; null when nothing is allocated.
Invalidated by any growing call (`reserve`, `push_byte`, `extend_*`).

items.name: reserve
items.signature: fn reserve(self: *mut BytesMut, additional: i64) -> void
items.doc:
Ensures capacity for at least `additional` more bytes beyond the current length.
Grows geometrically (doubling, minimum 8), reallocating the buffer in place.

items.name: push_byte
items.signature: fn push_byte(self: *mut BytesMut, b: u8) -> void
items.doc: Appends a single byte, growing the buffer if needed.

items.name: extend_from_slice
items.signature: fn extend_from_slice(self: *mut BytesMut, src: *const u8, n: i64) -> void
items.doc: Appends `n` bytes copied from `src`, growing the buffer if needed; `n <= 0` is a no-op.

items.name: extend_from_string
items.doc: Appends the contents of `s`.

items.name: extend_from_str
items.doc: Appends the contents of `s`.

items.name: freeze
items.doc:
Hands out an immutable `Bytes` view covering the populated region.
Consumes the `BytesMut` (caller must not reuse it; it is reset to empty).

items.name: drop_mut
items.doc:
Releases the underlying buffer and resets this handle to empty; frees the buffer
when the last handle drops.

items.name: io_error_new
items.doc: Creates an `IoError` not attributed to any OS error; `raw_os_error()` on it returns `None`.

items.name: io_error_from_errno
items.doc:
Wraps an OS-derived `errno` into a structured `IoError`.
Maps the usual errno subset to `ErrorKind` variants; unknown values map to `ErrorKind::Other`.
The message is left empty.

items.name: kind
items.doc: Returns the error's `ErrorKind`.

items.name: raw_os_error
items.doc: Returns the originating OS `errno`, or `None` if the error is not OS-derived.

items.name: borrowed_buf_new
items.signature: fn borrowed_buf_new(data: *mut u8, capacity: i64) -> BorrowedBuf
items.doc:
Creates a `BorrowedBuf` over `capacity` writable bytes at `data`, with nothing filled.
`data` must point to at least `capacity` writable bytes that outlive the buffer.

items.name: capacity
items.doc: Returns the total buffer size in bytes.

items.name: filled
items.doc: Returns the count of bytes filled so far.

items.name: remaining
items.doc: Returns the number of unfilled bytes remaining.

items.name: advance
items.signature: fn advance(self: &mut BorrowedBuf, n: i64) -> void
items.doc:
Advances `filled` by `n`; no bounds check against `capacity`.
Caller asserts `n` bytes are valid in `[self.filled, self.filled + n)`.

items.name: clear
items.doc: Resets `filled` to `0`; the underlying bytes are untouched.
