items.doc: Generator invoked once per `next`.

items.name: SuccessorsIter
items.doc: Unfolding source: yields the pending value, then advances it via `succ`.

items.name: next_val
items.doc: Value the next `next` call will yield; `None` = exhausted.

items.name: succ
items.doc: Successor function producing the follow-up value.

items.name: RepeatNIter
items.doc: Bounded repeat source: yields `value` exactly `count` times.

items.name: count
items.doc: Remaining number of yields.

items.name: value
items.doc: Value copied out on each yield.

items.name: OnceWithIter
items.doc: Single-shot generator source: `f` fires exactly once, on the first `next`.

items.name: f
items.doc: Generator invoked on the first `next`.

items.name: fired
items.doc: Set after the single yield.

items.name: _t
items.doc: Phantom anchor for `T`; value unused.

items.name: RepeatWithIter
items.doc: Infinite generator source: calls `f` on every `next`, never returns `None`.

items.name: f
items.doc: Generator invoked once per `next`.

items.name: _t
items.doc: Phantom anchor for `T`; value unused.

items.name: Splitter
items.doc:
Yields substrings of the input separated by a fixed multi-byte `str` separator.

Segments are sub-`str` views into the original buffer, which must outlive iteration.

items.name: ByteSplitter
items.doc:
Yields substrings of the input separated by a fixed single-byte separator.

Common fast path for `','`, `'/'`, etc. Segments are sub-`str` views into the
original buffer, which must outlive iteration.

items.name: SplitPat
items.doc:
Generic pattern-driven splitter over a `str`, parameterized by any `Pattern` type.

Segments are sub-`str` views into the original buffer, which must outlive iteration.

items.name: Chars
CharIndex
items.doc: A byte-offset + codepoint pair yielded by `CharIndices`.

items.name: idx
items.doc: Byte offset of the codepoint's first byte in the source slice.

items.name: ch
items.doc: The decoded codepoint (U+FFFD for invalid input).

items.name: CharIndices
items.doc: Iterator over the codepoints of a string slice together with their byte offsets.

items.name: Bytes
items.doc: Iterator over the raw bytes of a string slice, with no UTF-8 decoding.

items.name: Lines
items.doc:
Iterator over the lines of a string slice, split on `\n` with a preceding `\r` stripped.
A trailing newline does not produce a final empty line (Rust `str::lines` semantics).

items.name: Utf8Error
items.doc: Describes where a UTF-8 byte stream failed validation; returned by `utf8_validate`.

items.name: valid_up_to
items.doc: Number of bytes that were valid before the error.

items.name: error_len
items.doc: Length of the invalid sequence (1..=4), or -1 for a truncated sequence at the tail.

items.name: Utf8Chunk
items.doc: A chunk from `Utf8Chunks`: a maximal valid prefix plus a trailing run of invalid bytes.

items.name: valid
items.doc: The well-formed UTF-8 slice (possibly empty).

items.name: invalid_bytes
items.doc: Number of invalid bytes following `valid`; 0 when the input ends after `valid`.

items.name: Utf8Chunks
items.doc: Iterator over runs of valid UTF-8 interleaved with invalid bytes; mirrors Rust's `Utf8Chunks`.

items.name: ParseBoolError
items.doc: Error returned by `bool_from_str`; opaque (no fields), mirroring Rust.

items.name: Matches
items.doc:
Iterator over the non-overlapping matches of a pattern in a string slice.
A zero-length match advances one byte, so empty patterns cannot loop forever.

items.name: StrMatchIndex
items.doc: A byte-offset + matched-slice pair yielded by `MatchIndices`.

items.name: idx
items.doc: Byte offset of the match within the haystack.

items.name: m
items.doc: The matched slice.

items.name: MatchIndices
items.doc: Iterator over non-overlapping pattern matches together with their byte offsets.

items.name: Location
items.doc:
Identifies the source position (file, line, column) where a panic originated.
Mirror of `core::panic::Location`; populated only by explicit construction today —
there is no compiler-injected `caller()` yet.

items.name: file
items.doc: Source file path.

items.name: line
items.doc: Line number within `file`.

items.name: column
items.doc: Column number within `line`.

items.name: PanicInfo
items.doc: Carries a panic's message plus its optional origin; mirror of `core::panic::PanicInfo`.

items.name: message
items.doc: The `str` that was passed to `panic()`.

items.name: location
items.doc: Panic origin; currently always `None` — the hook does not capture call-site location.

items.name: AssertUnwindSafe
items.doc:
Wraps a value to unconditionally assert `UnwindSafe`/`RefUnwindSafe` — the escape
hatch for types without the marker impls.

items.name: value
items.doc: The wrapped value.

items.name: TypeId
items.doc:
Identifies a type as an opaque, comparable 128-bit nominal UID.
Carried as two `u64` halves (`lo` = bits 0..63, `hi` = bits 64..127); 128 bits keeps
cross-type collision probability at 2^-128 (matches Rust's `TypeId`) — a collision
would make `downcast` unsound.

items.name: lo
items.doc: Low 64 bits (bits 0..63) of the type UID.

items.name: hi
items.doc: High 64 bits (bits 64..127) of the type UID.

items.name: ArrayIntoIter
items.doc: Owned-array iterator; yields the elements of `[T; N]` by value.

items.name: buf
items.doc: The array being consumed.

items.name: idx
items.doc: Index of the next element to yield.

items.name: AsciiChar
items.doc:
An ASCII character: a `u8` guaranteed to be in `0..=127`.

Newtype struct, not Rust's 128-variant enum; constructors enforce the range invariant.

items.name: UnsafeCell
items.doc:
Wraps a `T` mutable through `&self` — the primitive underlying all interior-mutability types.
Auto-`!Sync` and invariant in `T`; aliasing discipline is the caller's `unsafe` obligation.

items.name: Cell
items.doc: Provides safe interior mutability for `Copy` types via by-value `get`/`set`.

items.name: BorrowError
items.doc: Returned by `RefCell::try_borrow` when the value is already mutably borrowed.

items.name: BorrowMutError
items.doc: Returned by `RefCell::try_borrow_mut` when the value is already borrowed.

items.name: RefCell
items.doc:
Wraps a value with runtime-checked dynamic borrowing via `Ref`/`RefMut` guards.
Guards hold raw pointers, not lifetimes — a guard must not outlive its cell.

items.name: Ref
items.doc: Guards a shared borrow of a `RefCell` value; access via `get`, released on drop.

items.name: RefMut
items.doc: Guards an exclusive borrow of a `RefCell` value; access via `get`/`get_mut`, released on drop.

items.name: OnceCell
items.doc:
Provides write-once interior mutability: set once via `&self`, then read-only.
Construct with `once_cell_new` (bears the `T: Default` bound that seeds storage).

items.name: LazyCell
items.doc:
Defers initialisation: first `force` calls the stored `fn() -> T` and caches the result.
Construct with `lazy_cell_new`.

items.name: AtomicI32
items.doc: Wraps an `i32` for lock-free atomic access shared across threads and fibers.

items.name: AtomicU32
items.doc: Wraps a `u32` for lock-free atomic access; stored internally as `i32` bits.

items.name: AtomicI64
items.doc: Wraps an `i64` for lock-free atomic access shared across threads and fibers.

items.name: AtomicU64
items.doc: Wraps a `u64` for lock-free atomic access; stored internally as `i64` bits.

items.name: AtomicUsize
items.doc: Wraps a `usize` (64-bit on this target) for lock-free atomic access; `i64`-backed.

items.name: AtomicIsize
items.doc: Wraps an `isize` (64-bit on this target) for lock-free atomic access; `i64`-backed.

items.name: AtomicBool
items.doc: Wraps a `bool` for lock-free atomic access; backed by an `i32` (0 = false, 1 = true).

items.name: AtomicPtr
items.doc: Wraps a raw `*mut T` pointer for lock-free atomic access; stored as `i64` bits.

items.name: AtomicI8
items.doc: Wraps an `i8` for lock-free atomic access; storage widened to 32 bits (4-byte footprint).

items.name: AtomicI16
items.doc: Wraps an `i16` for lock-free atomic access; storage widened to 32 bits (4-byte footprint).

items.name: AtomicU8
items.doc: Wraps a `u8` for lock-free atomic access; storage widened to 32 bits (4-byte footprint).

items.name: AtomicU16
items.doc: Wraps a `u16` for lock-free atomic access; storage widened to 32 bits (4-byte footprint).

items.name: ConvertError
items.doc:
Reports a failed `TryFrom`/`TryInto` conversion.

Empty marker payload: these conversions only fail in one way (out-of-range),
so the error value is information-free.

items.name: Buffer
items.doc: Typed homogeneous container backed by `DT::Store`; elements pass as `DT::ViewInStore`.

items.name: PrimVec
items.doc: Growable heap-backed vector of POD `T` — the generic `DynamicStorage` leaf.

items.name: data
items.doc: Pointer to the heap buffer; null when nothing is allocated.

items.name: len
items.doc: Number of live elements.

items.name: cap
items.doc: Allocated capacity, in elements.

items.name: WritStatic
items.doc: Compile-time handle to a `@`-literal writ blob laid out in rodata.

items.name: ptr
items.doc: Points at the first blob byte, past the 8-byte size prefix.

items.name: StringView
items.doc:
Non-owning byte-slice view (pointer + length) into blob-resident string data.
Returned by string reads; a pure value — no allocation.

items.name: ptr
items.doc: Pointer to the first byte of the string within its blob.

items.name: len
items.doc: Length of the string in bytes.

items.name: WritStringStorage
items.doc:
SoA storage for strings: one packed byte buffer plus parallel end offsets.
Owns `bytes` and `ends`; call `storage_drop` to free them before dropping the struct.

items.name: bytes
items.doc: Packed string bytes, back-to-back with no separators.

items.name: b_len
items.doc: Bytes currently used in `bytes`.

items.name: b_cap
items.doc: Allocated capacity of `bytes`, in bytes.

items.name: ends
items.doc: Exclusive end offset of each stored string within `bytes`.

items.name: o_len
items.doc: Number of strings stored (length of `ends`).

items.name: o_cap
items.doc: Allocated capacity of `ends`, in elements.

items.name: StrDt
items.doc: Marker datatype: `Buffer<StrDt>` stores `StringView`s in the SoA string store.

items.name: NonNull
items.doc:
A `*mut T` that is guaranteed non-null.

Type-level "never null" invariant used inside smart pointers (`Box`, `Rc`,
`Arc`, `Vec`). Construct via `new` (checked) or `new_unchecked` (unsafe).

items.name: FxHasher
items.doc:
Fast non-cryptographic `Hasher` using rustc's FxHash mix; not collision-resistant
against adversarial inputs.

items.name: SipHasher
items.doc:
SipHash-2-4 `Hasher` with a 128-bit key; each `write_*` feeds one 64-bit block.

`v0..v3` are the four SipHash internal state words.

items.name: length
items.doc: Count of bytes compressed so far; each `write_*` accounts eight.

items.name: DefaultBuildHasher
PhantomPinned
items.doc:
A zero-sized field that opts a struct out of `Unpin`. A struct containing one (or marked `#[pinned]`) is `!Unpin`, so it cannot be moved out of a `Pin

`.

items.name: PhantomData
items.doc:
A zero-sized type-anchor for `T`.
Unlike Rust, does not drive dropck/variance/auto-traits here — serves only as a
type anchor, equivalent to a `_t: T` field without needing an actual `T` value.
Construct with the empty struct literal `PhantomData::<T>{}`.

items.name: ParseIntError
items.doc: Represents a failed integer parse; returned by `i32::from_str_radix` / `from_ascii` / etc.

items.name: kind
items.doc: Classifies the failure.

items.name: TryFromIntError
items.doc:
Represents a failed `try_from` integer conversion: the value did not fit the target type.

Unlike Rust's opaque `core::num::TryFromIntError`, the `kind` is public.

items.name: kind
items.doc: Classifies the failure.

items.name: ParseFloatError
items.doc: Represents a failed float parse; returned by `f32::from_str` / `f64::from_str`.

items.name: kind
items.doc: Classifies the failure.

items.name: Wrapping
items.doc: Newtype wrapper whose `+` / `-` / `*` silently wrap on overflow (two's complement).

items.name: v
items.doc: The wrapped value.

items.name: Saturating
items.doc: Newtype wrapper whose `+` / `-` clamp to `T::MIN` / `T::MAX` instead of overflowing.

items.name: v
items.doc: The wrapped value.

items.name: NonZero
items.doc:
Wraps a value of type `T` with the invariant that it never equals zero (`T::default()`).
Unlike Rust, `Option<NonZero<T>>` gets no niche optimization yet — layout equals plain `T`.

items.name: val
items.doc: The wrapped value; non-zero whenever constructed via `new` / `new_unchecked`.

items.name: Pin
items.doc:
Wraps a pointer `P`, guaranteeing the pointee never moves again.
For `T: !Unpin`, safe code cannot obtain `&mut T` or a by-value `T` through the pin.

items.name: RangeOf
items.doc: Iterates the half-open range `[current, end)` for any `T: Step + Copy`.

items.name: current
items.doc: Next value to yield.

items.name: end
items.doc: Exclusive upper bound.

items.name: RangeOfIncl
items.doc: Iterates the inclusive range `[current, end]` for any `T: Step + Copy`.

items.name: current
items.doc: Next value to yield.

items.name: end
items.doc: Inclusive upper bound.

items.name: done
items.doc: Set once the final value has been yielded.

items.name: RangeOfFrom
items.doc: Iterates unboundedly from `current`; `next()` never returns `None`.

items.name: current
items.doc: Next value to yield.

items.name: RangeOfTo
items.doc:
Carries the exclusive upper bound of a start-less range `[.., end)`.
Slicing-index shape; not an iterator.

items.name: end
items.doc: Exclusive upper bound.

items.name: RangeOfToIncl
items.doc:
Carries the inclusive upper bound of a start-less range `[.., end]`.
Slicing-index shape; not an iterator.

items.name: end
items.doc: Inclusive upper bound.

items.name: RangeOfFull
items.doc: Marks the full unbounded range (`..`); stateless slicing-index shape.

items.name: RangeI32
items.doc: Iterates the half-open `i32` range `[current, end)`.

items.name: current
items.doc: Next value to yield.

items.name: end
items.doc: Exclusive upper bound.

items.name: RevRangeI32
items.doc: Iterates the elements of a `RangeI32` in reverse order.

items.name: current
items.doc: Exclusive upper cursor (decrement-then-yield).

items.name: start
items.doc: Inclusive lower bound (yielded last).

items.name: RangeI64
items.doc: Iterates the half-open `i64` range `[current, end)`.

items.name: current
items.doc: Next value to yield.

items.name: end
items.doc: Exclusive upper bound.

items.name: RevRangeI64
items.doc: Iterates the elements of a `RangeI64` in reverse order.

items.name: current
items.doc: Exclusive upper cursor (decrement-then-yield).

items.name: start
items.doc: Inclusive lower bound (yielded last).

items.name: RangeFromI32
items.doc: Iterates `i32` values unboundedly from `current`; `next()` never returns `None`.

items.name: RangeFromI64
items.doc: Iterates `i64` values unboundedly from `current`; `next()` never returns `None`.

items.name: RangeToI32
items.doc: Carries the exclusive `i32` upper bound of a start-less range `[.., end)`; not an iterator.

items.name: RangeToI64
items.doc: Carries the exclusive `i64` upper bound of a start-less range `[.., end)`; not an iterator.

items.name: RangeFull
items.doc: Marks the full unbounded range (`..`); bound-less, carries no state.

items.name: RangeInclI32
items.doc: Iterates the inclusive `i32` range `[current, end]`.

items.name: current
items.doc: Next value to yield.

items.name: end
items.doc: Inclusive upper bound.

items.name: done
items.doc: Set once `end` has been yielded (or when constructed empty).

items.name: RangeInclI64
items.doc: Iterates the inclusive `i64` range `[current, end]`.

items.name: current
items.doc: Next value to yield.

items.name: end
items.doc: Inclusive upper bound.

items.name: done
items.doc: Set once `end` has been yielded (or when constructed empty).

items.name: RcInner
items.doc:
Heap control block for `Rc`/`Weak`: counters followed by the value, in one allocation.
`val` is dropped when `strong` reaches 0; the block is freed when `weak` reaches 0.

items.name: strong
items.doc: Count of live `Rc` handles; `val` is dropped when it reaches 0.

items.name: weak
items.doc: Count of live `Weak` handles plus one implicit weak held collectively by strong handles.

items.name: val
items.doc: The stored value.

items.name: Rc
items.doc:
Single-threaded reference-counted pointer: shared ownership of a heap-allocated `T`.
Cloning bumps the strong count; the last strong drop runs `T`'s destructor. NOT `Send`.

items.name: Weak
items.doc:
Non-owning handle to an `Rc` allocation; does not keep `T` alive.
`upgrade()` returns `Some(Rc)` only while a strong reference still exists, else `None`.
Use to break `Rc` cycles (e.g. a child's back-pointer to its parent).

items.name: SplitPair
items.doc: Raw `(ptr, len)` halves of a slice split, as produced by `slice_split_at`.

items.name: left_ptr
items.doc: Pointer to the first element of the left half.

items.name: left_len
items.doc: Element count of the left half.

items.name: right_ptr
items.doc: Pointer to the first element of the right half.

items.name: right_len
items.doc: Element count of the right half.

items.name: Chunks
items.doc: Iterator over non-overlapping chunks of a slice, created by `slice_chunks`.

items.name: data
items.doc: Base pointer of the underlying slice.

items.name: len
items.doc: Total element count of the underlying slice.

items.name: pos
items.doc: Current element offset; advances by the yielded chunk's length.

items.name: step
items.doc: Chunk size in elements.

items.name: Windows
items.doc: Iterator over overlapping `size`-element windows of a slice, created by `slice_windows`.

items.name: data
items.doc: Base pointer of the underlying slice.

items.name: len
items.doc: Total element count of the underlying slice.

items.name: pos
items.doc: Current window start offset; advances by 1 per step.

items.name: size
items.doc: Window size in elements.

items.name: Vec
items.doc:
Growable heap-allocated array of `T`, contiguous in memory (Rust `Vec` analog).
On drop, each live element is dropped and the buffer is freed.

items.name: VecIter
items.doc:
Borrowing iterator over a `Vec<T>` (`Vec::iter()` / `for x in &v`); yields `&T`.
Owns nothing; `Drop` is a no-op. Mirrors Rust's `slice::Iter`.

items.name: VecIntoIter
items.doc:
Owning iterator over a `Vec<T>` (`into_iter` / `for x in v`); yields `T` by value.
On drop, un-yielded elements are dropped and the buffer is deallocated.

items.name: VecIterMut
items.doc: Mutable borrowing iterator over a `Vec<T>` (`Vec::iter_mut()`); yields `&mut T`.

items.name: WAnyMut
items.doc:
Mutable dual of `WAny`: a fat handle pairing an at-rest element slot with its Writ
zone, able to read a Pod, descend/grow a Ref, and replace the element in place.
Produced by container `get_mut`; the parent's zone cascades into the child.

items.name: slot
items.doc: At-rest element slot (never-move arena); `*slot` materialises, `*slot = v` lowers.

items.name: zone
items.doc: The Writ zone (allocator) the slot lives in, erased to `*mut u8` (cast at use).

items.name: DView
items.doc:
Non-owning, type-erased view: borrowed object bytes plus a `(space, code)` tag and metadata.
Does not extend lifetimes; to own a runtime-typed value use the Writ container (`WAny`).

items.name: DViewStore
items.doc:
Variable-width, aligned, owning at-rest store for `DView` elements (backs `Buffer<DView>`).
Homogeneous: the first stored element declares the container's `tag`/`meta`.
`storage_get` returns a view INTO the arena — valid only until the arena reallocates.

items.name: String
items.doc:
Owned, heap-allocated, growable byte string.
Invariant: `data[nbytes] == 0` (null terminator always present when `cap > 0`).

items.name: Span
items.doc: Source location of a token.

items.name: file_id
items.doc: Id of the source file; `-1` marks an unknown location.

items.name: line
items.doc: Line number within the file.

items.name: col
items.doc: Column number within the line.

items.name: Ident
items.doc: Identifier token text as a non-owning `(ptr, len)` byte view.

items.name: ptr
items.doc: Pointer to the first byte of the identifier text (UTF-8, not owned).

items.name: len
items.doc: Length of the identifier text in bytes.

items.name: TokenStream
items.doc: Flat sequence of `TokenTree`s; nesting lives inside `Group` trees.

items.name: trees
items.doc: Trees in source order.

items.name: span
items.doc: Outer span of the whole stream, set by the producer.

items.name: ArcInner
items.doc: Heap control block shared by `Arc<T>`/`Weak<T>`: atomic strong/weak counters, then the value.

items.name: Arc
items.doc: Atomically reference-counted shared-ownership pointer; `Send`/`Sync` when `T: Send + Sync`.

items.name: Weak
items.doc:
Non-owning, thread-safe handle to an `Arc` allocation; breaks `Arc` cycles.
`upgrade()` yields `Some(Arc)` only while a strong reference survives.

items.name: NodeBase
items.doc: Uniform per-block header shared by every concrete node type in a store.

items.name: id
items.doc: Persistent block id.

items.name: snapshot_id
items.doc: Creating snapshot id — the CoW skip key.

items.name: is_leaf
items.doc: Branch/leaf discriminator.

items.name: block_code
items.doc: Physical schema code of the concrete node type, stamped at alloc.

items.name: ShuttleResult
items.doc: Outcome of one branch-descent step: which child to enter and the adjusted target.

items.name: idx
items.doc: Index of the child to descend into.

items.name: remainder
items.doc: Adjusted target to carry into the recursive descent (e.g. `target - prefix`).

items.name: Segment
items.doc: A never-moved chunk: an inline header followed by `cap` bytes of data.

items.name: next
items.doc: Absolute pointer to the next segment, or null if this is the last one.

items.name: cap
items.doc: Data capacity in bytes for this segment, excluding the header.

items.name: used
items.doc: Bump cursor: bytes already handed out within the data region.

items.name: data
items.doc: Inline flexible-array tail holding the segment's raw data bytes.

items.name: Allocator
items.doc:
Owns a singly-linked list of never-moved segments.
Bump-allocates within `current`, appends a new segment when full, frees all on drop.

items.name: head
items.doc: First segment — the walk start for freeing all segments en masse.

items.name: current
items.doc: The segment currently being bump-allocated into.

items.name: seg_size
items.doc: Default data capacity in bytes for newly appended segments.

items.name: Writ
items.doc:
Owned, mutable root container for a never-move zoned document.

Owns the segment arena by value; placed objects never move, so pointers and
self-relative references into them stay valid for the container's lifetime.

items.name: allocator
items.doc: The never-move segment arena; interior-mutable so placement works through `&Writ`.

items.name: root
items.doc: The document root — the top-level `WAny` stored as its raw 8-byte word (0 = null/unset).

items.name: Slot
items.doc:
Opaque handle to a `T` placed in the container — a stable, never-moving location.

Not directly dereferenceable; read it via `Writ::get`/`get_mut`, which tie the
borrow to the container so views cannot outlive it.

items.name: Held
items.doc: An escaped reference paired with a residency holder that keeps its arena alive.

items.name: HeldAny
items.doc: An escaping `WAny` (stored by value) paired with a Writ holder that keeps the arena alive.

items.name: WString
items.doc:
UTF-8 string interned in the never-move arena as a self-describing custom DST.
Layout is exactly the wire shape `[vlen(payload_len)][UTF-8 payload]` — the length is
encoded in-band, no sized prefix. Borrow as `&WString`; read back with `as_str`.

items.name: bytes
items.doc: The whole tail: vlen length prefix followed by the UTF-8 payload.

items.name: WArray
items.doc:
Typed homogeneous packed array of a primitive `T: WArrTag`, in-arena and never moved.
Generic base of the pair; the `WArray<WAny>` specialisation is the heterogeneous array.

items.name: len
items.doc: Number of elements.

items.name: cap
items.doc: Allocated element capacity.

items.name: data
items.doc: Element buffer: packed plain `T`s behind a self-relative pointer.

items.name: WMapEntry
items.doc: Stores one open-addressing slot: two at-rest `WAny` words (key @0, val @8).

items.name: key
items.doc: At-rest Ref to the interned `WString` key; `0` marks an empty slot.

items.name: val
items.doc: The stored value as an at-rest `WAny` word.

items.name: WMap
items.doc:
Anchors the `WMap<K, V>` family as an empty base; never instantiated directly.
Per-key-shape partial specialisations attach layout and impls (mirrors legacy `Map<K,V>`).

items.name: Wu6
items.doc:
Labels the 6-bit key (keys `0..51`) at the type level.
The bitmap-indexed map is `WMap<Wu6, WAny>`, aliased `WTinyValMap`.

items.name: WSchemaH
items.doc:
Carries the runtime shape of a schema view: TOM pointer + arena allocator (ADR 0011).
The compiler's view type `S` has this exact layout; `make::<S>()` retypes `WSchemaH` to `S`.

items.name: m
items.doc: The underlying TOM (`WMap<Wu6, WAny>`) header.

items.name: z
items.doc: Arena allocator for boxing wide values on write.

items.name: Error
items.doc: Unit error returned by failed formatter operations; carries no payload.

items.name: Formatter
items.doc:
Formatting state threaded through `Display`/`Debug` impls; mirror of `core::fmt::Formatter`.
Bundles the output sink with the parsed spec of the current `{...}` placeholder.

items.name: w
items.doc: Output sink all writes go through.

items.name: fill
items.doc: Fill byte used for width padding (default `' '`).

items.name: align_code
items.doc: Alignment code.

items.name: sign_plus
items.doc: Whether `+` was given: force a sign on non-negative numbers.

items.name: alternate
items.doc: Whether `#` (alternate form) was given.

items.name: zero_pad
items.doc: Whether `0` (zero padding) was given.

items.name: width
items.doc: Minimum field width.

items.name: precision
items.doc: Maximum content length.

items.name: indent
items.doc: Pretty-print nesting depth; preserved across `reset_spec`.

items.name: Arguments
items.doc: Placeholder mirror of `core::fmt::Arguments`; currently just carries the rendered text.

items.name: rendered
items.doc: The already-formatted result string.

items.name: DebugStruct
items.doc: Builder for struct-shape `Debug` output (`Foo { x: 1 }`); from `Formatter::debug_struct`.

items.name: f
items.doc: Backing formatter.

items.name: result
items.doc: Running result returned by `finish`.

items.name: has_fields
items.doc: Whether any field has been written (drives comma/brace emission).

items.name: DebugTuple
items.doc: Builder for tuple-shape `Debug` output (`Foo(1, 2)`); from `Formatter::debug_tuple`.

items.name: f
items.doc: Backing formatter.

items.name: result
items.doc: Running result returned by `finish`.

items.name: fields
items.doc: Count of fields written so far.

items.name: DebugList
items.doc: Builder for list-shape `Debug` output (`[a, b]`); from `Formatter::debug_list`.

items.name: f
items.doc: Backing formatter.

items.name: result
items.doc: Running result returned by `finish`.

items.name: has_any
items.doc: Whether any entry has been written (drives comma emission).

items.name: DebugSet
items.doc: Builder for set-shape `Debug` output (`{a, b}`); from `Formatter::debug_set`.

items.name: f
items.doc: Backing formatter.

items.name: result
items.doc: Running result returned by `finish`.

items.name: has_any
items.doc: Whether any entry has been written (drives comma emission).

items.name: DebugMap
items.doc: Builder for map-shape `Debug` output (`{k: v}`); from `Formatter::debug_map`.

items.name: f
items.doc: Backing formatter.

items.name: result
items.doc: Running result returned by `finish`.

items.name: has_any
items.doc: Whether any entry has been written (drives comma emission).

items.name: MaybeUninit
items.doc:
Wraps a possibly-uninitialized `T`; mirrors `core::mem::MaybeUninit`.
Storage is an inner `value: T` field, not a union: `uninit()` zero-fills, so reads are
defined-but-meaningless rather than UB. Caller must still initialize before reading as `T`.
If `T: Drop`, going out of scope without `assume_init` runs drop on the zero-shaped value.

items.name: Item
items.doc: Handle to an untyped AST item node (struct/enum/fn/... declaration).

items.name: raw
items.doc: Value-form `WAny` Ref to the item's TinyObjectMap node.

items.name: Type
items.doc:
Type-level value handle produced by `type_of::<T>()`.

`kind` mirrors `LogosType::Kind` (Void=0, I32=1, ...); `name`/`size`/`align`/`uid`
are filled by the compiler when materialising a `Type` literal.

items.name: kind
items.doc: Numeric `LogosType::Kind` discriminant (Void=0, I32=1, ...).

items.name: name
items.doc: Type name bytes (rodata-backed; empty on values decoded by `type_from_anyval`).

items.name: size
items.doc: Size of the type in bytes.

items.name: align
items.doc: Alignment of the type in bytes.

items.name: uid
items.doc: Compiler-assigned unique type id.

items.name: Template
items.doc:
Declaration-as-data handle: a struct/enum/fn/trait/impl declaration carrying type params.

Inspection goes through `OView`.

items.name: raw
items.doc: Value-form `WAny` Ref to the declaration's AST node.

items.name: TypeList
items.doc: Zero-sized A-layer carrier for a variadic type pack.

items.name: Ident
items.doc: Borrowed identifier bytes; `ptr` borrows the source arena.

items.name: ptr
items.doc: Pointer to the identifier bytes (borrowed, not owned).

items.name: len
items.doc: Byte length.

items.name: QuoteItemBlob
items.doc:
Bundle returned by `quote_item! { ... }` and consumed by the compiler's item-splice path.

Carries the serialized item template plus packed ident/blob/cursor payloads.

items.name: template_ptr
items.doc: Pointer to the serialized item-template blob (rodata).

items.name: template_size
items.doc: Template blob size in bytes.

items.name: idents_blob
items.doc: Null or `[u64 N][N x {ptr,len}][bytes]` payload of `$ident` substitutions.

items.name: blobs_blob
items.doc: Null or `[u64 N][N x {offset,size}][bytes]` payload of spliced sub-blobs.

items.name: cursors_blob
items.doc: Null or packed repeat-cursor payload driving `$x...` repeat expansion.

items.name: ExprBlob
items.doc: Marker return type for quoted expressions (ABI = `WritStatic`; a single `*const u8`).

items.name: ptr
items.doc: `WritStatic`-shaped blob pointer.

items.name: IdentSpan
items.doc: Describes one substitution binding passed to the `quote_expr!` host shim.

items.name: ptr
items.doc:
Pointer to an `Ident` slot array (kind 0) or to `ExprBlob` data past its 8-byte
size prefix (kind 1).

items.name: count
items.doc: Slot count at `ptr` for kind 0 (1 = scalar, M = cursor); unused for kind 1.

items.name: kind
items.doc: Substitution mode: 0 = `Ident` (scalar or cursor), 1 = `ExprBlob` splice.

items.name: Emitter
items.doc:
Buffers one Logos compilation unit (a `package <name>; ...` chunk) for the host compiler.
Fill via `emit_*`/`push_*`/`tpl*` (or `buf` directly), then flush with `commit`; the host
parser wants exactly one `package` statement per committed chunk.

items.name: buf
items.doc: Buffered source text of the current chunk; cleared by `commit`.

items.name: ItemList
items.doc: Multi-item return value for a metacall that emits more than one item.

items.name: blobs
items.doc:
The emitted items, in emission order; each forwarded to
`logos_emit_item_blob_subst` by the metacall thunk.

items.name: SView
items.doc: Borrowed, non-owning `(ptr, len)` view of UTF-8 string bytes.

items.name: ptr
items.doc: Pointer to the first byte; null for an empty view.

items.name: len
items.doc: Length in bytes.

items.name: OView
items.doc:
Owning view over the trigger module's writ AST document.

Holds one retained ref on the host `MemHolder`, released on `Drop`.

items.name: holder
items.doc: Opaque retained host `MemHolder`; null when no document is loaded.

items.name: base
items.doc: Absolute base address of the document's single segment.

items.name: size
items.doc: Segment size in bytes.

items.name: LeafNodeForBranch
items.doc:
Seeds `#[derive_branch_node]`, which emits `BranchNode` from this struct's type params.
Not used as a node type itself; the emitted branch carries `keys`, `children`, and one
aggregate column per `BRANCH_OPS` op (`subtree_size: PrimVec<u64>`).

items.name: base
items.doc: Holds the common node header (`id`, `snapshot_id`, `is_leaf`, `block_code`).

items.name: keys
items.doc: Stores the sorted keys, parallel to `vals`.

items.name: vals
items.doc: Stores the values, parallel to `keys`.

items.name: LeafNode
items.doc: Data-leaf node: `keys` and `vals` are parallel `Buffer`s of container data, no `NodeARC` children.

items.name: base
items.doc: Holds the common node header (`id`, `snapshot_id`, `is_leaf`, `block_code`).

items.name: keys
items.doc: Stores the sorted keys, parallel to `vals`.

items.name: vals
items.doc: Stores the values, parallel to `keys`.

items.name: LeafDirNode
items.doc: Directory-leaf node: `vals` are `NodeARC` handles to child container roots, not raw data.

items.name: base
items.doc: Holds the common node header (`id`, `snapshot_id`, `is_leaf`, `block_code`).

items.name: keys
items.doc: Stores the sorted keys, parallel to `vals`.

items.name: vals
items.doc: Stores `NodeARC` handles to child container roots, parallel to `keys`.

items.name: SView2
items.doc: Borrowed UTF-8 bytes of a blob string.

items.name: ptr
items.doc: Pointer to the first byte of the UTF-8 data (borrowed from the blob).

items.name: len
items.doc: Length in bytes.

items.name: WView2
items.doc:
Read view over a writ rodata blob.
`base` is the first blob byte (the DocumentHeader, holding the root AnyVal at offset 0).
For a `WritStatic` the blob ptr already points past the 8-byte size prefix, so `base = hs.ptr`.

items.name: base
items.doc: First blob byte (the DocumentHeader).

items.name: IterFrame
items.doc:
Records one step of a `BtIter` descent: a pinned branch node plus the child index whose
subtree the iterator currently occupies.

items.name: branch_arc
items.doc: Owns an rc share of the branch block, pinning it for the frame's lifetime.

items.name: branch_p
items.doc: Typed-pointer view of the branch, derived from `branch_arc`'s data pointer.

items.name: idx
items.doc: Child index within the branch whose subtree the iterator currently occupies.

items.name: BtIter
items.doc:
Forward (or reverse-opened) snapshot iterator over a persistent BTree.

`leaf_arc` is null iff the iter is exhausted; `leaf_p` is the typed view derived from it.

items.name: path
items.doc: Branch frames from root to the current leaf's parent.

items.name: leaf_arc
items.doc: Owns an rc share of the current leaf block, pinning it for the iter's lifetime.

items.name: leaf_p
items.doc: Typed-pointer view of the current leaf; null iff the iter is exhausted/invalid.

items.name: cursor
items.doc: Index of the current entry within the leaf.

items.name: InsertOutcome
items.doc:
Outcome of a recursive insert: whether an existing key was replaced, and whether
the visited node split.

items.name: hit
items.doc: True if `key` already existed (value was replaced, not inserted).

items.name: split
items.doc: True if the node split during this insert; `right`/`right_max*` are then valid.

items.name: right
items.doc: The new right sibling produced by a split.

items.name: right_max_present
items.doc: True if `right_max` holds a valid value (K isn't `Default`-able in general).

items.name: right_max
items.doc: Max key of `right`'s subtree, valid only when `right_max_present`.

items.name: RemoveOutcome
items.doc:
Outcome of a recursive remove: whether the key was found, and whether the visited
node underflowed and needs the parent to rebalance.

items.name: hit
items.doc: True if the key was found and removed.

items.name: underflow
items.doc: True if the node is now below `min_keys` and the parent must rebalance it.

items.name: Mutex
items.doc: A mutual-exclusion lock protecting a `T`, backed by a heap-allocated `pthread_mutex_t`.

items.name: RwLock
items.doc: A reader-writer lock protecting a `T`, backed by a heap-allocated `pthread_rwlock_t`.

items.name: CommitEntry
items.doc: Pins one commit in the store DAG: a frozen directory-tree root plus its DAG edges.

items.name: root
items.doc: Holds the pinned directory-tree root NodeARC for this commit.

items.name: parents
items.doc: Lists the immediate ancestor commit ids; empty for a root/genesis commit.

items.name: children
items.doc: Lists the commit ids forked directly from this one.

items.name: released
items.doc: Marks the entry as tombstoned; when set, `root` is a null-handle NodeARC.

items.name: LockingStore
items.doc: Implements `IStore` v1: a `Vec<CommitEntry>` DAG guarded by a `Mutex`.

items.name: dag
items.doc: Holds every commit ever registered, indexed by `SnpId`.

items.name: lock
items.doc: Guards all DAG reads/writes and id allocation.

items.name: next_block_id
items.doc: Tracks the next block id to hand out.

items.name: next_snapshot_id
items.doc: Tracks the next snapshot id to hand out.

items.name: MutSnapshot
items.doc: Holds a single-thread mutable handle onto one forked-off commit, pre-`commit`.

items.name: root
items.doc: Owns one rc share of the directory-tree root being mutated.

items.name: parent_id
items.doc: Identifies the commit this snapshot forked from.

items.name: snapshot_id
items.doc: Identifies this snapshot for the CoW protocol; stamped onto mutated nodes.

items.name: id_ctr_p
items.doc: Points at the store's block-id counter, letting CoW allocate ids without a store round-trip.

items.name: DirInsertOutcome
items.doc: Reports the result of a recursive dir-tree insert; on split, carries the new right subtree.

items.name: split
items.doc: Indicates the node split; `right`/`right_max` are then populated.

items.name: right
items.doc: Holds the new right subtree; `Some` iff `split`.

items.name: right_max_present
items.doc: Mirrors `split`; set when `right_max` is meaningful.

items.name: right_max
items.doc: Carries the max ctr id in the new right subtree; valid only on split.

items.name: Store
items.doc: Wraps a shared `Arc<LockingStore>` — the user-facing handle to a persistent store.

items.name: arc
items.doc: Shares the backing `LockingStore`; cloned into every forked `Snap`.

items.name: Snap
items.doc:
Represents a live writable snapshot: the dir-tree root plus a shared ref to its store.
Dropping releases the whole dir chain; `commit` consumes the snap into the DAG instead.

items.name: dir
items.doc: Owns the snapshot's directory-tree root.

items.name: arc
items.doc: Shares the backing store.

items.name: snapshot_id
items.doc: Identifies this snapshot — the CoW key `make_unique` compares against.

items.name: parent_id
items.doc: Records the commit this snap forked from; becomes the new commit's parent.

items.name: open_containers
items.doc: Counts `ContainerRef`s open on this snap; `commit` panics while nonzero.

items.name: SnapCtx
items.doc: Carries the snap-side wiring a container handle embeds: dir slot, CoW key, ids, commit guard.

items.name: dir_root_p
items.doc: Points at `Snap.dir`; stable for the snap's life.

items.name: ctr_id
items.doc: Identifies this container within the dir.

items.name: snap_id
items.doc: Keys the CoW check in `make_unique`.

items.name: id_ctr_p
items.doc: Points at the store's `next_block_id`.

items.name: open_ctr_p
items.doc: Points at `Snap.open_containers` (the commit guard).

items.name: ContainerRef
items.doc:
Handles one map container in a snap: owns the root rc and embeds the snap-side `SnapCtx`.
Dropping releases the snap's open-container claim.

items.name: arc
items.doc: Owns an rc share of the container root.

items.name: ctx
items.doc: Embeds the shared snap-side wiring.

items.name: HashMap
items.doc:
Hash map with open addressing and linear probing; load factor 0.75, power-of-two capacity.
Mutating/lookup methods are `unsafe` and take raw `self` pointers; construct via
`hashmap_new` or `hashmap_with_capacity`.

items.name: HashMapIter
items.doc:
Iterator over `(&K, &V)` entries of a `HashMap`; created by `iter()`.
Holds raw pointers into the table — inserting during iteration may resize and dangle them.

items.name: HashMapKeys
items.doc: Iterator over `&K` keys of a `HashMap`; created by `keys()`.

items.name: HashMapValues
items.doc: Iterator over `&V` values of a `HashMap`; created by `values()`.

items.name: QError
items.doc:
Runtime error value returned by the fallible Deem APIs (`compile`/`render`/`run`).
Carries the offending name/operator inside the message text; no source offsets.

items.name: msg
items.doc: The owned error message text.

items.name: QEnv
items.doc:
Runtime binding environment for `Tpl::render` / `Query::run`: named scalar parameters,
data nodes/sources, and the UDF/UDA registry.
Holds at most 24 value/source bindings; binds past capacity are silently ignored.
Rebinding an existing name overwrites it (last write wins).

items.name: QRows
items.doc:
Materialized query result. Self-contained: string cells are interned into a `QRows`-owned
arena and outlive both the run and the `Query`; node cells are handles into the caller's
data doc, which must stay alive while they are read (the `bind_source` contract).

items.name: Query
items.doc:
Compiled relational query — compile once with `Query::compile`, run many with `run`.
Owns the lowered algebra, a catalog snapshot, and the compiled `rel` blocks.

items.name: IoSlice
items.doc: A borrowed `(pointer, length)` buffer slice for vectored I/O.

items.name: base
items.doc: Pointer to the first byte of the slice.

items.name: len
items.doc: Number of bytes in the slice.

items.name: FdReader
items.doc: Adapts a raw file descriptor as a `Read` source; does not own or close the fd.

items.name: FdWriter
items.doc: Adapts a raw file descriptor as a `Write` sink; does not own or close the fd.

items.name: BufWriter
items.doc:
Buffers writes to a wrapped `Write` sink; flushes when the buffer fills.
Call `close` to flush and free the buffer when done.

items.name: BufReader
items.doc:
Buffers reads from a wrapped `Read` source.
Call `close` to free the buffer when done.

items.name: Bytes
items.doc:
Refcounted immutable byte slice; cheap to clone (refcount bump) and to slice (zero-copy).
The buffer's refcount is atomic, so handles may cross fibers/threads.

items.name: BytesMut
items.doc:
Owned mutable byte buffer for building `Bytes`; grows on demand, `freeze` yields the
immutable view.

items.name: IoError
items.doc: Structured I/O error: an `ErrorKind` plus an optional OS `errno` and a static message.

items.name: kind
items.doc: Error category.

items.name: raw_os_error
items.doc: Raw OS `errno`; `-1` when the error is not OS-derived.

items.name: message
items.doc: Static human-readable message; `""` when absent.

items.name: BorrowedBuf
items.doc:
Writable byte-buffer cursor tracking how many bytes have been filled so far.
Simplified port of Rust's `io::BorrowedBuf`: memory is assumed initialised (no uninit tracking).

items.name: data
items.doc: Base pointer of the underlying writable buffer.

items.name: capacity
items.doc: Total buffer size in bytes.

items.name: filled
items.doc: Count of valid bytes written so far, from the start of `data`.

items.name: IoRing
items.doc:
Wraps liburing's `struct io_uring` in submit-and-wait mode: each op submits one SQE and
blocks until the kernel posts its CQE.
The ring bytes are heap-allocated and non-null once constructed; `Drop` releases them.

items.name: DirEntry
items.doc: One directory entry, as yielded by `read_dir` and `walk_dir`.

items.name: name
items.doc: Entry name: bare child name from `read_dir`, full path from `walk_dir`.

items.name: is_dir
items.doc:
True iff the entry is a directory (`read_dir`: `d_type`-based, symlink/unknown reads
as `false`; `walk_dir`: `stat`-based, follows symlinks).

items.name: ReadDir
items.doc:
Non-recursive directory iterator: yields the children of a path, skipping `"."` and `".."`.
Obtain via `read_dir`; the OS handle is closed on exhaustion or drop.

items.name: File
items.doc: Open file handle paired with its own `IoRing`; all I/O is issued through io_uring.

items.name: Url
items.doc:
Parsed absolute URL: `scheme "://" host [":" port] [path] ["?" query]`.
Components are stored raw (not percent-decoded); the fragment is not retained.

items.name: scheme
items.doc: Scheme, without the `://` separator (e.g. `http`).

items.name: host
items.doc: Host component, without the port.

items.name: port
items.doc: Port, or -1 when unspecified — callers pick the scheme default.

items.name: path
items.doc: Path; empty when absent, otherwise starts with `/`.

items.name: query
items.doc: Query string without the leading `?`; empty when absent.

items.name: FiberCtx
items.doc:
Holds the register state of a suspended fiber (callee-saved regs + `rsp`).
Maps 1:1 to the register save area in `fiber_ctx.S`; field order and sizes
MUST NOT change without updating the asm offsets.

items.name: Fiber
items.doc:
Represents one cooperative fiber: saved context, owned stack, FLS slots,
and intrusive ready-queue link.

items.name: Scheduler
items.doc:
Owns one thread's cooperative fiber state: intrusive ready FIFO, running
fiber, deferred-free slot, and optional retired-stack pool.

items.name: FutureSlot
items.doc: Heap-allocated one-shot slot shared between a producer and its consumer.

items.name: value_ptr
items.doc: Pointer to the delivered value; null until `future_complete` fills it in.

items.name: waiter
items.doc: `*mut Fiber` of the suspended consumer as `i64`, or 0 if none is waiting.

items.name: waiter_thr
items.doc: `*mut Thread` of the waiting consumer as `i64`; 0 means same-thread.

items.name: Reactor
items.doc: Per-thread io_uring reactor: one ring plus a count of fibers parked on in-flight I/O.

items.name: Latch
items.doc:
Count-down latch: a single waiter fiber suspends until the count reaches 0.

Single-thread only; supports at most one concurrent waiter.

items.name: Chan
items.doc:
Bounded FIFO channel between fibers on one thread, backed by a ring buffer.

At most one suspended sender AND one suspended receiver at a time; violating
this invariant is a caller error (see `chan_send` / `chan_recv`).

items.name: SpscRing
items.doc:
A bounded single-producer/single-consumer ring of `(fn_ptr, arg)` pairs.
One slot stays unused: a ring of capacity `cap` holds at most `cap - 1` entries.

items.name: Thread
items.doc: A worker thread: cooperative `Scheduler`, io_uring `Reactor`, SPSC inbox, wakeup eventfd.

items.name: ThreadPool
items.doc: A fixed set of worker `Thread`s, each running its own per-thread reactor loop.

items.name: SockAddr4
items.doc: IPv4 socket address matching `struct sockaddr_in` (16 bytes); port/addr in network byte order.

items.name: TcpListener
items.doc: A TCP socket listening for incoming IPv4 connections; check `is_valid` after construction.

items.name: TcpStream
items.doc: A connected TCP socket; its `Read`/`Write` impls use blocking `recv`/`send` syscalls.

items.name: UdpSocket
items.doc: An unconnected IPv4 UDP socket; check `is_valid` after construction.

items.name: AsyncTcpStream
items.doc:
Fiber-aware adapter around `TcpStream`. Its `Read`/`Write` impls route through
`async_recv`/`async_send` so calls suspend the current fiber instead of blocking
the whole thread. Use this variant when feeding a `TcpStream` into a generic
reader/writer (`BufReader<AsyncTcpStream>`, HTTP servers, ...) inside a reactor.

items.name: inner
items.doc: The wrapped stream; owns the socket fd.

items.name: ConnTask
items.doc:
Packs a connection fd + type-erased service pointer for handoff to a worker.
Heap-allocated by the acceptor, freed by the worker fiber after it is consumed.

items.name: fd
items.doc: Raw fd of the accepted connection.

items.name: pad
items.doc: Alignment padding; unused.

items.name: svc
items.doc: Type-erased `*mut FnService` (as `i64`) shared with the worker thread.

items.name: FnService
items.doc: `Service` adapter wrapping a plain `fn(&Request) -> Response` handler.

items.name: Method
items.doc: HTTP request method: a small integer `code` plus the literal token.

items.name: code
items.doc: Method code; known methods use 0–6, unknown/extension methods share 7.

items.name: name
items.doc: Method token verbatim (e.g. `"GET"`).

items.name: Header
items.doc: Offset/length quad locating one header's name and value inside `Headers::buf`.

items.name: name_off
items.doc: Byte offset of the name within `Headers::buf`.

items.name: name_len
items.doc: Byte length of the name.

items.name: value_off
items.doc: Byte offset of the value within `Headers::buf`.

items.name: value_len
items.doc: Byte length of the value.

items.name: Headers
items.doc:
Ordered list of HTTP header `(name, value)` pairs; duplicates allowed.
Name lookup is ASCII case-insensitive (RFC 7230 §3.2).

items.name: buf
items.doc: Backing storage: every name and value back-to-back, NUL-separated.

items.name: entries
items.doc: Per-header offset/length quads indexing into `buf`.

items.name: Request
items.doc: HTTP request: method, target URL, headers, and body.

items.name: method
items.doc: Request method.

items.name: url
items.doc: Target URL.

items.name: headers
items.doc: Request headers.

items.name: body
items.doc: Request body bytes.

items.name: version_minor
items.doc:
HTTP version minor digit from the request line: 0 for HTTP/1.0, 1 for HTTP/1.1.
Major is always 1 (higher majors are rejected by the parser).

items.name: Response
items.doc: HTTP response: status code, reason phrase, headers, and body.

items.name: status
items.doc: Status code (e.g. `200`).

items.name: reason
items.doc: Reason phrase for the status line.

items.name: headers
items.doc: Response headers.

items.name: body
items.doc: Response body bytes.

items.name: ChunkedReader
items.doc: Decodes a chunked-encoded `Read` source, exposing the body as a plain `Read`.

items.name: ContentLengthReader
items.doc:
Caps reads from a `Read` source at a fixed byte budget; frames a `Content-Length: N` body.
Returns 0 (EOF) once the budget is exhausted; never reads past it on the underlying reader.

items.name: ChunkedWriter
items.doc:
Frames each `write` call to the wrapped `Write` sink as one chunked-encoded record.
Call `finish` to emit the terminator; dropping without it leaves the stream malformed.
For chatty producers, wrap the sink in a `BufWriter` first to coalesce small chunks.

items.name: Pipe
items.doc:
Unix pipe: a connected read/write file-descriptor pair.
Descriptors are NOT closed on drop; call `close` (or `close_read`/`close_write`) explicitly.

items.name: read_fd
items.doc: Read end of the pipe; `-1` when closed or on creation failure.

items.name: write_fd
items.doc: Write end of the pipe; `-1` when closed or on creation failure.

items.name: Regex
items.doc:
Compiled regex pattern: a raw `(pointer, length)` view into the pattern bytes.
Does not own the bytes — the `str` passed to `compile` must outlive this `Regex`.

items.name: ManuallyDrop
items.doc:
Wraps a `T` and suppresses its destructor at scope-exit.
Destroy or recover the value explicitly via `manually_drop_drop` / `manually_drop_into_inner`.

items.name: value
items.doc: Wrapped value; no drop glue runs for it automatically.

items.name: MaybeDangling
items.doc:
Marks the inner `T` as exempt from dangling-reference checks.
Informational for now: borrowck does not yet honour the marker.

items.name: value
items.doc: Wrapped, possibly-dangling value.

items.name: Alignment
items.doc: Represents a power-of-2 alignment, in bytes.

items.name: bytes
items.doc: Alignment in bytes; a nonzero power of 2 when constructed via `alignment_new`.

items.name: DropGuard
items.doc: Runs the closure `f` at scope-exit (drop) unless defused via `disarm`.

items.name: f
items.doc: Cleanup closure; called exactly once at drop while `armed`.

items.name: armed
items.doc: Whether the closure fires on drop; cleared by `disarm`.

items.name: Box
items.doc:
Owns a heap-allocated `T` exclusively; move-only, drops the value and frees the block on
scope exit (no manual free).

items.name: ExitStatus
items.doc: Exit status of a terminated child process, wrapping the raw `waitpid` status word.

items.name: Child
items.doc: Handle to a spawned child process; owns the parent-side pipe fds.

items.name: LineReader
items.doc:
Buffered line iterator over a pipe fd, yielding lines with the `\n` terminator stripped.
A trailing partial line at EOF is also yielded. Reads block (no reactor integration yet).

items.name: Rng
items.doc:
Fast seedable pseudo-random number generator based on splitmix64.
Deterministic for a given seed; not cryptographically secure — use `os_random` for that.

items.name: TestCtx
items.doc: Accumulates test pass/fail counts; each failing assertion prints a diagnostic to stderr.

items.name: JoinHandle
items.doc: Owned handle to a spawned OS thread; used to `join` or `detach` it exactly once.

items.name: CivilTime
items.doc: Broken-down UTC civil time in the proleptic Gregorian calendar.

items.name: year
items.doc: Proleptic-Gregorian year; may be negative.

items.name: month
items.doc: Month of year, `1..=12`.

items.name: day
items.doc: Day of month, `1..=31`.

items.name: hour
items.doc: Hour of day, `0..=23`.

items.name: minute
items.doc: Minute of hour, `0..=59`.

items.name: second
items.doc: Second of minute, `0..=59`; leap seconds are not represented.

items.name: wday
items.doc: Day of week, `0` = Sunday through `6` = Saturday.

items.name: Duration
items.doc:
A span of time, stored as a signed nanosecond count (range ~±292 years).
Negative values are valid (represent time going backwards).

items.name: Instant
items.doc:
A measurement from a monotonic clock.
The absolute value has no meaning — only differences between `Instant`s.

items.name: SystemTime
items.doc:
Wall-clock time (Unix epoch = 1970-01-01 00:00:00 UTC).
May go backwards on NTP adjustments.

items.name: HashSet
items.doc: Unordered set of unique keys, backed by `HashMap<K, u8>`.

items.name: HashSetIter
items.doc: Iterator over a `HashSet`'s elements, yielding `&K`.

items.name: BTreeMap
items.doc:
Ordered map backed by a sorted parallel-array (not a real B-tree).

`get`/`contains_key` are `O(log n)` binary search; `insert`/`remove` are `O(n)`
due to element shifting.

items.name: BTreeMapIter
items.doc: Iterator over a `BTreeMap`'s entries in ascending key order, yielding `(&K, &V)`.

items.name: Result
items.doc:
Represents either success (`Ok(T)`) or failure (`Err(E)`).

The `?` operator propagates `Err` early from functions returning `Result<_, E>`.

items.name: Ordering
items.doc: The result of a total-order comparison between two values.

items.name: ControlFlow
items.doc: Directs short-circuiting operations: keep going with a `C`, or halt early with a `B`.

items.name: Option
items.doc: Represents an optional value: either `Some(T)` or `None`.

items.name: Ordering
items.doc:
Specifies memory ordering for atomic operations; mirrors `core::sync::atomic::Ordering`.

On the x86-64 backend every variant currently lowers to the same seq-cst
primitive (see module header); the distinction matters on weaker targets.

items.name: ControlFlow
items.doc: Two-state control-flow signal used to short-circuit iteration or `?`-desugaring.

items.name: Infallible
items.doc:
An empty (uninhabited) enum: the error type of a `Result` that can never be `Err`.
Not the never type — Logos has a real `!` (type of a divergent `fn … -> !`); use `!`
for divergence and `Infallible` only as a "this variant is impossible" stand-in.

items.name: IntErrorKind
items.doc:
Classifies why integer parsing or a narrowing integer conversion failed.

Same variants as Rust's `core::num::IntErrorKind`.

items.name: FloatErrorKind
items.doc:
Classifies why float parsing failed.

Rust's equivalent enum is private; exposed here so callers can branch on it.

items.name: FpCategory
items.doc:
Classification of an IEEE 754 floating-point number; mirrors `core::num::FpCategory`.
Variants in IEEE-754-classification order: Nan / Infinite / Zero / Subnormal / Normal.

items.name: GeneralCategory
items.doc:
Subset of Unicode general-category codes.
Variants mirror UCD GC abbreviations (as in Rust's `core::unicode::category::Category`);
only the categories our scaffolded tables populate are exposed — the rest map to `Other`
(Cn = unassigned).

items.name: WAny
items.doc:
Heterogeneous Writ value slot: one 8-byte word holding either an inline tagged
primitive (`Pod`) or a type-erased pointer to a tagged zone object (`Ref`).
`null` = `Ref(0)`. Non-owning (`Copy`) — the Writ arena owns Ref pointees.

items.name: LitKind
items.doc: Lexical category of a `TokenTree::Literal` token.

items.name: Spacing
items.doc: Whether a `Punct` is glued to the following punct as part of a multi-char operator.

items.name: Delim
items.doc: Delimiter kind enclosing a `TokenTree::Group`.

items.name: TokenTree
items.doc: One node of a token stream: a leaf token or a balanced delimited subgroup.

items.name: InsertError
items.doc: Describes why a checked insert (`try_insert`) rejected a key or value.

items.name: ErrorKind
items.doc: Category of an I/O error, mirroring Rust's `std::io::ErrorKind`.

items.name: IoUringError
items.doc: Represents an `IoRing` failure; one variant per logical failure class.

items.name: HttpError
items.doc: Errors produced by HTTP client and server operations.

items.name: Default
items.doc: Types with a canonical zero/false/empty value.

items.name: Eq
items.doc:
Defines total equality via `eq`, with `ne` derived by default.
Not implemented for floats — NaN != NaN breaks totality; see `PartialEq` instead.

items.name: PartialEq
items.doc:
Defines partial equality via `eq`, with `ne` derived by default.
Unlike `Eq`, does not require totality — implemented for floats where NaN != NaN.

items.name: PartialOrd
items.doc: Marker trait for partial ordering — currently empty pending a `partial_cmp` method shape.

items.name: Ord
items.doc:
Trait for types that form a total order.
Float types are intentionally excluded: `NaN` breaks totality.

items.name: Add
items.doc: Overloads the binary `+` operator.

items.name: Sub
items.doc: Overloads the binary `-` operator.

items.name: Mul
items.doc: Overloads the binary `*` operator.

items.name: Div
items.doc: Overloads the binary `/` operator.

items.name: Rem
items.doc: Overloads the binary `%` (remainder) operator.

items.name: Neg
items.doc: Overloads the unary `-` (negation) operator.

items.name: Shl
items.doc: Overloads the binary `<<` (left-shift) operator.

items.name: Shr
items.doc: Overloads the binary `>>` (right-shift) operator.

items.name: BitAnd
items.doc: Overloads the binary `&` (bitwise AND) operator.

items.name: BitOr
items.doc: Overloads the binary `|` (bitwise OR) operator.

items.name: BitXor
items.doc: Overloads the binary `^` (bitwise XOR) operator.

items.name: Not
items.doc: Overloads the unary `!` (logical/bitwise NOT) operator.

items.name: AddAssign
items.doc: Overloads the `+=` operator, adding `rhs` in place.

items.name: SubAssign
items.doc: Overloads the `-=` operator, subtracting `rhs` in place.

items.name: MulAssign
items.doc: Overloads the `*=` operator, multiplying by `rhs` in place.

items.name: DivAssign
items.doc: Overloads the `/=` operator, dividing by `rhs` in place.

items.name: RemAssign
items.doc: Overloads the `%=` operator, taking the remainder by `rhs` in place.

items.name: BitAndAssign
items.doc: Overloads the `&=` operator, bitwise-ANDing with `rhs` in place.

items.name: BitOrAssign
items.doc: Overloads the `|=` operator, bitwise-ORing with `rhs` in place.

items.name: BitXorAssign
items.doc: Overloads the `^=` operator, bitwise-XORing with `rhs` in place.

items.name: ShlAssign
items.doc: Overloads the `<<=` operator, left-shifting by `rhs` in place.

items.name: ShrAssign
items.doc: Overloads the `>>=` operator, right-shifting by `rhs` in place.

items.name: FnOnce
items.doc: Overloads call syntax for callables consumed by the call (by-value receiver).

items.name: FnMut
items.doc: Overloads call syntax for callables that may mutate captured state.

items.name: Fn
items.doc: Overloads call syntax for callables that only read captured state.

items.name: Deref
items.doc: Overloads immutable dereference: `*x` on a `Deref` type yields the pointee.

items.name: DerefMut
items.doc: Overloads mutable dereference; `*x = v` is not yet auto-dispatched (call `deref_mut`).

items.name: Index
items.doc: Overloads immutable indexing: `a[i]` on user types (e.g. maps).

items.name: IndexMut
items.doc: Overloads mutable indexing; `a[i] = v` is not yet auto-dispatched (call `index_mut`).

items.name: FromIterator
items.doc: Builds `Self` from an iterator of `A` values; the dispatch target of `Iterator::collect`.

items.name: Sum
items.doc: Folds an iterator of `A` into `Self` by addition; the dispatch target of `Iterator::sum`.

items.name: Product
items.doc: Folds an iterator of `A` into `Self` by multiplication; dispatch target of `Iterator::product`.

items.name: Iterator
items.doc: Yields a sequence of `Item` values one at a time; the core protocol of `std.iter`.

items.name: IntoIterator
items.doc: Converts a value into an iterator over its items; `for x in expr` desugars through this.

items.name: DoubleEndedIterator
items.doc: Extends `Iterator` with consumption from the back; the two ends never yield the same item.

items.name: ExactSizeIterator
items.doc: Extends `Iterator` with an exact remaining-item count.

items.name: FusedIterator
items.doc: Marks an iterator that keeps returning `None` forever after its first `None`.

items.name: Pattern
items.doc: A searchable pattern over `str` haystacks; implemented for `str` (substring) and `u8` (byte).

items.name: UnwindSafe
items.doc:
Marks a type as safe to observe after an unwind (`catch_unwind` bound).
Not auto-derived today — opt in with an explicit impl or `AssertUnwindSafe`.

items.name: RefUnwindSafe
items.doc:
Marks `&T` as unwind-safe — the shared-reference analogue of `UnwindSafe`.
Not auto-derived today.

items.name: Any
items.doc: Trait for runtime type identification; a blanket impl makes every type `Any`.

items.name: Clone
items.doc: Types whose values can be explicitly duplicated.

items.name: Copy
items.doc: Marker trait for types that can be duplicated by a plain bitwise copy.

items.name: Drop
items.doc: Types with custom cleanup logic run automatically when a value goes out of scope.

items.name: From
items.doc:
Converts a value of type `T` into `Self`; infallible and value-consuming.

Implementing `From<T> for U` also enables `t.into()` via the blanket `Into` impl,
so implement `From` rather than `Into`.

items.name: Into
items.doc:
Converts `self` into a value of type `T`; infallible and value-consuming.

Do not implement directly: blanket-derived from every `From<S> for T` impl.

items.name: TryFrom
items.doc: Converts a value of type `T` into `Self`, failing with `ConvertError` when out of range.

items.name: TryInto
items.doc:
Converts `self` into a value of type `T`, failing with `ConvertError` when out of range.

Do not implement directly: blanket-derived from every `TryFrom<S> for T` impl.

items.name: Borrow
items.doc:
Borrows `self` as a `&Borrowed` view.

Contract (as in Rust): `Eq`/`Ord`/`Hash` of the borrowed view must agree with
the owned value — keyed collection lookups (`HashMap::get`) rely on it.

items.name: BorrowMut
items.doc: Borrows `self` as a `&mut Borrowed` view; mutable counterpart of `Borrow`.

items.name: Error
items.doc: Root error trait with chained-cause (`source()`) introspection.

items.name: Storage
items.doc: Base storage capability: element type, length, and indexed read.

items.name: OwningStorage
items.doc: `Storage` that owns its backing memory and must be explicitly dropped.

items.name: DynamicStorage
items.doc: `OwningStorage` that can grow and mutate in place.

items.name: BorrowedStorage
items.doc: Marker for read-only, non-owning storage views; no drop, no grow, safe to discard.

items.name: Datatype
items.doc: Describes how to interpret bytes held by a `Storage`: `View<S>` is the processing form.

items.name: Container
items.doc: A `Datatype` paired with its canonical `DynamicStorage`, as used by `Buffer<DT>`.

items.name: Primitive
items.doc: Marks ≤16-byte POD datatypes whose processing view is the `Prim` value type, copied in/out.

items.name: PodRef
items.doc: Marks >16-byte POD datatypes viewed by reference: `*const DT` reads, `*mut DT` writes.

items.name: ContainerOrd
items.doc: `Container` with 3-way comparison of stored views, for ordered collections (Map, Set).

items.name: UnsizedPayload
items.doc:
Marks "fixed `Meta` + variable `Atom` tail" datatypes (string bytes, bigint limbs, ...).
Class-marker only for now: the `Container`/SoA blanket over it is not yet landed.

items.name: Hasher
items.doc:
Accumulates a hash over a stream of integer writes; `finish` yields the result.

No byte-stream writer yet — values are fed as whole integers (see `Hash for str`
for the byte-slice workaround).

items.name: Hash
items.doc: Types hashable through any `Hasher`; mirrors `core::hash::Hash`.

items.name: BuildHasher
items.doc:
Factory producing fresh `H` hashers; lets containers keep one seed across many hashes.

Parameterised over `H: Hasher` in lieu of an associated type (Logos has none yet).

items.name: Send
Sync
items.doc:
Marks a type safe to share by reference across threads: `&T` is `Send` iff `T: Sync`.
Auto trait: synthesized from field types; raw pointers are `!Sync`.

items.name: Sized
items.doc:
Marks a type whose size is known at compile time.
Implicit bound on every generic type parameter unless opted out with `T: ?Sized`.
Unsized types (`str`, `[T]`, `dyn Trait`) appear only behind (fat) references/pointers.

items.name: Unpin
items.doc:
Marks a type that can be safely moved even after being pinned. Auto trait: derived structurally — a type is `Unpin` unless it stores a `PhantomPinned` field or is marked `#[pinned]`; refs and raw pointers are always `Unpin`. `Pin

` ergonomics ship in `logos.lang.pin`.

items.name: SelfDescribing
items.doc:
Implemented by custom-DSTs (`#[self_describing]` structs) to recover their tail length.
All reference forms (`*Foo`, `&Foo`, `&mut Foo`) become thin pointers; the compiler
calls `dst_len` whenever it needs the tail length (materializing a fat `&Foo`,
building a `foo.tail` view, bound-checking `foo.tail[i]`).

items.name: FromStr
items.doc:
Parses a value of `Self` from a `str`; Rust's `core::str::FromStr` with the
error type as trait type-param `E` (Logos traits carry no associated types
outside the Fn-family). Backs the `s.parse::<T>()` idiom.

items.name: Step
items.doc: Provides the primitive stepping operations that range iterators require.

items.name: Try
items.doc:
Types that can be short-circuited by the `?` operator.
`Continue`/`Residual` are Rust's `type Continue`/`type Residual` associated types,
lifted to free generic params (slice-1 surface; no associated types yet).

items.name: FromResidual
items.doc: Types a `?`-propagated residual can be converted into at the early-return site.

items.name: Datum
items.doc: Presents a type as a `DView` — the capture seam from a concrete value to its storage form.

items.name: ToString
items.doc:
Converts a value to an owned `String` (Rust-style `.to_string()`).
Takes `self` by value; the blanket `impl<T: Display> ToString for T` lives in `mem.fmt`.

items.name: BTreeNode
items.doc: Type-erased operations every concrete B-tree node (branch/leaf) implements.

items.name: Resident
items.doc:
A residency root (a segment set), type-erased so a holder is opaque.

Dropping the last `Rc<dyn Resident>` runs the concrete container's `Drop`,
freeing its segments.

items.name: WArrTag
items.doc:
Marks a primitive as a typed-array element; `arr_code()` returns its Writ wire tag.
Implemented for u8..u64, i8..i64, f32, f64 (codes 2101–2110); `WAny` does not satisfy it,
which keeps the typed and heterogeneous method sets from colliding.

items.name: WritField
items.doc:
Converts schema field values between `WAny` and `Self` (the seam behind schema field sugar).
Implementing it makes a type schema-storable; generic schemas resolve it at monomorphization.

items.name: WIntKeyTag
items.doc: Supplies the per-key Writ wire tag (MapI32AnyVal=3101 .. MapU64AnyVal=3104).

items.name: FmtWrite
items.doc: Byte/string sink that formatters write into; mirror of `core::fmt::Write`.

items.name: Display
items.doc: User-facing formatting (`{}` placeholders); mirror of `core::fmt::Display`.

items.name: Debug
items.doc: Developer-facing formatting (`{:?}` placeholders); mirror of `core::fmt::Debug`.

items.name: LowerHex
items.doc: Lowercase hexadecimal formatting (`{:x}`); mirror of `core::fmt::LowerHex`.

items.name: UpperHex
items.doc: Uppercase hexadecimal formatting (`{:X}`); mirror of `core::fmt::UpperHex`.

items.name: Octal
items.doc: Octal formatting (`{:o}`); mirror of `core::fmt::Octal`.

items.name: Binary
items.doc: Binary formatting (`{:b}`); mirror of `core::fmt::Binary`.

items.name: LowerExp
items.doc: Lowercase scientific-notation formatting (`{:e}`); mirror of `core::fmt::LowerExp`.

items.name: UpperExp
items.doc: Uppercase scientific-notation formatting (`{:E}`); mirror of `core::fmt::UpperExp`.

items.name: IStore
items.doc: Narrows the cross-backend store ABI: snapshot lifecycle, DAG navigation, id allocation.

items.name: Read
items.doc: Abstraction for byte-oriented input sources.

items.name: Write
items.doc:
A byte-oriented output sink.

`write`/`flush` follow the shared Read/Write convention: positive = byte
count, zero = sink refused further bytes (treat as error), negative = `-errno`.

items.name: Service
items.doc: Turns a `Request` into a `Response`; implement to plug a handler into the conn loop.

undocumented: logos.lang.hash.(A, B, C, D, E, F)::hash
logos.lang.hash.(A, B, C, D, E, F, G)::hash
logos.lang.hash.(A, B, C, D, E, F, G, I)::hash
